name: Marisol Vega
review: Roatan ended up being the highlight for me. I booked a small group tour that included a wildlife sanctuary and a short beach stop. The sloth interaction felt a little touristy but still memorable, and the guides were genuinely funny and engaging. The beach portion was short, maybe too short, but the water was calm and clear. In Costa Maya, we tried to explore on foot but quickly realized everything near the port is built for cruise passengers. We ended up taking a taxi to a quieter beach, which was better but felt rushed because of limited port time. Cozumel was the easiest to navigate—we just walked around town, grabbed lunch, and did some light shopping. Overall, I liked the variety of stops but wished Costa Maya had either more time or better access to less commercial areas.

name: Derrick Johnson
review: I’ve cruised a few times and Roatan felt the most authentic of the three stops. We hired a driver for the day and visited local neighborhoods before heading to a snorkeling spot. The reef was impressive, probably the best water visibility of the trip. Costa Maya was my least favorite. The port area is basically a shopping complex, and the beach clubs all felt similar. We picked one randomly and it was fine but nothing special. Cozumel had more flexibility—we rented a scooter and drove along the coast, stopping wherever looked interesting. That freedom made a big difference. If I did this itinerary again, I’d plan something more structured for Costa Maya and keep Roatan and Cozumel more open-ended.

name: Linh Tran
review: First cruise for me, and I didn’t realize how much planning the ports would need. In Roatan, we booked a zipline and beach combo excursion. The zipline part was fun but very quick, and most of the day ended up being transportation. Costa Maya was a long day because we chose the ruins excursion. The ruins themselves were fascinating, but the bus ride was close to an hour each way, which made the day feel exhausting. Cozumel was the only port where we just wandered without a plan. We found a small café and walked along the waterfront, which ended up being my favorite experience because it felt relaxed. If I could redo it, I’d skip the long excursions and stay closer to the port areas.

name: Carlos Mendez
review: Roatan surprised me in a good way. We did a snorkeling trip right off a small boat, and the coral and fish were incredible. It didn’t feel overcrowded like I expected. Costa Maya, on the other hand, felt very manufactured. We stayed in the port area and honestly just used the pool and had a few drinks there. Didn’t feel worth getting off for long. Cozumel was somewhere in between. We took a taxi to a beach club and spent a few hours swimming and relaxing. That was nice, but it got crowded by midday. Timing really matters at these ports.

name: Aisha Rahman
review: Traveling with my sister, we were most excited about Costa Maya because of the Mayan ruins. The site itself was beautiful and surrounded by jungle, but the schedule was very tight. We barely had time to take photos before being called back to the bus. In Roatan, we did a cultural tour that included local food tasting and a brief island history overview, which I appreciated more than I expected. It felt less rushed. Cozumel was more relaxed—we booked a beach club with limited capacity, which made it feel less chaotic than the main areas. Overall, the itinerary is good but you really feel the time pressure at Costa Maya.

name: Trevor Collins
review: We skipped Costa Maya completely and stayed on the ship. From what I could see at the port, it looked very crowded and commercial. Roatan was worth getting off for—we did an ATV tour through some rural areas and it felt more adventurous than typical excursions. Got pretty muddy but that was part of the fun. Cozumel was more laid back. We just took a taxi downtown and walked around, stopping for food and drinks. Nothing fancy but a good way to spend the day without being on a schedule.

name: Natalie Brooks
review: Roatan felt a bit disorganized when we first got off the ship. Lots of tour operators trying to get your attention. Once we got past that, our snorkeling excursion was great and well run. Costa Maya was honestly disappointing. The port area is nice visually, but it didn’t feel like we were seeing anything real. We tried to venture out but ended up back at the port because of time constraints. Cozumel redeemed the trip—we did a catamaran snorkel and beach stop, and that was easily the most enjoyable day.

name: Ethan Park
review: Costa Maya cooking class was a standout. Small group, very interactive, and the staff kept it fun. It wasn’t a long excursion, which I actually liked because we still had time to explore a bit after. Roatan was more nature-focused—we visited a botanical area and learned about local plants, which was interesting but buggy. Cozumel was our least planned day and also the easiest. We just found a place to swim and relax.

name: Sofia Dimitriou
review: In Roatan we did a beach break that included lunch. The beach itself was beautiful, but it got crowded quickly as more groups arrived. Costa Maya was better than I expected—we took a taxi to a quieter stretch of beach and had a much calmer experience. Cozumel felt the most developed. Lots of shops and restaurants, but also very busy. I preferred the quieter moments in the other ports.

name: Jamal Peterson
review: Roatan had the best excursion of the trip for us. We combined snorkeling with a short island tour, and both parts were well organized. Costa Maya felt like a missed opportunity. We didn’t book anything ahead and the options at the port didn’t look appealing, so we ended up just walking around. Cozumel was fun but very tourist-heavy. We did a tequila tasting and it was entertaining, but definitely designed for cruise crowds.

name: Hannah Weiss
review: We chose a long excursion in Costa Maya to see the lagoon area, and while it was beautiful, the travel time took up most of the day. I didn’t realize how far things are from the port. Roatan was much easier—we did everything within a short drive, including a beach and some local sightseeing. Cozumel was flexible and easy to explore without a plan. That balance made the itinerary enjoyable overall.

name: Omar Haddad
review: Roatan was my favorite stop. We did a private driver tour and saw parts of the island you wouldn’t get with a large group. Costa Maya was my least favorite because everything felt built around the cruise schedule. Cozumel had better independent options, especially if you’re willing to take a taxi away from the port.

name: Grace Kim
review: We spent the day at a marine park in Cozumel and it was worth it for the snorkeling alone. Clear water and plenty of fish. Roatan was also great for water activities, though our excursion felt a bit rushed. Costa Maya was more about cultural sights, but the timing made it hard to fully enjoy.

name: Luis Fernandez
review: Costa Maya ruins tour was impressive but very structured. You move quickly and don’t have much freedom to explore on your own. Roatan felt more relaxed—we had time at a beach and a small local restaurant. Cozumel was easygoing and probably the best for just wandering without a plan.

name: Priya Nair
review: I appreciated the variety of ports but found the scheduling a bit tight. In Roatan, our excursion included a wildlife stop and beach time, but both felt shortened. Costa Maya required the most planning because everything is far from the port. We chose a beach club and enjoyed it, but transportation took time. Cozumel was the easiest to manage independently, which made it less stressful.

name: Ben Carter
review: We rented a golf cart in Costa Maya and drove along the coast, stopping at different beach spots. That ended up being more fun than a structured excursion. Roatan was more about organized tours for us, which worked fine. Cozumel had a mix of both options, which I liked.

name: Elena Petrova
review: Roatan’s scenery stood out the most to me, especially along the coast. Costa Maya felt more artificial near the port, though the farther areas were nicer. Cozumel was lively but crowded. Each port had something different, but I preferred the quieter experiences.

name: Marcus Lee
review: Snorkeling in Roatan was easily the best activity of the cruise. Costa Maya didn’t impress me much—we stayed near the port and it felt like a shopping stop more than a destination. Cozumel had better balance with things to do both near and away from the port.

name: Isabella Rossi
review: We focused on beaches at every stop. Roatan had the clearest water, Costa Maya was quieter once you left the port, and Cozumel had the most amenities. Each had pros and cons depending on what you want.

name: David Nguyen
review: This itinerary gave a good mix, but it really depends on how you plan each port. Roatan worked best with a guided excursion, Costa Maya required leaving the port to enjoy it, and Cozumel was the easiest to explore casually. I’d do it again but plan Costa Maya differently next time.

name: Marissa Delgado
review: Roatan was our favorite stop on this itinerary. We booked a guided island tour that included a visit to a sloth and monkey sanctuary, a short scenic drive over the hills, and a beach break at the end. The animal encounter was the highlight because the guides explained a lot about conservation and how tourism supports local work. After that we went to a small local beach where the water was calm and clear, and we had about an hour to swim before heading back. Costa Maya felt very different. The port area was built up and easy to navigate, but we chose to leave the immediate shopping zone and take a taxi to a quieter beach club. It was less crowded and we appreciated having space to relax instead of being surrounded by cruise crowds. The ride there was short, maybe fifteen minutes, and the driver pointed out small villages along the way. Cozumel was our most active day. We rented a private driver and did a mix of snorkeling and a quick stop near a beach club area. The snorkeling spot had decent visibility but currents picked up later in the morning so we did not stay long. Overall, I liked how each port offered a different pace: Roatan for nature and animals, Costa Maya for a relaxed beach day, and Cozumel for activity and exploring.

name: Jamal Whitaker
review: Roatan was a pleasant surprise for us. We joined a small group excursion that combined a visit to a wildlife sanctuary with a short island drive. The highlight was holding a sloth and seeing how the staff cared for rescued animals. It felt more educational than I expected, and not overly commercialized. After that we had a short stop at a beach where we could swim, though we did not have much time there. Costa Maya was more about convenience. The port area was organized but we wanted something quieter, so we took a taxi to a small beach club outside the main tourist zone. It was calm and easygoing, and we spent most of our time just sitting by the water. In Cozumel, we tried snorkeling near a reef site booked through a local operator. The water clarity was good but there were quite a few boats around. Still, we saw plenty of fish and enjoyed the experience, even if it felt a bit rushed compared to Roatan.

name: Helen M.
review: Roatan was our favorite stop. We did a quick sloth sanctuary visit and a short beach break afterward. The animals were the highlight, especially getting close to the sloths. The beach time felt too short but the water was warm and clear. Costa Maya we mostly stayed near the port and walked around the shops, nothing too exciting. In Cozumel we just took a taxi to a small beach area and relaxed for a couple of hours.

name: Rafael Ortega
review: Roatan we booked a half-day island tour that included a monkey and sloth encounter and a stop at a hillside viewpoint. The guide shared a lot about local life which made the drive interesting. We did not feel rushed and had enough time for photos. Costa Maya was very relaxed for us. We took a taxi to a quieter beach club instead of staying near the port. The beach was calm, with shallow water and light crowds, which was exactly what we wanted after a busy travel day. Cozumel we chose a snorkeling excursion near a reef. The visibility was decent but there were many other groups in the water, so it felt a bit crowded. Still, we saw colorful fish and enjoyed the day overall.

name: Nina Caldwell
review: In Roatan we booked a zipline and animal sanctuary combo which ended up being the most memorable part of the trip. The zipline went over thick jungle and then we stopped to see sloths and monkeys up close. The guides were friendly and kept things moving without feeling rushed. Costa Maya was more laid back. We grabbed a taxi to a beach club just outside the main port area and spent the afternoon in the water and under shade. It was not fancy but it was peaceful and easy to enjoy. In Cozumel we walked around the port area first, then took a short ride to a snorkeling spot. The water was clear but currents made it tricky for beginners in our group. I would say Roatan had the best balance of activity and nature, while Costa Maya was best for pure relaxation and Cozumel was somewhere in between.

name: Derrick Lawson
review: Roatan was mostly about the sloth sanctuary for us. We did a short guided visit and it was interesting to hear how the animals are rescued and cared for. The rest of the island tour was fine but not as memorable. Costa Maya we stayed close to the port and just explored the shops and small pools in the area. It was convenient but felt very touristy so we did not stay long. In Cozumel we took a taxi to a local beach instead of an organized excursion. It was a simple day with swimming and sitting under umbrellas. Nothing overly structured, which was actually nice after several busy stops.

name: Sofia Ramirez
review: Roatan was the highlight of our cruise. We did a guided wildlife and beach excursion and the sloth encounter was honestly the best part. The animals are clearly well cared for and the staff took time to explain everything. After that we had a short beach stop that felt very relaxed and not crowded. Costa Maya was more of a walking day for us. We stayed in the port area, browsed shops, and sat by the small pool. It was fine but not something I would book again as an excursion day. Cozumel was better. We joined a snorkeling trip and saw a lot of fish, though the water was a bit choppy. The guide was helpful and kept the group together well. Overall Roatan stood out the most for nature and interaction, while Cozumel was good for activity and Costa Maya was the weakest for us personally.

name: Marcus Bennett
review: In Roatan we went on a small group island tour that included a sloth and monkey stop plus a scenic drive through the hills. The views were beautiful and the guide talked a lot about local communities which made it feel more personal. Costa Maya we decided to skip a formal excursion and instead went to a nearby beach club. It was quiet and we could actually relax without crowds. Cozumel we tried snorkeling near a reef and although there were many boats around, the water clarity was good enough to enjoy spotting fish and coral. Each port had a different vibe and Roatan felt the most authentic overall.

name: Tanya Brooks
review: Roatan we did a quick animal sanctuary visit and it was the best part of the stop. Getting close to sloths and hearing about their rescue was really memorable. Costa Maya we mostly stayed near the port shops and it felt very commercial so we did not venture far. Cozumel we took a taxi to a small beach area and just relaxed in the shade for a while. It was a simple but nice day overall.

name: Hector Morales
review: Roatan we booked a private island tour that included a sloth encounter and a stop at a local beach. The drive through the island gave us a good sense of everyday life there, not just tourist spots. Costa Maya we chose a beach club outside the port and spent most of the day swimming and eating simple local food. It was less hectic than staying near the cruise center. In Cozumel we did a snorkeling trip that felt a bit crowded but still enjoyable because the water was so clear. We saw coral and small fish pretty easily even without diving far.

name: Linda Park
review: Roatan was very nature focused for us. We did a combined sloth sanctuary and beach excursion and it felt well paced. The animals were definitely the highlight. Costa Maya we stayed in the port area and walked around for a bit but it was very tourist heavy so we returned early to relax. Cozumel we joined a reef snorkeling tour and while it was busy with other groups, the underwater visibility made it worth it. Each stop had something different to offer but Roatan stood out most.

name: Chris Donovan
review: Roatan we went to a wildlife park and it was surprisingly meaningful. Seeing sloths up close and learning about conservation efforts made the stop feel worthwhile. Costa Maya we skipped excursions and just stayed near the port pools and shops. It was okay for a short walk but not much more. Cozumel we took a taxi to a beach spot and spent a couple of hours swimming. It was a simple day but enjoyable because the water was warm and calm.

name: Alicia Nguyen
review: Roatan we did a guided tour that included a sloth and monkey sanctuary and a short beach visit. The animals were the main reason we chose it and it did not disappoint. Costa Maya we went to a beach club about fifteen minutes away from the port and it was much quieter than expected with plenty of space to relax. Cozumel we booked a snorkeling excursion that had good visibility but felt slightly rushed due to multiple stops and other boats around. Still, we saw a lot of marine life and enjoyed the day overall.

name: Brian Sato
review: Roatan we joined a small group tour focused on wildlife and scenery. The sloth encounter was definitely the highlight and the guides were informative without being overwhelming. Costa Maya we took a taxi to a beach club and just spent the afternoon in the water. It was peaceful and not too crowded which we appreciated. In Cozumel we tried a reef snorkeling trip and although there were a lot of people, the water clarity made it worth going. Each port felt different in pace and energy.

name: Meghan Foster
review: Roatan was mostly about the animal sanctuary for us and it ended up being a really good decision. The sloths were adorable and the guides were knowledgeable. Costa Maya we stayed near the port and walked around but it felt very commercial so we did not stay long. Cozumel we took a short taxi ride to a beach area and relaxed under umbrellas for a few hours. It was a calm and easy day overall.

name: Diego Alvarez
review: Roatan we booked a tour that mixed sightseeing with a sloth sanctuary visit. It was a good introduction to the island and not too rushed. Costa Maya we opted for a beach club away from the port and it was much quieter than expected with clear water and relaxed atmosphere. Cozumel we did snorkeling near a reef and while there were several groups, the marine life made it worth the trip. Overall Roatan felt the most unique compared to the other ports.

name: Harper Collins
review: Roatan was a great nature stop. We did a sloth and monkey sanctuary visit and it felt well organized and not overly touristy. Costa Maya we mostly stayed in the port area and it was fine for a short walk but we did not find much to do. Cozumel we took a taxi to a beach club and spent the afternoon swimming and resting. It was the most relaxing of the three ports for us.

name: Samuel Kim
review: Roatan we joined a guided wildlife excursion that included sloths and a short scenic drive. The experience felt personal and not rushed. Costa Maya we went to a quiet beach club outside the port and enjoyed a very relaxed afternoon by the water. Cozumel we did snorkeling near a reef and although it was busy, the water clarity was excellent and we saw plenty of fish. Each port had its own appeal depending on what you are looking for.

name: Veronica James
review: Roatan we did a sloth sanctuary tour and it was easily the highlight of the cruise itinerary. The animals were the main attraction and the staff were great. Costa Maya we stayed close to the port and it felt too commercial so we left early. Cozumel we took a short ride to a beach area and relaxed for a couple of hours. It was a simple but enjoyable stop.

name: Owen Mitchell
review: Roatan we booked a wildlife and beach tour and the sloth encounter was the best part of the entire day. Costa Maya we went to a beach club outside the port and it was calm, not crowded, and easy to enjoy. In Cozumel we tried snorkeling but the currents were stronger than expected so we did not stay in the water long. Still, the visibility was good and we saw enough marine life to make it worthwhile.

name: Jordan Peterson
review: Roatan was the highlight for us. We did the monkey and sloth hangout which was cute, though it felt a bit like a production line for tourists. The drive through the island was eye-opening; it's much more rugged than I expected. In Costa Maya, we didn't bother with an excursion and just walked around the port area. Big mistake. It's basically a giant outdoor mall with overpriced drinks. We tried to find a way out to the local town but the taxi situation was chaotic and we just gave up and went back to the ship early. Cozumel was much better for independent exploring. We grabbed a taxi to a local spot called Money Bar. The snorkeling right off the shore was surprisingly decent for being so close to the pier. I wish we had more than six hours in Cozumel though, as we felt rushed to get back before the gangway closed. Overall, a decent itinerary but stay away from the port 'villages' if you want anything authentic.

name: Sarah_Travels88
review: Cozumel was incredible! We did the Amazing Cozumel Race which is like a scavenger hunt around the downtown area. It was a great way to see the local plazas and backstreets rather than just the tourist shops by the pier. We ended at a local restaurant for chips and salsa. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown. We took the bus to the Chacchoben ruins. The ruins themselves are stunning and our guide was very knowledgeable about the Mayan history, but the 1-hour bus ride each way in a cramped van was exhausting. Roatan was beautiful but the traffic is insane. It took us forever to get back to the ship from West Bay beach. If you go to Roatan, make sure you leave plenty of buffer time because those narrow roads get backed up fast when multiple ships are in port.

name: Miguel Rodriguez
review: I've been to Cozumel three times now, so this time we skipped the beach and did a cooking class. We learned to make authentic mole and it was the best meal of the whole trip. Very hands-on and the chef was hilarious. Roatan was also great for diving. I went with a local shop instead of the ship excursion and saved about forty bucks. The reef is in much better shape than I saw in Mexico. My only complaint was Costa Maya. The port is so artificial. We tried to walk to Mahahual, but the sun was brutal and there isn't much shade. If I did it again, I'd just book a beach club and stay put.

name: Grandma_Jan
review: We had a lovely time in Roatan. We took a private taxi tour which was much easier on my husband's knees than the big tour buses. The driver took us to a high point for photos and then to a nice chocolate factory. Costa Maya was a bit too much walking for us, so we stayed on the ship and enjoyed the quiet. In Cozumel, we did the glass bottom boat. It was nice to see the fish without getting wet, but the boat was a bit crowded and hard to see out of at times.

name: D_Chen_NY
review: Costa Maya was the surprise winner for me. We booked a trip to the Bacalar Lagoon. It is a long drive, almost 90 minutes, but the water is crystal clear and has seven different shades of blue. It felt way more exclusive than the crowded beaches in Cozumel. Speaking of Cozumel, it was just okay. We did a catamaran snorkel trip but there were at least five other boats at the same reef. It felt like snorkeling in a soup of people. In Roatan, we just did the beach at the port (Mahogany Bay). It’s convenient since you can walk there, but the sand is a bit coarse and it’s very commercialized. Good for a lazy day, bad for an adventure.

name: Tyler West
review: Roatan snorkeling is legit. Went to West End and the coral was vibrant. Cozumel was a tourist trap, honestly. Too many people pushing pharmacy meds and fake silver. Costa Maya was better if you get away from the pier.

name: Yelena Vasiliev
review: Our day in Costa Maya was quite frustrating. We booked a golf cart rental in advance, but when we arrived, they told us they were overbooked and we had to wait an hour. Once we finally got moving, the town of Mahahual was charming, but the initial stress ruined the vibe. Cozumel was much smoother. We spent the day at Mr. Sancho’s. It’s definitely a party atmosphere, which was fun for our group, but maybe not for families. The food was actually decent for an all-inclusive. Roatan was the most scenic port by far. We did a canopy zipline tour through the jungle. The views of the ocean while flying through the trees were breathtaking. Just be prepared for the humidity; it’s no joke in the Honduran jungle. I wish the ship stayed in Roatan until sunset because the departure was a bit early.

name: Marcus & Jen
review: We loved the history in Costa Maya! The Mayan ruins at Chacchoben are a must-see if you haven't been. Our guide, Ivan, was so passionate and pointed out spider monkeys in the trees above the temples. It felt very Indiana Jones. Cozumel was a bit of a contrast. We did the Jeep tour around the island. The east side of the island is much more rugged and beautiful than the side where the ships dock. No electricity over there, just wild waves and small beach bars. Roatan was our relaxation day. We went to Little French Key. It's a bit pricey to get out there, but the water is calm and the lunch was fantastic—fresh lobster and coconut rice. One thing to note: the pier in Roatan is very long, so be prepared for a walk if you don't use the 'magic chair' lift.

name: Fatima Al-Sayed
review: The ports were a mixed bag. Cozumel felt very safe and easy to navigate. We walked to the local museum and had coffee in the square. It was peaceful. Costa Maya felt like a giant souvenir shop. Every time we turned around, someone was trying to sell us a massage or a tequila tasting. It was very high-pressure. In Roatan, we had a bit of a scare when our tour van got a flat tire on the way back from the iguana farm. The driver handled it well and got a replacement vehicle quickly, but we were worried about missing the ship. I'd recommend only using reputable tour companies there because the infrastructure isn't great. The iguana farm was interesting though, hundreds of them just roaming around.

name: BeachBum_92
review: Cozumel: Palancar Beach is the way to go if you want quiet. No loud music, just waves. Costa Maya: rented a bike and rode along the malecon. Best $15 I spent. Roatan: skip the port beach and head to West Bay. Best sand in the Caribbean.

name: Kevin Shields
review: I was disappointed with the time allocation in Cozumel. We arrived at 10 AM and had to be back by 4 PM. By the time you get through the crowds at the pier and find a taxi, half the day is gone. We tried to go to the San Gervasio ruins, but our driver told us we wouldn't have enough time to really see them and get back safely. We ended up just getting lunch downtown. Costa Maya was better for timing, as we had a full 8 hours. We spent it at a beach club called Maya Chan. The service was impeccable and they limit the number of guests so it never feels crowded. Roatan was beautiful but we didn't do much. Just walked through the local shops near the port. The prices in Roatan seemed a bit higher than in Mexico for similar souvenirs.

name: Linda G.
review: We did the dolphin swim in Cozumel. It was my daughter's dream, so it was worth it for her, but it felt very 'factory-like' to me. You're in and out in 30 minutes. Costa Maya ruins were much more my speed. The jungle setting is very peaceful. In Roatan, we just took a taxi to the West End village and walked around. It has a very laid-back, diver vibe. Lots of cute little cafes and docks. It felt much more authentic than the other two ports. I would have liked to stay there for dinner, but the ship left too early. All three ports were very easy to navigate in English, which made things stress-free.

name: Raj_Patel
review: Roatan is stunning. We did the 'Best of Roatan' tour. The views from the hills are amazing. We saw the sloths which were the highlight for the kids. Costa Maya was okay, we did the beach break. Water was a bit choppy that day. Cozumel was great for shopping, got some nice vanilla and hot sauce.

name: CruisingCouple01
review: In Costa Maya, we did the 'Lost Mayan Kingdom' water park. It was actually a lot of fun, especially the 'zip-coaster' thing. It's right next to the port so no long bus rides. Cozumel was our snorkeling day. We went to Chankanaab. The park is nice and clean, but the snorkeling area was very crowded. You had to be careful not to kick someone! Roatan was a bit of a letdown because it rained most of the day. We tried to go to the beach but ended up just sitting under a tiki hut waiting for the downpour to stop. The roads in Roatan turn into rivers when it rains, so the drive back to the ship was a bit nerve-wracking. Still, the people were very friendly everywhere we went.

name: Samantha Reed
review: Cozumel is the gold standard for port organization. Everything flows so well. We did a chocolate and wine tasting which was very sophisticated and tasty. Costa Maya is the opposite. It feels like you're being funneled through a maze of shops just to leave the port area. It's annoying. We eventually made it out to a small restaurant in Mahahual called Nohoch Kay for amazing fish tacos. Roatan's natural beauty is the best of the three, but the infrastructure is lacking. We went to the botanical gardens and it was lovely, but the path was very muddy and slippery. Definitely wear good shoes if you go off the beaten path in Roatan. The itinerary overall was great for seeing different sides of the Western Caribbean.

name: Tom_H_66
review: For our day in Cozumel, we booked a private catamaran. Best decision ever. We got away from the cruise crowds and had a private reef to ourselves. The crew caught fresh conch and made ceviche right on the boat. It was the highlight of the trip. In Costa Maya, we did the tequila seminar. It was educational, though they really push you to buy the expensive bottles at the end. Roatan was our least favorite. We tried to do a walking tour but it was so hot and the sidewalks were uneven. We ended up just heading back to the ship for a nap. I think next time I'd book a water-based excursion for Roatan since being on land was just too humid for us.

name: V_Gomez
review: The ports were nice but very busy. Roatan had three ships in, and it felt like the island was at capacity. We did the sloth sanctuary, which was fun but very quick. Costa Maya was better for lounging. We just stayed at the port pool. It's free and actually quite large, though the drinks are pricey. Cozumel was our best day. We rented a car and drove around the whole island. The 'wild' side of the island is spectacular—no buildings, just nature and some small bars. It was nice to escape the cruise crowds for a few hours. Just make sure you fill up the gas tank before returning it, as the stations near the pier are a ripoff.

name: Clara Bow
review: I loved Cozumel! We went to the Pearl Farm. It's a bit of a boat ride to get there, but it's so unique. You get to snorkel over the pearl beds and see how they grow them. Lunch was served on a deserted beach. It felt like a true escape. Costa Maya was a bit disappointing in comparison. It felt very manufactured. We did the shore excursion to the lagoon, but it was so crowded we couldn't find a lounge chair. Roatan was better; we went to a small resort called Turquoise Bay. It was quiet and the water was like glass. The only issue was the long drive across the island, which took about 45 minutes each way.

name: Gary_The_Traveler
review: Costa Maya has some great ruins if you don't mind the bus ride. Chacchoben is impressive. Roatan's West End is great for a stroll and a local beer. Cozumel is just too crowded for me these days. Too many ships.

name: Aisling O'Connor
review: We had a mixed experience with the port excursions. In Roatan, we booked a 'semi-sub' tour to see the reef. It was great for my elderly mother who can't snorkel. We saw turtles and rays through the windows. However, the tour started 30 minutes late and we were worried about our afternoon plans. In Costa Maya, we went to the beach at Mahahual. The seaweed was quite bad that day, which isn't anyone's fault, but it made swimming difficult. The local vendors are also very persistent, which got tiring after an hour. Cozumel was our favorite stop. We went to the Mayan Cacao Company. We learned how to make traditional chocolate and the samples were delicious. The facility was clean and the staff were very professional. I think the time spent in Cozumel was just right, but I would have traded some Costa Maya time for more time in Roatan.

name: Marcus Thompson
review: Roatan was the absolute highlight for us. We booked a private driver named Victor who took us away from the main tourist hubs and up into the hills for some incredible photos of the reef. We spent the afternoon at West Bay Beach. The water was crystal clear, though the vendors selling massages and jewelry were a bit relentless if you sat near the water's edge. In Cozumel, we did the bar hop bus to the 'wild side' of the island. It was amazing to see the rugged coastline with no electricity or big resorts, just crashing waves and fresh ceviche. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown in comparison. The port area felt very artificial, like a giant outdoor mall. We tried to get a taxi to Mahahual, but the line was so long we just gave up and went back to the ship for a nap. If I did this itinerary again, I'd definitely plan a real excursion for Costa Maya instead of winging it.

name: Sarah_CruiseLover82
review: Cozumel is always a favorite for our family. We headed straight to Paradise Beach. It’s $68 for the all-inclusive pass now, which felt a bit steep, but the service was top-notch and the kids loved the inflatable water park. The taxi ride was easy and only cost about $16 for the four of us. Roatan was beautiful but the traffic is getting out of hand. It took us nearly 45 minutes to get back from the monkey sanctuary, and we were starting to get nervous about the all-aboard time. Costa Maya was okay, but the walk from the ship to the actual port exit is incredibly long, especially in that humidity. We mostly just did some light shopping for silver and vanilla before heading back. Not a bad trip, but I wish we had more time in Roatan to actually see the island instead of just sitting in a van.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: We did the Chacchoben ruins in Costa Maya and it was a spiritual experience. Our guide was actually Mayan and shared so much about the calendar and the limestone structures. The bus ride is about an hour each way, but it was worth it to see the spider monkeys in the trees. Cozumel was just a shopping day for us, nothing too exciting. Roatan snorkeling was incredible at West End!

name: David Stein
review: First time on this route. Roatan's coastline is stunning but the port area at Mahogany Bay feels like a trap. You have to pay for a chair lift just to get to a crowded beach? No thanks. We walked the nature trail instead which was nice but buggy. Cozumel was better; we rented a Jeep and drove the perimeter road. Highly recommend stopping at the small beach shacks on the east side for a beer. Costa Maya was the only disappointment. We didn't book anything and found the 'port village' to be overpriced and loud. It's basically a maze designed to keep you from finding the local town. If you don't have a plan for Costa Maya, stay on the boat.

name: pete_travels_99
review: Cozumel was great for diving. Visibility was at least 80 feet at Palancar Reef. Saw a nurse shark and a few turtles. Roatan was okay, but we had a delay getting off the ship which cut into our beach time. Costa Maya was just a tourist trap.

name: Jasmine Kareem
review: Roatan was a dream! We spent the day at Little French Key. It’s a bit of a trek to get there—bus then a small boat—but once you arrive, it’s paradise. No vendors, just clear water and good food. Costa Maya was much more stressful. The taxi drivers were very aggressive at the pier, shouting prices. We eventually got to a beach club in Mahahual, but the seaweed was everywhere. Cozumel was a solid day. We did the chocolate workshop and it was very hands-on. We even got to grind the cocoa beans ourselves. The port time in Cozumel felt perfect, but I felt rushed in Roatan. I could have stayed at the key for another three hours easily. Overall a great mix of culture and relaxation.

name: Robert L. Miller
review: I'm a bit of a history buff, so I was looking forward to the ruins. Chacchoben in Costa Maya didn't disappoint. The climbable sections are limited now to preserve them, but the scale is still impressive. Cozumel felt too commercialized for me. It’s basically one giant pharmacy and jewelry store after another. We took a ferry to Playa del Carmen but the crossing was very rough—lots of seasick passengers. Roatan was the most authentic-feeling stop. We visited a local school as part of a 'giving back' excursion. It was humbling and much better than sitting on a beach. The logistics in Roatan were a bit messy with the bus dispatch, but the experience itself was the highlight of my cruise. Wish we had stayed later in Mexico to see the sunset.

name: Linda_and_Jim
review: We stayed on the ship in Costa Maya because we’ve been there so many times and the port is just too crowded when there are three ships in. In Cozumel, we did the submarine excursion. It was pricey but seeing the wall of the reef from 100 feet down was spectacular. My husband doesn't swim so it was the only way for him to see the coral. Roatan was very lush. We did a botanical garden tour. Bring bug spray! I got eaten alive despite wearing long sleeves. The island is beautiful but the humidity is on another level compared to the Mexican ports. We enjoyed the local cashew wine we bought near the pier.

name: Xiao Wei Chen
review: Costa Maya snorkeling was disappointing. Not much to see and the water was murky. Cozumel was much better. We went to Skyreef and the fish were everywhere right off the shore. Roatan was the best for scenery but the port pier is a very long walk.

name: Brenda Pitts
review: Roatan we did the Daniel Johnson Monkey and Sloth hangout. Best day ever! Holding a sloth was on my bucket list. The monkeys are mischievous though, one tried to grab my sunglasses! Our driver then took us to a local spot for lunch where we had the best fried fish of the trip. In Costa Maya, we just took the tram to the end of the pier and sat by the pool. It was fine but very loud with the DJ. Cozumel was our last stop and we were a bit tired, so we just walked around the Plaza del Sol. It’s a nice area but be prepared to be hassled by every shop owner you pass. The timing for all the ports was decent, though an extra hour in Cozumel would have been nice for a sit-down dinner.

name: GregH76
review: The ports were okay. Roatan is beautiful but the roads are terrible. We spent too much time in traffic. Costa Maya is a manufactured 'village' that I could do without. Cozumel is the only place with real infrastructure. We had a great lunch at Pancho’s Backyard.

name: Siddharth Mehta
review: The itinerary is solid for beach lovers. In Cozumel, we booked a private catamaran. Snorkeling at El Cielo was the best experience of my life. The water is shallow and filled with starfish—it looks fake! Costa Maya was a bit more 'rustic.' We rented golf carts and drove down to the lighthouse. It was fun but the carts are in rough shape. Roatan was also great. We did the zipline and it was quite a rush. The safety standards seemed okay, though the equipment looked a bit weathered. My only real complaint is the shore excursion prices through the ship. I'd recommend booking third-party in Cozumel and Roatan to save money. Costa Maya is small enough that you can just wing it if you're brave.

name: Kathy_G_61
review: I struggled with the walking in Costa Maya. The pier is huge and the shuttle was full. Once in the port, it's all cobblestones which was hard for my walker. Cozumel was much more accessible. We took a van to the dolphin encounter and it was very well managed for people with mobility issues. Roatan was a bit of a challenge too, very hilly. We stayed in the port area and looked at the shops. The crafts in Roatan were much more unique than the stuff in Mexico—lots of beautiful wood carvings. I wish the cruise line provided more information about the walking distances at each pier before we got off.

name: Jordan P.
review: Cozumel was 10/10. Went to Nachi Cocom. It’s capped at 130 people so it was very peaceful. Open bar and a 4-course lunch. In Roatan, we did a kayak tour through the mangroves. It was very hot but seeing the ecosystem up close was cool. Costa Maya was just 'meh' for me. Too many people trying to sell me stuff I didn't want.

name: Amina J.
review: I was really disappointed with the time we had in Roatan. We didn't dock until 10:00 AM and had to be back by 5:00 PM. By the time you get through the crowds, you only have about 5 hours. We went to Tabyana Beach and it was gorgeous, but we only got to stay for about three hours before the bus driver started rounding everyone up. Costa Maya felt like a long day in a shopping mall. The 'Maya' part of Costa Maya is mostly just branding. Cozumel was the highlight for me because we did a private taco tour downtown. We went to four different local spots and the food was incredible. It felt like we actually saw the real Mexico for a few hours. I’d recommend skipping the ship excursions in Cozumel and hiring a local guide.

name: Bill_N_SanDiego
review: If you are going to Costa Maya, go to Bacalar. It’s a long drive, about 90 minutes, but the "Lake of Seven Colors" is breathtaking. It's fresh water so no salt or sand to deal with. Cozumel was just a zoo. Five ships in port meant thousands of people everywhere. We tried to go to San Gervasio ruins but the traffic was backed up all the way to the pier. We ended up just grabbing a beer at a local bar and headed back. Roatan was much better. We did a glass bottom boat tour. The reef is in much better shape than the ones near Cozumel. The port staff in Roatan were also much friendlier and less pushy than the ones in Mexico.

name: Chloe Summers
review: Roatan ziplining was scary but fun! The guides were hilarious. Costa Maya we did a beach club called Blue Kay. It was cheap but the service was slow. Cozumel was just for shopping. Found some great vanilla but you have to haggle hard or they'll overcharge you.

name: Frankie DeLuca
review: I'm not much of a beach person so I was looking for something else. In Cozumel, I did the 'Amazing Cozumel Race' which was like a scavenger hunt. It was a blast and I got to see parts of the town I never would have found otherwise. Costa Maya was a bit of a waste of time for me. I walked around the port, saw the flamingos (which looked a bit sad), and went back to the ship. Roatan was better. I took a local taxi to the West End and just sat at a cafe and watched the divers. It’s a very laid-back vibe there. I think the itinerary is good, but you really need to research your options if you aren't into just sitting on a beach chair all day.

name: Sam_Travels_World
review: Cozumel: Don't miss the ferry to the mainland if you want real adventure, but watch the time! Costa Maya: Maya Chan is the best beach club ever. They treat you like family. Roatan: The sloths are cute but the island is very poor outside the gates. Be prepared for that contrast.

name: Mariah J.
review: This was my third time on this itinerary. Roatan continues to be my favorite. The lush green mountains meeting the blue water is just unbeatable. We did a fishing charter this time and caught some snapper that a local place cooked up for us. Costa Maya hasn't changed much—still a tourist trap at the pier, but Mahahual is slowly getting more developed. The seaweed problem in Costa Maya seems to be getting worse every year though. Cozumel is always reliable. We did the salsa and salsa tour (making salsa and dancing salsa). It’s cheesy but a lot of fun with a group of friends. The port logistics were smooth at all three stops, though the security line to get back on the ship in Cozumel was nearly 30 minutes long in the direct sun. Overall, a great trip for anyone who loves the sun, but I'd suggest more time in Roatan and less in Costa Maya if they ever change the schedule.

name: Daniel R.
review: Roatan ended up being the highlight for us. We booked a small-group snorkeling trip and the water clarity was unreal compared to what we’ve seen elsewhere. The boat ride was short and smooth, and we had plenty of time in the water without feeling rushed. Costa Maya felt very manufactured in comparison—we walked through the port area and didn’t feel compelled to go further, so we headed back early. Cozumel was somewhere in between; we grabbed a taxi downtown and wandered around, which was enjoyable but crowded. Overall, the itinerary felt a bit uneven, but Roatan alone made it worth it.

name: Aisha Khan
review: We skipped Roatan entirely because we weren’t interested in the excursion options and honestly just needed a slow morning. In Costa Maya, we tried one of the beach clubs about 20 minutes away. It was relaxing but the taxi situation was a little chaotic getting back. Cozumel was more our pace—we walked around town, found a quiet café, and just people-watched for a few hours. Not every stop needs to be packed with activities, and this itinerary let us pick and choose.

name: Miguel Torres
review: In Costa Maya we did the ruins tour, which involved about an hour bus ride each way. The ruins themselves were impressive, especially being surrounded by jungle, but the timing felt tight once we got back. Roatan was more relaxed—we did a beach break and some light snorkeling. Cozumel felt the most tourist-heavy, with lots of shops and crowds right off the ship. If I did it again, I’d prioritize more independent exploring instead of structured excursions.

name: Emily J
review: Roatan was my favorite day by far. We visited a wildlife park where we held a sloth and saw monkeys, which felt like a once-in-a-lifetime experience. The rest of the day was a mix of driving around the island and stopping at viewpoints. Costa Maya didn’t impress me much—we stayed near the port and it felt like a big shopping complex. In Cozumel, we booked a snorkeling excursion that was fun but pretty crowded. Still, I appreciated the variety across the three stops.

name: TravelDad84
review: Traveling with two teenagers, Cozumel worked best for us. We did a snorkeling and beach combo excursion that kept them busy the whole time. Roatan was enjoyable but the drive between stops took longer than expected, which cut into our time. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown—we walked outside the port area and didn’t find much that appealed to us. I’d recommend planning something specific there rather than winging it.

name: Sofia Martinez
review: Costa Maya surprised me in a good way. We did a small cooking class excursion where we made tortillas and salsa, and it felt much more personal than typical cruise activities. Roatan was nice but a little disorganized getting off the ship and meeting our guide. Cozumel was easy to navigate, though very busy. If you like quieter, more intimate experiences, Costa Maya might actually be the best stop depending on what you book.

name: James Whitaker
review: Roatan had the best water we saw all trip. We went diving and visibility was excellent, easily the highlight of the cruise. Costa Maya felt like it required more effort to enjoy—we didn’t book anything and ended up back on the ship early. Cozumel was decent for walking around and grabbing food, but it didn’t stand out. The itinerary is solid if you plan ahead, otherwise some ports can feel underwhelming.

name: Linh Nguyen
review: We focused on relaxing rather than packing in excursions. In Roatan, we found a quiet beach spot through a local driver and just stayed there most of the day. Costa Maya felt more like a shopping stop, so we didn’t venture far. Cozumel was crowded but had a lot more to see within walking distance. It’s an easy itinerary if you don’t want to overplan, but you definitely get more out of it if you research ahead.

name: Chris P.
review: Roatan involved a lot more driving than I expected. We booked a combo tour and spent a good chunk of time in a van. The stops were nice, just wish there was more time at each. Costa Maya we skipped excursions and stayed onboard. Cozumel was simple—we took a cab to a beach club and stayed there all day. That ended up being the most relaxing part of the trip.

name: Anika Patel
review: Cozumel felt the most developed and easiest to explore independently. We walked around town, did some shopping, and had lunch without needing a structured plan. Roatan was more about organized excursions, and we enjoyed a guided island tour that included scenic stops and a short beach break. Costa Maya was the only port where we felt unsure what to do without an excursion, so we didn’t spend much time off the ship there.

name: Marcus L
review: We tried to maximize each port and honestly it got exhausting. Roatan was a full-day excursion with multiple stops, Costa Maya was an early morning ruins trip, and by Cozumel we were worn out. Cozumel ended up being a simple walk around and early return to the ship. If I did this itinerary again, I’d plan at least one low-key port day.

name: Olivia Chen
review: Costa Maya was better than expected because we booked something ahead of time. A beach club outside the port had good service and fewer crowds. Roatan was beautiful but a bit chaotic at the port when disembarking. Cozumel felt the most touristy, but also had the most options. Each stop had its pros and cons, so it really depends on your travel style.

name: Brian O'Connor
review: Roatan had the best mix of activities. We did snorkeling in the morning and still had time to explore a bit of the island. Costa Maya felt more limited unless you booked something specific. Cozumel was fun but crowded, especially near the port area. I’d rank them Roatan first, Cozumel second, Costa Maya third.

name: Nadia Petrova
review: In Roatan we chose a chocolate-making excursion in a botanical setting, which was very unique compared to typical beach activities. Costa Maya we spent mostly walking around the port area, which felt artificial. Cozumel was easier to explore and had more variety. I appreciated having different types of experiences across the itinerary, even if not every port was equally strong.

name: Jordan B
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to enjoy without planning anything. Roatan required more coordination with excursions, and Costa Maya felt like you needed to leave the port area to really experience anything. We didn’t do that, so it ended up being our least favorite stop.

name: Fatima Al-Sayed
review: Roatan stood out because of the natural scenery. We booked a guided tour that included a beach and some local stops, and it felt more authentic than the other ports. Costa Maya felt very controlled and tourist-focused, especially near the port. Cozumel had a good balance—you could do excursions or just explore. Timing at each port felt adequate, though I wouldn’t have minded an extra hour in Roatan.

name: Kevin S.
review: We had mixed feelings about this itinerary. Roatan was excellent, especially for snorkeling. Costa Maya didn’t offer much unless you had an excursion planned, and we didn’t. Cozumel was enjoyable but crowded. I’d say one great stop, one decent, one weak.

name: Isabella Rossi
review: Costa Maya ended up being our favorite because we booked a smaller, more intimate experience away from the main port. Roatan was beautiful but felt rushed with the excursion schedule we chose. Cozumel was fun but busy, especially near the main shopping areas. I think the key with this itinerary is picking the right excursions for your preferences.

name: Derrick Johnson
review: Roatan had the best outdoor experiences overall, especially if you like water activities. Costa Maya felt like it required a longer excursion to really see anything interesting, which we didn’t commit to. Cozumel was easy but crowded. I appreciated the variety, but not every stop felt equally worthwhile.

name: Hannah K.
review: We approached each port differently, which made the trip more enjoyable. In Roatan we did a structured excursion, Costa Maya we stayed close to the port, and Cozumel we explored on our own. That variety helped balance things out. Roatan was probably the most memorable, but Cozumel was the easiest day. Costa Maya was just okay for us.

name: Alicia Gomez
review: Roatan ended up being my favorite stop, which surprised me. We booked a small group snorkeling trip, and the water clarity was unreal. I’ve snorkeled before but never saw reefs that vibrant so close to shore. The only downside was how rushed the return felt—we barely had time to dry off before heading back to the ship. In Costa Maya, we tried to explore on our own, but the port area felt very manufactured and crowded with shops. We eventually took a taxi to a quieter beach, which improved the day a lot. Cozumel felt more flexible; we just wandered downtown, grabbed drinks, and people-watched. It wasn’t as visually striking as Roatan, but it felt less rushed and easier to navigate.

name: Trevor L
review: Did a ruins excursion in Costa Maya and honestly the bus ride was the biggest part of it. The site itself was interesting, but I didn’t feel like we had enough time to really take it in before heading back. Roatan was more relaxed—we just found a beach club and stayed there most of the day. Cozumel was kind of in between, good for walking around but pretty tourist-heavy.

name: Nina Patel
review: As someone who travels a lot, I appreciated how different each stop felt. Roatan had this lush, slightly chaotic energy, especially once you leave the immediate port. We did a wildlife-focused tour and learned a lot, though I did question how ethical some of it was. Costa Maya was less appealing to me—very commercialized unless you go far out. Cozumel felt the easiest to enjoy independently; we rented bikes and explored along the coast. Timing-wise, I wish we had longer in Roatan because that’s where I felt there was the most to discover beyond the basics.

name: Marcus Hill
review: Roatan was cool but very humid, and our zipline excursion got delayed which cut into our beach time. Still fun, just felt a bit disorganized. Costa Maya didn’t do much for me—we stayed near the port and it felt like one big shopping plaza. Cozumel was better, we took a cab to a quieter area and swam for a few hours.

name: Lena K.
review: Cozumel stood out because we didn’t book anything and it still worked out great. We just walked, found a small café, and later ended up at a beach spot that wasn’t crowded. In Costa Maya, we joined a cooking class excursion that turned out to be more fun than expected—very interactive and the group was small. Roatan was beautiful but felt rushed. I wish the schedule allowed more time there because the island seemed like it had a lot more to offer beyond the short excursion window.

name: Derrick Johnson
review: We’ve cruised a few times and this itinerary was decent but uneven. Roatan had the best scenery by far. We did a guided island tour that included a beach stop, and that was the highlight of the trip. Costa Maya felt like you had to commit to a long excursion or else you were stuck in a very touristy zone. Cozumel was easy to navigate, but also crowded with other ships. Timing at all three ports felt slightly tight, especially if anything ran late.

name: Emily Chen
review: Roatan was incredible for snorkeling, probably the best I’ve done. Costa Maya was more about the excursion—we went to ruins, which were interesting but the travel time was long. Cozumel felt like a good balance, with enough to do close to the port without overplanning.

name: Jorge Ramirez
review: I traveled with my parents, so we picked calmer activities. In Roatan, we did a scenic drive and beach stop, which they really enjoyed. Costa Maya was tougher because the walking areas near the port were crowded and hot, and we didn’t want to do a long bus ride. Cozumel ended up being the easiest for our group—plenty of shaded spots and short taxi rides to quieter beaches. Overall, I’d say the itinerary works better if you plan ahead for Costa Maya specifically.

name: Sophia Bennett
review: Costa Maya surprised me in a good way. We booked a small beach club experience away from the main port, and it was peaceful and well organized. Roatan felt more adventurous—we did a jungle-based excursion and learned about local plants, which was unique but also very hot and buggy. Cozumel was more casual; we didn’t plan much and just explored. I appreciated that flexibility after two more structured port days. The only complaint I had was that time in port always seemed just a bit too short to fully relax.

name: Caleb W
review: Roatan was the highlight, hands down. Costa Maya felt like a missed opportunity since we didn’t book anything. Cozumel was fine, just crowded.

name: Fatima Noor
review: What stood out to me most was how different the pacing felt at each port. In Roatan, our excursion ran late returning, which made the whole group anxious about getting back in time. Costa Maya required more planning than I expected—without an excursion, there wasn’t much appeal beyond the immediate port area. Cozumel felt the most forgiving; even with no strict plans, we had a pleasant day exploring shops and waterfront areas. If I did it again, I’d prioritize Roatan and Costa Maya excursions more carefully.

name: Greg H
review: Didn’t get off in Costa Maya, just didn’t see the appeal after reading about it. Roatan was worth it though, especially for the water activities. Cozumel was easy to explore but very commercial.

name: Olivia Sanders
review: This was my first cruise, so I didn’t know what to expect from port days. Roatan felt like stepping into a completely different environment—lush, busy, and a bit chaotic once you left the port. We did a snorkeling trip and saw more marine life than I expected. Costa Maya was more structured since we booked a ruins excursion. The site itself was fascinating, but the long transportation time made the day feel rushed overall. Cozumel was my favorite for just relaxing; we found a quieter beach area and stayed there for hours. I liked having that balance between planned and unplanned days across the itinerary.

name: Andre Dupont
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to enjoy without planning anything in advance. Costa Maya required more effort, and Roatan was the most visually interesting but also the most hectic.

name: Rachel Kim
review: I appreciated Roatan the most because it felt less manufactured once you got outside the port. Costa Maya felt the opposite—very controlled unless you booked something farther away. Cozumel had a nice middle ground, especially for walking and casual exploring.

name: Samir Ali
review: Roatan had the best excursion we booked, a combination of sightseeing and beach time that felt well paced. Costa Maya was more challenging; without a solid plan, it felt like you were stuck in a tourist-heavy area. Cozumel was flexible and easy to navigate, which made for a relaxing final port day.

name: Heather J
review: We stayed on the ship in Costa Maya because none of the excursions appealed to us. Roatan and Cozumel were both worth getting off for though.

name: Daniela Rossi
review: Each port had its pros and cons, but the biggest factor for me was how time was managed. In Roatan, we booked a nature-focused excursion that included a guided walk through a forested area and a short beach stop. It was memorable, but we were constantly aware of the clock, which made it hard to fully relax. Costa Maya required a longer commitment; we chose to visit nearby ruins, which meant an early departure and a fairly long bus ride. The site itself was impressive, but I would have preferred either more time there or a shorter travel distance. Cozumel felt like a reset—we intentionally avoided excursions and just explored at our own pace. That ended up being the most relaxing day. If anything, I’d say this itinerary works best if you mix structured excursions with at least one completely open day.

name: Tyler Brooks
review: Roatan snorkeling alone made the trip worth it for me. Costa Maya was forgettable since we didn’t venture far. Cozumel had better options close to the port.

name: Isabella Torres
review: Costa Maya ended up being my favorite because we booked a smaller, less crowded excursion. Roatan was beautiful but felt rushed, and Cozumel was enjoyable mostly because we kept it simple and didn’t overplan.

name: Jordan Mitchell
review: Roatan was the highlight for us. We booked a private driver through a local company and went to the West End. The snorkeling right off the beach was incredible—better than any paid excursion I’ve done before. The only downside was the traffic getting back to the pier; it took almost an hour and we were getting a bit nervous about the

time. In Costa Maya, we decided to stay on the ship because the port area just felt like one giant shopping mall designed to take your money. It’s very polished but lacks any real soul. Cozumel was okay, but extremely crowded with five other ships in port. We walked a few blocks away from the main tourist drag and found a small place for lunch, which was much better than the overpriced spots right on the water. Overall, a decent itinerary but Roatan definitely stole the show for our group.

name: Elena_Rodriguez77
review: Cozumel was fantastic! We did the Amazing Race excursion where you run around the downtown area solving clues. It was a great way to see the city instead of just sitting at a beach bar. My legs are sore today but it was worth it. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown though. The walk from the ship to the exit of the port is surprisingly long, especially in that humidity. We took a taxi to Mahahual, which was pretty, but the vendors there are very aggressive. Every two steps someone is trying to sell you a massage or a cigar. It makes it hard to actually relax. We ended up just heading back to the ship early. We missed Roatan due to high winds and not being able to dock, which was disappointing, but that's the risk you take with cruising. I wish we had more time in Cozumel to compensate.

name: BigTravelerDave
review: Just okay. Roatan was pretty but the excursion we did to the sloth sanctuary felt very rushed. We had maybe 10 minutes with the animals and then were shuffled into a gift shop for half an hour. I hate that. Costa Maya port is basically a Disney-fied version of Mexico. If you don't leave the port area, you haven't seen Mexico. We did the ruins tour and the bus had no A/C. 50 minutes of sweating each way. The ruins themselves were cool, but the logistics were a mess.

name: Sarah Jenkins
review: This was my first time visiting these ports. In Costa Maya, we went to Maya Chan and it was the best day of the trip. It’s a bit of a bumpy ride to get there, but the service was amazing and the food was actually authentic. It felt very private. In Cozumel, we did the dolphin swim. It was fun for the kids, but honestly, the facility felt a bit small for the number of people they had in there. We didn't do much in Roatan except walk around the port area because the weather looked like it was going to turn. The port in Roatan is nice enough for a short walk, but there isn't much to do without an excursion. I think next time I'd plan better for Roatan. The itinerary overall is great if you love water activities, but it can feel a bit repetitive if you aren't a big swimmer.

name: Kwame O.
review: Roatan was beautiful but the port pier is quite long. We did a catamaran snorkel trip and the reef was healthy with lots of fish. For Costa Maya, we did the Chacchoben ruins. It’s a long drive but our guide was very knowledgeable about Mayan history. Cozumel was just a shopping day for us. We went to Los Cinco Soles and bought some vanilla and pottery. It’s a reliable stop.

name: M_Thompson_92
review: Cozumel is a bit too commercialized for my taste now. I’ve been there three times and it just gets more crowded every year. We took the ferry over to Playa del Carmen this time, which was a mistake because the ferry ride was extremely rough and half the people on board were sick. By the time we got there, we only had two hours before we had to head back. Costa Maya was much better; we rented a golf cart and just drove along the coast until we found a quiet spot. Much more my speed. In Roatan, we did the zipline. It was exhilarating and the views of the ocean from the top of the mountain were breath-taking. I think if I did this itinerary again, I would just stay on the ship in Cozumel and enjoy the empty pool. The port times were generally good, though I felt Costa Maya was a bit short.

name: Linda G.
review: The ports were the reason we booked. Roatan ziplining was a blast, and the sloths were so cute! My only complaint is the pier at Costa Maya. It is so long and they don't have enough shuttles for people with mobility issues. My husband struggled quite a bit. Once we got to the port, it was fine, but that walk is brutal in the heat. In Cozumel, we just did a beach day at Mr. Sanchos. It was loud and crowded, but the drinks were flowing so everyone seemed happy. Not really my scene, but the kids liked the inflatable water park. I think I prefer the more natural excursions.

name: Chris and Sam
review: We are experienced cruisers and this itinerary is a classic. We usually skip the ship excursions and book private. In Roatan, we used a local guide for a private island tour. Best decision ever. We saw the local neighborhoods and a beautiful hidden beach away from the cruise crowds. Costa Maya is still a bit of a 'fake' port to me, but the tequila tasting near the fountain was actually informative and fun. Cozumel was our 'adventure' day where we rented Jeeps and drove to the other side of the island. It’s much more rugged and beautiful over there with no electricity and just small beach bars. It’s a totally different vibe from the downtown area where the ships dock. I highly recommend getting away from the pier in all three of these locations if you want a real experience. The scheduling was fine, but we felt a bit rushed in Roatan.

name: Pat_H_1955
review: Costa Maya was disappointing. Too much construction and the port area is very confusing to navigate. We tried to find the taxi stand and ended up walking in circles for twenty minutes. Cozumel was much better. We did a glass bottom boat tour which was perfect for us since we don't swim well. Saw lots of coral and even an old shipwreck. Roatan was lovely but very hilly.

name: Jessica Low
review: I loved the variety on this trip! Roatan had the best nature—the chocolate factory tour was so cool and the jungle smells amazing after it rains. Costa Maya was our beach day; we just sat under a palapa in Mahahual and watched the waves. It was very relaxing, though the seaweed was a bit thick in some spots. Cozumel was our big excursion day where we went to the San Gervasio ruins. They aren't as big as the ones on the mainland, but they were very peaceful and not nearly as crowded. I felt like we had plenty of time in each port, though I wish Cozumel stayed open a bit later into the evening so we could have had dinner on land. The transition from the ship to the shore was easy at all three stops, which isn't always the case. Definitely a solid itinerary for people who like a mix of history and relaxation.

name: Amit Sharma
review: For Roatan, I suggest the West Bay beach. The water is crystal clear. We took a water taxi from the pier which was cheap and fast. In Cozumel, we did the chocolate and tequila tour. It was a bit touristy but the samples were good. Costa Maya was our least favorite stop as it felt like we were just being funneled into shops the whole time. We ended up going back to the ship early for lunch.

name: BeachLover_88
review: Cozumel was 10/10. We went to Chen Rio on the east side. No crowds, just beautiful blue water and great fish tacos. It's a bit of a drive but so worth it to escape the thousands of people from the ships. Roatan was also great for snorkeling, though the bugs were biting even with spray. Costa Maya was okay, but the port feels like a maze designed to keep you from leaving. We finally found the exit and walked to the local village. It's much cheaper once you get out of the main port zone. The tacos in the village were only $2 compared to $12 in the port. Be sure to bring cash!

name: Gary V.
review: The ports were fine but the time in Roatan was too short. We didn't get to the pier until 10:00 AM and had to be back by 5:00 PM. By the time you get through the crowds and find your tour, half the day is gone. We did the monkey farm which was fun for the grandkids, but it felt like we were constantly checking our watches. Cozumel had much better timing. We stayed there until nearly sunset which allowed us to actually explore the town without panicking about the ship leaving. Costa Maya is what it is—a tourist trap, but the pool in the middle of the port is nice if you just want to cool off without paying for a beach club. I think the cruise line needs to adjust the Roatan schedule.

name: S_Kim_Adventures
review: Costa Maya ruins were the highlight. Chacchoben is stunning and the trees provide a lot of shade which was needed. Our guide was a local and told us so much about the plants and animals too. Roatan was a bit of a mess for us because our excursion was cancelled last minute, so we just wandered around the port. It’s okay but fairly limited. Cozumel snorkeling was top notch. We saw a sea turtle and several rays. The water is so blue it doesn't look real. Great trip for photographers.

name: Robert L.
review: I'm not much of an excursion person. I usually just get off and walk. Cozumel is great for that since the town is right there. Lots of little shops and bars. Roatan is not as walkable; you really need a car or a bus to get anywhere interesting. I stayed on the ship in Costa Maya because I've been there before and it's just not my thing. The port is too loud and busy. It was nice having the ship almost to myself for a few hours though. Good weather at all stops.

name: Mariah_C
review: We did the cooking class in Cozumel and it was the best thing we did all week. We learned to make three different types of salsa and authentic ceviche. The chef was hilarious. In Roatan, we just went to the beach. It was pretty but very crowded. I think every single person on the ship went to the same beach. Costa Maya was a bit of a bore for us, we just did some souvenir shopping and went back on. I think I would skip Costa Maya next time or book a much longer tour to see something unique.

name: Tom_D_Cruiser
review: Decent ports. Roatan had some issues with the bus transport for our excursion—we waited for 45 minutes in a parking lot with no shade. Not ideal. Once we got to the beach it was fine, but the organization was poor. Costa Maya port is nice for what it is. I liked the salt water pool they have there. Cozumel is always a solid stop for diving. I went out with a local shop and the reefs are still looking good despite all the traffic. I'd do this itinerary again but maybe just do my own thing in Roatan.

name: Nancy P.
review: I loved the flowers in Roatan! We went to a botanical garden and it was so peaceful. Cozumel was a bit too fast-paced for me. So many people everywhere yelling about taxi rides and tours. It’s a bit overwhelming if you aren't used to it. Costa Maya was much calmer if you stayed in the port area. I enjoyed the dancers they had performing near the entrance. It was a nice touch of culture.

name: Zane_Explorer
review: Costa Maya's port is a maze. Seriously, follow the painted lines on the ground or you'll never find the exit to the actual town. We eventually made it to the malecon and had a great day. Roatan snorkeling was legendary. We went to a spot called West End Wall and the drop-off was incredible. Cozumel was just okay; we've been there so many times it's lost its charm. We just grabbed some guac and chips and went back to the ship to avoid the heat. The port stayed open late which was a plus.

name: Hargrove_Family
review: Traveling with three kids is hard but these ports made it easy. Roatan's monkey park was the biggest hit. The kids are still talking about the monkeys jumping on their heads. In Costa Maya, we just stayed at the port pool because the kids didn't want to get on another bus. It was free and kept them entertained for hours. Cozumel we did a submarine tour. It was a bit pricey but a once-in-a-lifetime thing for the little ones to see the reef without having to swim. All in all, a very family-friendly set of destinations. We didn't feel unsafe at any point, which was a concern of mine before we left. Everything was well-organized.

name: Hannah Brooks
review: Roatan was easily the highlight for me. We booked a snorkeling excursion and the water clarity was unreal, like you could see everything without even trying. The only issue was timing because we felt rushed getting back to the ship. Costa Maya was kind of disappointing unless you book something. We stayed near the port at first and it felt like a giant shopping complex. We ended up taking a taxi to a quieter beach which helped salvage the day. Cozumel was more flexible. We didn’t plan anything and just walked around, grabbed drinks, and found a spot by the water. Overall I liked having a mix of structured and unstructured days.

name: Marcus D
review: Did the ruins in Costa Maya and the bus ride was longer than expected. Cool site but felt rushed. Roatan beach day was better. Cozumel was crowded but easy to get around.

name: Priya Shah
review: Each port had a very different feel which I appreciated. Roatan felt more natural and less built up once you left the port. We did a wildlife and beach combo tour which was fun but also raised some questions for me about how the animals are treated. Costa Maya felt very commercial unless you go far out, which we didn’t. Cozumel was the easiest to explore independently. We rented bikes and rode along the coast, stopping whenever we wanted. That flexibility made it my favorite day even though Roatan was probably more visually impressive.

name: Ethan Cole
review: Roatan zipline was fun but got delayed so we lost time at the beach. Costa Maya didn’t impress me much. Cozumel was decent once we got away from the crowds.

name: Maya Lopez
review: Cozumel worked really well for us because we didn’t want a structured day. We just wandered and found a small beach club that wasn’t too busy. Costa Maya was the opposite—we booked a cooking class excursion and that ended up being one of the more memorable experiences. Very hands-on and the group was small. Roatan was beautiful but I wish we had more time there. It felt like we barely scratched the surface before needing to head back.

name: Darius Green
review: Roatan had the best scenery and felt the most unique compared to the other stops. We did a guided island tour that included a beach break, which was well organized. Costa Maya was tricky because if you don’t have an excursion planned, you’re kind of stuck in a very tourist-focused area. Cozumel was easier to navigate but also more crowded with other ships in port. I think this itinerary works best if you plan ahead for at least two of the stops.

name: Lily Tran
review: Roatan snorkeling was amazing, probably the best part of the trip. Costa Maya ruins were interesting but the travel time took up a lot of the day. Cozumel was nice for just walking around.

name: Carlos Mendez
review: Traveling with my parents, we needed easier options. Roatan worked well with a short island tour and beach stop. Costa Maya was harder because most things required longer travel. Cozumel ended up being the easiest since everything was relatively close and accessible.

name: Samantha Reed
review: Costa Maya ended up being better than I expected, mostly because we booked a smaller beach club away from the main port. It felt relaxed and not overcrowded. Roatan was more adventurous—we did a jungle excursion that was interesting but also very hot and a little chaotic. Cozumel was the most laid back day since we didn’t book anything. I do wish we had a bit more time in each port because everything felt slightly rushed, especially when excursions ran even a little late.

name: Jordan Blake
review: Roatan was the standout stop. Costa Maya felt skippable unless you plan something. Cozumel was fine but very touristy.

name: Amina Yusuf
review: The biggest thing I noticed was how much planning impacted each port day. In Roatan, our excursion ran behind schedule which made everyone nervous about getting back on time. Costa Maya required more effort than expected because the port area alone wasn’t very appealing to us. Cozumel felt more forgiving, we didn’t have anything booked and still had a nice day just exploring. If I did this itinerary again, I would definitely prioritize booking something meaningful in Costa Maya.

name: Greg T
review: Skipped Costa Maya entirely and stayed onboard. Roatan and Cozumel were worth getting off for though, especially for water activities.

name: Olivia Hart
review: This was my first cruise and I wasn’t sure what to expect from the ports. Roatan felt very lush and different from anything I’ve experienced before. We did snorkeling and saw a lot of marine life. Costa Maya was more structured since we booked a ruins excursion. The ruins were impressive, but the long bus ride made it feel rushed. Cozumel was my favorite for relaxing. We found a quieter beach area and just stayed there most of the day without worrying about a schedule.

name: Jean-Pierre L
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to enjoy without planning. Costa Maya required more effort, and Roatan was the most interesting visually but also the most hectic.

name: Rachel Nguyen
review: Roatan felt the most authentic once you left the port area. Costa Maya felt very controlled and commercialized. Cozumel was a good middle ground with both options available.

name: Omar Haddad
review: Roatan had the best excursion we booked, a mix of sightseeing and beach time that felt balanced. Costa Maya was more difficult to enjoy without a plan. Cozumel was flexible and easy to navigate, which made for a relaxing day.

name: Jessica P
