review: We stayed on the ship in Costa Maya since nothing stood out to us. Roatan and Cozumel were both worth visiting though.

name: Elena Petrova
review: From a logistics perspective, each port required a different strategy. Roatan excursions felt slightly rushed, especially with transportation delays getting back. Costa Maya involved committing to longer travel times if you wanted to see anything beyond the port, which we did by visiting ruins. The site itself was impressive but I wish we had more time there. Cozumel was the easiest to manage. We skipped excursions and just explored at our own pace, which ended up being the most relaxing day of the trip. I think the itinerary works best if you balance structured activities with at least one flexible day.

name: Tyler Benson
review: Roatan snorkeling made the trip for me. Costa Maya was forgettable. Cozumel had decent options close to the port.

name: Isabella Cruz
review: Costa Maya ended up being my favorite because we chose a smaller excursion away from crowds. Roatan was beautiful but felt rushed, and Cozumel was enjoyable mainly because we kept things simple.

name: Marcus L.
review: Roatan was incredible but the logistics were a bit of a headache. We booked the shipwreck snorkel and while the reef was pristine, the bus drive from the pier took nearly an hour due to some local construction. It felt like we only got 45 minutes in the water before we had to dry off and head back. Cozumel was a much smoother experience; we just walked off the boat and grabbed a taxi to the town square. Had some of the best street tacos of my life and did a bit of silver shopping. Costa Maya was purely a beach day for us at the port pool. It's convenient if you don't want to deal with the hassle of excursions, but it definitely feels more like a theme park than a destination. I wish we had more time in Roatan to actually see the island instead of just sitting in traffic.

name: travel-bug-sarah
review: Costa Maya was okay but the pier is ridiculously long. If you have mobility issues, wait for the tram! We did the Chacchoben ruins and the guide was fantastic, but the 50-minute bus ride each way in a van with weak AC was rough. Cozumel was much better; we did a private Jeep tour to the wild side of the island. No electricity or crowds, just raw coastline. Roatan was beautiful but the insects are no joke—bring the heavy-duty spray.

name: D_Chen88
review: Cozumel was 100% the highlight for my husband and me. We did the clear kayak excursion and seeing the fish right through the bottom of the boat was surreal. For Costa Maya, we actually stayed on the ship. We’ve been there before and the port area is just too touristy for our liking. It was nice to have the pool to ourselves while everyone else was fighting for space at the beach clubs. In Roatan, we visited the Maya Key private island. It was a very short boat ride from the pier and had great snorkeling right off the dock. It was a nice mix of relaxation and activity. The timing at each port felt sufficient, though Cozumel always feels like it needs a full two days to really explore. Highly recommend the kayak tour if you want something unique!

name: Linda G.
review: Just got back and Roatan was my favorite. We did the ‘Easy Roatan’ tour which was perfect for seniors. No crazy hiking, just a nice air-conditioned bus showing us the views and the local culture. Cozumel was way too crowded for me. There were five ships in port and you could barely walk on the sidewalks downtown. We ended up going back to the ship after an hour. Costa Maya was better; the port shopping is great if you like to haggle for souvenirs.

name: Jared_V_Cruises
review: Third time on this itinerary. In Costa Maya, skip the port pool and take a $4 taxi to Mahahual. The beach there is way better and you’re supporting local businesses. We sat at a little place called Nohoch Kay and the grilled lobster was phenomenal. Roatan is getting very crowded; we did the sloths but it felt like an assembly line. Cozumel was great as always—rented a scooter and just did a lap around the island. Best way to see it without the tour bus crowds.

name: Aditi Sharma
review: I was really looking forward to the ruins in Costa Maya, but the tour was cancelled due to a mechanical issue with the bus. We were pretty bummed. We ended up just staying in the port area which is basically one big mall. In Cozumel, we did the chocolate tasting and it was actually very educational. I didn't realize how much history goes into it! Roatan was the best for nature. We did a botanical garden walk and saw some amazing birds. I just wish the ports weren't so geared towards selling you plastic trinkets. The natural beauty of Honduras is stunning, but you have to look past the tourist traps to find it. Next time I'd book a private car in Roatan to see the East End where it's supposedly much quieter. Cozumel is still the king of shopping though.

name: pete_the_pilot
review: Roatan snorkeling was world-class. Visibility was at least 60 feet. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown—just felt very manufactured. Cozumel is always fun for a pub crawl, but the pier is a long walk after a few margaritas. Overall, a solid itinerary for water lovers.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: This was my first cruise and I loved Cozumel. We did a salsa and salsa class—making salsa and dancing salsa! It was so much fun and the people were so high energy. Roatan was also lovely; we just went to West Bay Beach. The water was like glass. Costa Maya was my least favorite. The ‘port’ feels fake and the prices for everything are double what they are in Cozumel. I wish we had swapped the Costa Maya day for another day in Honduras.

name: TheMillerFamily
review: Costa Maya was a struggle with the kids. The walk from the ship to the taxis is long and there's not much shade. Once we got to the water park it was fine, but getting there was stressful. Cozumel was much easier. We did the glass-bottom boat and it kept the little ones entertained without them having to get in the water. Roatan was the highlight for the teens; they did the zipline and said it was the scariest and coolest thing they've ever done. I liked the chocolate factory there. It’s a good itinerary for families but be prepared for a lot of walking in the heat. Make sure to bring plenty of small bills for tips and taxis in Mexico, as they aren't great about making change for twenty-dollar bills. Roatan accepted US dollars everywhere we went.

name: Greg_Hargrove
review: Roatan was okay but very hilly. If you get motion sick, the bus rides on the island will be a challenge for you. Cozumel was great for diving—I did a two-tank dive and saw a nurse shark and a few turtles. The port in Costa Maya is beautiful but definitely a 'tourist bubble'. I wouldn't call it authentic Mexico, but it's safe and clean if that's what you're looking for. The shops there were a bit pushy though. Good time overall.

name: S_Jackson_99
review: We did the private cabana in Cozumel and it was worth every penny. Private waiter, quiet beach, and no one trying to sell us hair braiding. Roatan was a bit more rugged. We did a mangrove tour in a small boat which was cool but very hot. Costa Maya was just a shopping day for us. The port is huge and has a lot of variety, but it’s easy to get lost in the maze of shops. Make sure you leave enough time to walk back to the ship!

name: Nadia_V
review: Roatan is beautiful but the port area (Coxen Hole) is a bit chaotic when you first get off. Lots of people shouting for tours. We found our guide eventually and went to the Iguana farm which was a huge hit with my daughter. For Cozumel, we took the ferry to the mainland to see Tulum. It's a long day and the ferry can be very choppy, so take ginger pills! The ruins are spectacular but very crowded. Costa Maya was just a chill day by the pool for us. It’s a good mix of ports, but I felt a bit rushed at the ruins because we had to catch the ferry back. If I did it again, I’d stay on Cozumel island and skip the mainland trip just to save on the travel time. The water in Roatan is some of the clearest I've ever seen, even better than the Bahamas in my opinion.

name: Bill_from_Buffalo
review: Costa Maya has some of the best snorkeling I've ever seen near a port. We just swam out from the beach and saw tons of parrotfish. Roatan was a bit of a letdown as our tour was late picking us up, which cut our beach time in half. Cozumel was great—always love the atmosphere at the No Name Bar.

name: Chloe_Adventures
review: I loved Roatan! We did a horseback riding tour on the beach and it was like something out of a movie. The horses were well cared for and the guides were very friendly. Cozumel was our 'foodie' day. We did a walking tour of the local markets and tried real cochinita pibil. Costa Maya was mostly just for souvenirs. I got a beautiful hand-painted bowl there. The whole itinerary was great, though I wish we had more time in the actual towns and less time in the gated port areas. It’s hard to get a feel for the real culture when you’re surrounded by other cruise passengers. But for a week-long getaway, it hit all the right notes for relaxation and a little bit of adventure. I would definitely recommend the horseback riding to anyone visiting Honduras, it was the best part of my entire year!

name: Robert M.
review: Cozumel was fine but very commercial. Costa Maya was similar. Roatan was the only place that felt like we were actually in a different country. The greenery and the mountains are gorgeous. We did the eco-walk over the suspension bridges and the views were incredible. Highly recommend that if you aren't afraid of heights.

name: Isabella-Cruises
review: The ports were a mixed bag. I loved the history in Costa Maya—the ruins are a must-see even if the drive is long. Roatan was stunning but we had a bad experience with a local taxi driver who tried to overcharge us at the end of the day. It left a bit of a sour taste in my mouth. Cozumel was the most professional; everything is streamlined for tourists. We did the submarine tour and it was fascinating to see the reef from 100 feet down. I think the itinerary is good, but you have to be careful in Roatan if you aren't on an official ship excursion. The time spent in each port was mostly okay, but we felt very rushed in Costa Maya because the ruins tour takes up almost the whole window. I'd love an extra two hours there just to relax after the long bus ride back.

name: Kwame_O
review: Roatan sloths were the highlight. My wife loved it. Costa Maya was a bit of a tourist trap, honestly. We walked through the shops and went right back to the ship for lunch. Cozumel was great for a beach day at Nachi Cocom. Very private and quiet compared to the other clubs. Good mix for a first-time cruiser.

name: Janelle_T
review: In Cozumel, we did the 'Chef's Table' land excursion and it was amazing. Learning about the different spices used in Mexican cooking was a highlight. Roatan was a bit difficult for us because of the humidity; we did a short walking tour but had to cut it short. Costa Maya was beautiful but the port music is so loud! It’s hard to have a conversation.

name: Sam_and_Deb
review: We’ve done this route before and it’s always solid. In Roatan, we went to West End and just hung out at a local bar. Much cheaper than the excursions. Costa Maya we did the Bacalar Lagoon. It is a very long trip from the port—almost two hours—but the water is seven shades of blue. It’s incredible. Cozumel was just for picking up some tequila and vanilla. The port logistics were fine, but the Costa Maya pier is always a trek. I really think people should know that Bacalar is a full-day commitment; you won't have time for anything else in Costa Maya if you go there, but it is worth the travel time. We barely made it back to the ship before the gangway was pulled up, so it's a bit of a nail-biter if you aren't on a ship-sponsored tour. Otherwise, a great trip.

name: Marcus_The_Elder
review: Roatan was too crowded for the small roads they have. Our bus was stuck in a jam for 30 minutes. Cozumel was better organized. We did the museum and it was very quiet and educational. Costa Maya is just one big shopping mall, which isn't my style. I stayed on the ship there.

name: Marisol Vega
review: Roatan ended up being my favorite stop mostly because we booked a small group island tour that included a wildlife sanctuary. Holding a sloth felt surreal, even if it was a bit touristy. The guide also drove us through local neighborhoods which gave a better sense of daily life beyond the port. Costa Maya felt more manufactured to me. We walked past the shopping area and took a taxi to the beach, which was relaxing but nothing special. Cozumel was somewhere in between—we did a snorkeling trip and saw decent marine life, but it felt crowded with multiple boats in the same area. Timing-wise, Roatan felt just right, Costa Maya felt like too much idle time, and Cozumel felt slightly rushed.

name: Ethan R.
review: We skipped an excursion in Costa Maya and just wandered outside the port. Big mistake. It quickly turns into a strip of vendors and pushy sales tactics. Next time I’d book something structured. Roatan was better—we did ziplining through the jungle which was fun and well organized. Cozumel was easy to explore on our own, we just grabbed a cab downtown and walked around.

name: Tanya Okafor
review: Cozumel was the highlight for me because I’m into water activities. We did a reef snorkeling excursion and the water clarity was incredible. I did feel like the group was too large though, and it became chaotic getting in and out of the boat. Roatan was more relaxed—we visited a beach club and just lounged most of the day, which I needed. Costa Maya was the only stop I didn’t connect with. The port area feels like a maze of shops and it’s hard to tell what’s authentic. We ended up back on the ship earlier than expected because we ran out of things we wanted to do.

name: Jake_Midwest
review: Roatan surprised me in a good way. We booked a snorkeling and beach combo and the reef there seemed healthier than I expected. Costa Maya we did the Mayan ruins, which were interesting but the bus ride was long and early. Cozumel we just stayed near the port and shopped, nothing too memorable honestly.

name: Linh Tran
review: In Roatan, we did a cultural tour that included a chocolate-making experience. It felt more interactive than most excursions because you actually participate instead of just watching. Costa Maya was very hot and we underestimated that, so by the time we reached the ruins we were already drained. Still worth seeing, but I wish we had chosen something less physically demanding. Cozumel was the easiest port logistically. Taxis were straightforward and we spent the day at a quieter beach club away from the crowds. Overall, I appreciated how different each port felt, even if not all of them were equally enjoyable.

name: Carlos Mendez
review: We rented a golf cart in Costa Maya and drove along the coast, stopping wherever looked good. That ended up being one of the most flexible days of the trip. Roatan was more structured since we did an organized excursion with animals and snorkeling. Cozumel felt very commercial but still fun if you like shopping and bars.

name: Hannah Goldberg
review: I’ve cruised a few times and this itinerary was decent but not perfect. Roatan stood out because we booked a private driver who customized the day—snorkeling, a short hike, and lunch at a local spot. That felt authentic. Costa Maya, on the other hand, felt like it was built purely for cruise traffic. We walked beyond the port and found a quieter beach, but it took effort. Cozumel was predictable but enjoyable. We did a catamaran snorkel, which was fun but definitely crowded. Timing at each port felt slightly uneven, with Costa Maya having more time than we needed and Cozumel not quite enough.

name: Devon Lee
review: Cozumel was our lazy day. We didn’t book anything, just walked around and ended up at a small beach bar. Roatan we did ziplining which was exciting but a little rushed. Costa Maya we stayed on the ship because none of the excursions really appealed to us.

name: Aisha Khan
review: Roatan felt the most vibrant culturally. Our guide pointed out schools, markets, and different communities, which added context beyond just activities. Costa Maya was more about the excursion itself—we visited ruins and learned about Mayan history, which I appreciated, but the experience felt somewhat scripted. Cozumel was very tourist-forward, but I still enjoyed a beach club where the water was calm and clear. I noticed that Cozumel had the most crowd overlap with other ships, which made certain areas feel congested. Overall, I valued Roatan the most because it felt like we actually saw the island rather than just passing through.

name: Ryan D.
review: Costa Maya ruins were cool but the travel time was rough. Almost two hours total on a bus. Roatan was more fun overall, especially the snorkeling. Cozumel had good shopping but felt crowded with multiple ships in port.

name: Sophie Laurent
review: What stood out to me was how different each port felt in terms of pacing. In Roatan, our excursion had a nice balance—we visited a wildlife park, then had free time at a beach. It never felt rushed. Costa Maya was the opposite. The excursion to the ruins started very early, and everything felt tightly scheduled. I would have preferred a slower pace. Cozumel gave us more freedom since we planned our own day, but it was also the busiest port. Finding a quiet place required walking further than expected. I enjoyed all three, but the structure versus flexibility made a big difference in how each day felt.

name: Marcus J
review: Roatan was easily the best stop for me. The water was clear and the snorkeling was better than expected. Costa Maya felt like a shopping mall with a beach attached. Cozumel was fun but very touristy, lots of bars and souvenir shops.

name: Elena Petrova
review: We traveled as a couple and prioritized quieter experiences. In Cozumel, we found a smaller beach club with limited capacity, which made a huge difference compared to the crowded main areas. Roatan offered a mix—we did a guided island tour that included both scenic viewpoints and a short snorkeling stop. Costa Maya was the most challenging to enjoy independently. Once you leave the port, transportation options become a bit unclear unless prearranged. I would recommend booking something ahead of time there. The ports themselves were interesting, but the level of planning required varied a lot.

name: Tyler_and_Kim
review: We did a combo excursion in Cozumel with snorkeling and a beach break, which was fun but felt very staged. Roatan was more relaxed and felt less crowded. Costa Maya we honestly didn’t enjoy much, just walked around and went back early.

name: Noah Williams
review: Roatan was where we felt most engaged. The excursion included multiple stops—wildlife interaction, snorkeling, and a short city tour. It felt like good value for the time. Costa Maya was more hit or miss. We visited a beach club that was nice but fairly standard. Cozumel had the most developed infrastructure, which made it easy to navigate, but also made it feel less unique. I think expectations matter a lot—if you’re looking for convenience, Cozumel delivers, but if you want something different, Roatan stands out.

name: Priya S.
review: Costa Maya cooking class was actually really fun. Small group and hands-on. Roatan was nice but our excursion felt a bit rushed between stops. Cozumel we explored on our own and liked that freedom more than the organized tours.

name: George K.
review: I’ve been to Cozumel before so I skipped getting off this time. Roatan we did a fishing excursion which was unique but long. Costa Maya we just stayed near the port area and didn’t venture far.

name: Amelia Chen
review: This itinerary gave a good mix, but I think your experience depends heavily on what you book. In Roatan, we chose a smaller, locally run tour and it made all the difference. The guide shared personal stories and took us to less crowded spots. Costa Maya felt more generic, even though the ruins themselves were impressive. The logistics there—long bus rides, early departures—made the day feel tiring. Cozumel was the easiest to navigate and had the most options, but also the most people. If I had to rank them, Roatan first for authenticity, Cozumel second for convenience, and Costa Maya last due to the effort required.

name: Derrick L
review: Roatan ziplining was fun but over too quickly. Costa Maya we rented a cabana at a beach club which was relaxing. Cozumel snorkeling was decent but crowded with other groups.

name: Isabella Rossi
review: I went into this cruise mostly interested in cultural experiences, and the ports delivered that unevenly but still memorably. Roatan exceeded expectations because we booked a small-group excursion that included a visit to a local community, a short jungle walk, and time near the water. It felt immersive without being overwhelming. Costa Maya was more complicated. The ruins were fascinating and visually striking, but the long transportation time made the day feel dominated by logistics rather than exploration. Cozumel, while more commercial, offered flexibility. We opted out of excursions and created our own plan, which worked well but required some research beforehand. I noticed that crowd levels significantly impacted enjoyment, especially in Cozumel where multiple ships were docked. Overall, I appreciated the variety, but planning ahead was key to getting the most out of each stop.

name: Sarah J.
review: Roatan was the highlight of our entire trip. We booked a private driver named Victor who took us away from the main port area which felt a bit chaotic and crowded. He took us to a small sloth sanctuary where my daughter got to hold a sloth—it was a dream come true for her! After that, we spent the afternoon at West Bay Beach. The water was crystal clear and the snorkeling right off the shore was better than most paid boat tours I've been on. We did feel a bit rushed getting back because the traffic in Roatan is no joke, and the narrow roads made us nervous about the 'all aboard' time. In Cozumel, we just did a quick walk through the shops. It’s very commercialized now with the same stores you see at every Caribbean port. We ended up just grabbing some margaritas at a local spot and heading back early to enjoy the quiet ship.

name: Mike Henderson
review: Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown for us. The port area is basically a giant outdoor mall designed to keep you there spending money. We decided to take a taxi to Mahahual, which was much better, but the seaweed on the beach was quite heavy that day, so we couldn't really swim. We ended up just sitting at a beach bar with some chips and salsa. Cozumel was much more active. We did the Amazing Race excursion which was a blast! We were running all over the downtown area solving clues. It was a great way to see the city instead of just sitting on a bus. My only complaint was that we only had about an hour of free time left after the race before we had to be back at the pier. I wish the ship stayed in Cozumel until 8 or 9 PM so we could have had a proper dinner in town.

name: Traveler_Chris99
review: Did the Chacchoben ruins in Costa Maya. The bus ride is about an hour each way, but the guide was very knowledgeable about Mayan history. Walking through the jungle to see the temples was incredible, though it was extremely humid. Make sure you bring bug spray! In Roatan, we just stayed on the ship. We've been there before and didn't feel like dealing with the pushy tour operators at the gate.

name: Linda G.
review: Cozumel is always a favorite. We went to Mr. Sanchos for the day. It’s an all-inclusive beach club and they really take care of you. The food was surprisingly good for a buffet and the drinks kept coming. It was very crowded though, so if you want peace and quiet, this isn't it. Roatan was beautiful but the pier at Coxen Hole is very industrial looking. We did a catamaran snorkel trip and saw some great coral. The crew was fun but the equipment was a bit dated. Costa Maya was our last stop. We just walked the pier and did some souvenir shopping. The walk from the ship to the actual port exit is very long, especially in the heat, so take the little tram if it's running. Overall, a solid itinerary but I felt like the port times were a little short for Roatan.

name: AdventureSeeker88
review: I was really looking forward to Roatan but the weather didn't cooperate. It rained most of the morning so our ziplining excursion was canceled. We ended up just taking a taxi to a chocolate factory which was okay, but a bit of a 'tourist trap' feel. Cozumel saved the trip though! We rented a Jeep and drove to the other side of the island. It’s completely undeveloped and rugged—no electricity, just small bars on the beach. It felt like a totally different world compared to the busy cruise terminal. We found a small spot called Coconuts on a cliff and the view was breathtaking. Costa Maya was fine, we just did the pool at the port which was free and actually quite nice. I liked that I could see the ship while I was swimming. Just watch out for the drink prices at the port bars, they are higher than on the ship!

name: James P. Richardson
review: The itinerary was okay but I felt like we spent too much time in Costa Maya and not enough in Cozumel. In Costa Maya, we did the Maya Chan beach club. It’s a long, bumpy ride on a dirt road to get there, but once you arrive, it’s paradise. They limit the number of guests so it felt very private. The food was authentic Mexican and delicious. In Cozumel, we barely had time to do anything. We booked a snorkeling excursion through the ship that started late, so by the time we got back, we had maybe 45 minutes to shop before we had to board. Roatan was great for diving. I did a two-tank dive and the reef is in much better shape than what I saw in Cozumel. The visibility was easily 80 feet. I would definitely go back to Roatan just for the diving.

name: Bethany_W
review: Costa Maya ruins were cool but the mosquitoes were brutal. Ruined the vibe for me honestly. Roatan snorkeling was way better. We saw a sea turtle and several rays. The port in Cozumel is just too many people for me.

name: Carlos M.
review: Our day in Roatan was spent at Maya Key. It’s a short boat ride from the pier. They have a small animal rescue and a nice beach. It was very convenient for a family with kids. Cozumel was all about the shopping for my wife. We went to the main square and found some nice silver jewelry. You have to bargain though! They start the prices very high. Costa Maya was my favorite because we just did a simple beach day at Mahahual. The malecon there is great for walking and there are tons of places to eat. We had some amazing fish tacos for very cheap. I preferred the laid-back vibe of Costa Maya over the hectic feel of Cozumel. All in all, a great trip, though I wish we didn't have to tender in any of the ports.

name: CruisinSusan
review: Cozumel - Chocolate and Tequila tour was fun and informative. The lunch provided was authentic and the tequila samples were generous. Roatan - West End was beautiful but the traffic getting back to the ship was stressful. Costa Maya - Stayed in the port area. It's clean and safe but very artificial.

name: Robert L.
review: I’ve been to Cozumel ten times, so this time I decided to try something different and took the ferry over to Playa del Carmen to see the Tulum ruins. It’s a long day and I was worried about the ferry schedule, but it was worth it. The ruins overlooking the ocean are spectacular. However, be warned: the ferry ride can be very rough. A lot of people were getting sick. When we got back to Cozumel, we just had enough time to grab a coffee and get back on. In Roatan, we did a glass-bottom boat tour. It was good for people who don't want to get wet but still want to see the reef. The guide was funny and told us a lot about the island's history with pirates. Costa Maya was just a 'relax' day for us. We stayed on the ship until about 1 PM then walked around the port pool area for an hour.

name: Kelly_Reviews
review: Avoid the 'beach break' in Costa Maya sold by the ship. It was a crowded bus to a crowded beach with mediocre food. Next time I'll just walk to the local town. Roatan was lovely though, we did the monkey park and it was worth every penny.

name: Greg and Tina
review: The ports were the main reason we picked this cruise. Roatan was rugged and green. We did an island tour and learned about the Garifuna culture, which was fascinating. The local dancing was a highlight. In Costa Maya, we rented a golf cart and drove through the local village. It was a bit dusty but gave us a better sense of the area than the port mall did. Cozumel was our snorkeling day. We went to Palancar Reef. The coral formations are huge! I've snorkeled all over the Caribbean and this was top-tier. My only complaint was the amount of trash I saw in the water near the cruise pier in Cozumel. It’s a shame people don't respect the ocean more. The timing for all ports was decent, though we could have used another hour in Roatan to see the East end of the island.

name: Sam_T_82
review: Cozumel jeep tour was okay. The guide was great but the jeeps were in pretty rough shape—the air conditioning didn't work in ours. Roatan beach was great but too many vendors asking to braid your hair every five minutes.

name: Pat_K
review: We did the dolphin swim in Cozumel. It was expensive but my daughter loved it. The facility at Chankanaab is nice and there's a lot to do there after the swim. We stayed for the sea lion show which was cute. Costa Maya was very hot. We tried to walk to the village but gave up halfway and took a shuttle. The shopping in Costa Maya was better than Roatan, where most things felt like cheap imports. In Roatan, we did the pirate ship excursion. It was a bit cheesy but the kids had a blast 'walking the plank.' I think if I did this itinerary again, I would spend more time exploring the natural side of Roatan rather than the tourist attractions. The island is so beautiful and green, it deserves more than a quick stop.

name: Yolanda Foster
review: Loved the salsa and salsa dance class in Cozumel! It was held at a nice resort and we learned how to make several types of salsa while drinking margaritas. Then they taught us some basic steps. It was high energy and a lot of fun. Roatan was much more laid back. We just went to a beach club and read books. The water was calm and perfect for a slow day. Costa Maya was our least favorite stop. It just felt like a giant shopping mall and lacked the authentic feel of the other two ports. We ended up coming back to the ship early because there wasn't much to do once we finished looking at the shops. Also, the pier in Costa Maya is incredibly long. If you have mobility issues, definitely wait for the shuttle.

name: D_Miller_90
review: Roatan was beautiful but honestly, the port is a mess. So many people trying to sell you tours as soon as you walk off. We did a snorkel tour that was booked ahead of time and it was great, but getting to the meeting point was stressful. Cozumel was much more organized.

name: Brenda_V
review: I was disappointed with the time allocation. We were in Cozumel for 12 hours but only 5 hours in Roatan. Roatan has so much more natural beauty to offer! We rushed through the monkey sanctuary and barely had time for a quick lunch before heading back. Cozumel is just a big city now. We went to a beach club called Nachi Cocom which was peaceful since they limit the guests, but I would have traded some of that Cozumel time for more Roatan exploration. Costa Maya was a surprise. I didn't expect much but the snorkeling near the reef there was quite good, and the water was very shallow so you could see everything clearly. Just be careful of the current near the breakwater. The port area in Costa Maya has a nice pool but the music was way too loud.

name: Gary Thompson
review: Had a great time in Cozumel doing the buggies. Muddy and fun. Roatan was better for the scenery. The hills and the ocean views are spectacular. Costa Maya was just okay, felt a bit like a manufactured destination for tourists.

name: Alicia Reed
review: We chose this cruise specifically for the Western Caribbean ports. Roatan is definitely the star of the show if you like nature. We did a botanical garden tour that was so peaceful and full of exotic flowers. The guide knew every plant and bird. Cozumel is better for those who want a mix of history and fun. We went to the San Gervasio ruins which are small but interesting, then spent the afternoon at a beach bar. Costa Maya is purely for relaxation. If you don't book an excursion to the ruins, you're pretty much stuck at the beach or the port pool. We chose the beach in Mahahual and it was a nice, cheap day. The locals were friendly and we didn't feel as pressured to buy things as we did in Roatan. Good balance of ports overall.

name: Jonah_Travels
review: Cozumel is too busy. Five ships in port meant the downtown was packed. Roatan was better, very lush. Costa Maya was my favorite because we went to a lagoon excursion that was very quiet. It was nice to get away from the cruise crowds for a few hours.

name: Jordan Alvarez
review: Roatan ended up being the standout stop for us. We booked a small snorkeling excursion and the water clarity was honestly better than I expected. The guide kept the group moving so it never felt too crowded in one spot. Costa Maya was more of a mixed experience. We walked out past the port shops and grabbed a taxi to a beach, which was fine but nothing memorable. Cozumel felt the busiest by far. We did a beach club there, and while it was nice, it was definitely packed with cruise passengers. Timing wise, Roatan felt just right, Costa Maya dragged a bit, and Cozumel felt slightly rushed trying to get everything in.

name: Simone Carter
review: Costa Maya just didn’t click for me. We stayed near the port area and it felt very manufactured, like everything was designed for cruise traffic. In Roatan we did a guided island tour that included a wildlife stop and some scenic viewpoints, which I enjoyed a lot more. Cozumel was easy to navigate on our own, we just took a cab downtown and wandered around for a few hours.

name: Ravi Patel
review: Cozumel was my favorite stop because we booked a catamaran snorkeling trip. The reef was nice, but there were a lot of other boats around which took away from the experience a bit. Roatan felt more relaxed overall. We did a beach break and didn’t feel rushed. Costa Maya was the only port where I wished we had planned better. We tried to explore on our own but ended up spending most of the time walking through shops and heading back early.

name: Lacey W.
review: Roatan ziplining was easily the highlight of the cruise for me. It was fast-paced but really fun. Costa Maya we did the ruins, which were interesting but the bus ride felt long for the amount of time actually there. Cozumel we just walked around near the port and did some shopping.

name: Mateo Gutierrez
review: We tried to mix excursions with free exploration. In Roatan, we booked a combo tour that included snorkeling and a short visit to a local community. That gave a better sense of the island beyond just the beach. Costa Maya was more structured since we went to the ruins, which I enjoyed, but the schedule was tight and started early. Cozumel was our unplanned day. We grabbed a taxi to a beach club and just relaxed, which ended up being one of the most enjoyable parts of the trip. Each port required a different approach, and I think planning ahead mattered more in Costa Maya than the others.

name: Kendra J
review: We rented a golf cart in Costa Maya and drove along the coast, stopping at a random beach spot. That flexibility made the day feel more personal. Roatan was more organized since we did an excursion with snorkeling and a short tour. Cozumel felt crowded but still fun for walking around and people watching.

name: Noah Bernstein
review: I’ve done a similar itinerary before and this one felt pretty typical. Roatan stood out again because we hired a private driver who took us to less crowded areas, including a quiet beach and a small local restaurant. Costa Maya felt very geared toward tourists. Even outside the port, it didn’t feel like we were seeing much beyond what was set up for visitors. Cozumel had the most options, but also the most congestion. We did a snorkeling trip there and it felt a bit like a production line. Overall, Roatan felt the most authentic, while the other two required more effort to find something that didn’t feel overly commercial.

name: Emily Zhao
review: Cozumel was our easiest day. We didn’t book anything and just explored at our own pace. Roatan we did a snorkeling excursion which was nice but a little rushed between stops. Costa Maya we stayed on the ship because none of the excursions appealed to us.

name: Fatima El-Sayed
review: Roatan was the most interesting culturally for me. Our guide talked a lot about the island and pointed out places we wouldn’t have noticed otherwise. Costa Maya felt more like the excursion itself was the focus rather than the destination. We visited ruins, which were impressive, but the long travel time made it tiring. Cozumel was very lively but also crowded. We found a quieter beach club after some searching, which made the day more enjoyable. I think how much you enjoy each port depends on how much planning you put in ahead of time.

name: Chris D.
review: Costa Maya ruins were worth seeing but the bus ride was long and early. Roatan snorkeling was better than expected. Cozumel had good shopping but felt very crowded with multiple ships in port.

name: Olivia Martin
review: The pacing between ports was interesting. In Roatan, our excursion had a nice balance between activities and downtime, so we never felt rushed. Costa Maya felt more rigid. The ruins tour was informative, but everything was scheduled tightly and we didn’t have much flexibility. Cozumel gave us the most freedom since we planned our own day, but it was also the busiest stop. It took some effort to find a quieter area away from the crowds. I appreciated that each port offered something different, but I definitely preferred the more relaxed structure we had in Roatan compared to the more rigid experience in Costa Maya.

name: Andre Lopes
review: Roatan was my favorite stop by far. The water was clear and the snorkeling was great. Costa Maya felt like a tourist zone more than anything else. Cozumel was fun but very commercial with lots of shops and bars.

name: Svetlana Ivanova
review: We approached each port differently and that made a big difference. In Cozumel, we booked a smaller beach club that limited capacity, which helped avoid the crowds. Roatan was more of a guided experience for us, with a mix of sightseeing and water activities. Costa Maya was the most difficult to navigate independently. Transportation outside the port wasn’t as straightforward, and I wish we had arranged something in advance. The ruins were interesting, but the time spent traveling there reduced the overall enjoyment. I would say Roatan felt the most balanced, Cozumel the most convenient, and Costa Maya required the most effort to enjoy.

name: Dylan & Brooke
review: We did a snorkeling and beach combo in Cozumel which was fun but crowded. Roatan felt less busy and more relaxed overall. Costa Maya didn’t stand out much to us, we just walked around and went back early.

name: Marcus Thompson
review: Roatan was where we felt like we experienced something different. Our excursion included multiple stops, including a wildlife visit and snorkeling, and it didn’t feel rushed. Costa Maya was more about the specific activity—we went to a beach club, which was nice but fairly standard. Cozumel was easy to explore and had plenty of options, but it also felt the most crowded. I think the itinerary works well if you balance structured excursions with independent time, because each port offers a different kind of experience.

name: Anita S.
review: Costa Maya cooking class was surprisingly fun and interactive. Roatan excursion felt a bit rushed between stops. Cozumel we explored on our own and liked that better than the organized tours.

name: Harold Greene
review: I stayed on the ship in Cozumel since I’ve been there before. Roatan we did a fishing excursion which was interesting but long. Costa Maya we stayed near the port and didn’t venture too far out.

name: Mei Lin Chen
review: This itinerary offered variety, but the experience depended heavily on planning. In Roatan, we chose a smaller group excursion and it felt more personal and less rushed. Costa Maya was more challenging. The ruins were impressive, but the long bus ride made the day feel dominated by transportation. Cozumel had the most options, but also the most people. We decided to skip excursions there and create our own plan, which worked well but required some research beforehand. I noticed that crowd levels really impacted enjoyment, especially in Cozumel where several ships were docked at once. Overall, Roatan felt the most unique, while the other ports required more effort to find something that suited us.

name: Jamal Rivers
review: Roatan ziplining was fun but over pretty quickly. Costa Maya beach day was relaxing but nothing special. Cozumel snorkeling was decent but crowded with other groups.

name: Isabella DeLuca
review: I tend to prefer cultural and historical experiences, so I had mixed feelings about this itinerary. Roatan surprised me because we booked a small group tour that included a visit to a local area and a short nature walk, which felt more immersive than expected. Costa Maya was probably the most educational stop because of the ruins, but the logistics made it tiring. The early departure and long drive took away from the overall experience, even though the site itself was impressive. Cozumel was the most flexible port, and we chose to explore on our own instead of booking anything. That worked well, but it did require some planning ahead to avoid the most crowded areas. I think this itinerary works best if you’re intentional about what you book at each stop.

name: Marcus Chen
review: Cozumel was the absolute highlight for our group. We bypassed the typical ship excursions and booked a private Jeep tour that took us to the 'wild side' of the island. There’s no electricity on that side, just these incredible rugged beaches and small bars like Coconuts. It felt like a completely different world compared to the crowded pier. Roatan was also great; we did the zip-lining through the jungle. The views from the top were breathtaking, though the bus ride to the site was a bit bumpy and the local traffic is definitely a concern if you're on a tight schedule. We skipped Costa Maya entirely and stayed on the ship to enjoy the quiet decks. From what we heard from others, the port area there is just a big shopping mall anyway, so I don't feel like we missed much. Overall, the time in Cozumel felt perfect, but I wish we had an extra hour in Roatan to see the west end beaches properly.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown for me. The port area feels very manufactured and touristy. We took a taxi to Mahahual, which was better, but the vendors there are extremely pushy, making it hard to just relax on the beach. Cozumel was much better organized. We did the underwater oxygen helmets and it was a surreal experience seeing the fish so close without needing a SCUBA certification. Roatan was beautiful but we had a major delay getting off the ship due to local port authorities, which cut our excursion time short by nearly an hour. We still managed to see the monkeys and sloths, but everything felt rushed. I'd recommend this itinerary for the scenery, but be prepared for some logistical headaches at the piers.

name: Aisha_Travels88
review: Had a fantastic day at Little French Key in Roatan! It’s a private island paradise. The water was turquoise and the horseback riding in the ocean was a bucket list moment. Cozumel was just okay; we just walked around the town and did some light shopping. Everything is priced in USD and quite expensive. Costa Maya was actually my favorite for history; the Chacchoben ruins are a must-see if you don't mind a long bus ride.

name: D_O_Sullivan
review: In Cozumel, we did the bar hop tour to the east side. It was a great way to see the undeveloped parts of the island away from the tourist traps. Our guide was local and had so many stories about the island's history. We didn't do much in Costa Maya, just grabbed a couple of drinks at the port pool. It's a nice facility but very expensive compared to local spots. Roatan was our snorkeling day. The reef there is in much better shape than the ones I've seen in the Virgin Islands. We saw a sea turtle and several stingrays. My only gripe with the itinerary is the early departure from Roatan. 3:00 PM is just too early to get back on the ship when there is so much to see on the island. You really only get half a day there which is a shame.

name: Sarah Jenkins
review: First time cruiser here. Roatan was stunningly green. We did the sloth and monkey hang-out and it was the best part of the whole trip. The animals seem well cared for and the guides were very helpful with photos. In Costa Maya, we did the lost Mayan kingdom water park. It was fun for the kids, but honestly, it’s something you could do anywhere. I regret not seeing the actual ruins instead. Cozumel was extremely crowded with four other ships in port. We tried to go to a public beach but it was packed, so we just headed back to the ship early. Next time I'll book a private beach club in advance for Cozumel.

name: Kwame_O
review: Cozumel snorkeling at Palancar Reef was top tier. The visibility was incredible. Roatan was a bit chaotic at the pier but the island tour was worth it for the views alone. Skip the shops in Costa Maya, total tourist trap.

name: Linda_and_Bill
review: We’ve done this itinerary before, so we were looking for something different. In Costa Maya, we took a local bus to the Bacalar Lagoon. It is a long trek, nearly 90 minutes each way, but the 'Lake of Seven Colors' is absolutely real. Swimming in that fresh water was so refreshing compared to the salty ocean. We almost missed the ship though because our bus got stuck behind some construction on the way back! Very stressful. In Cozumel, we just went to a local bakery and sat in the central plaza watching the people. Roatan we stayed on board because of the rain. It’s a decent itinerary but the transit times to get to the good stuff in Costa Maya are a bit of a hurdle.

name: Javeria Khan
review: Roatan was lovely. We did a glass-bottom boat because my mother isn't a strong swimmer but wanted to see the reef. It was perfect for her. Costa Maya was just a shopping day for us. Found some nice silver, but you have to haggle hard! Cozumel was very busy.

name: Grandpa_Joe
review: The pier in Costa Maya is way too long for seniors. They have a trolley but it was broken when we arrived. My wife struggled with the heat. We enjoyed the catamaran in Cozumel, though the music was a bit loud for our taste. Roatan was the most scenic port by far.

name: Tyler M.
review: I'm a solo traveler and I found Roatan very easy to navigate. I just walked off and found a local driver who took me to West End for $20. Spent the day at a small cafe on the water and did some great shore snorkeling. Cozumel was a bit much for me alone—everyone is trying to pull you into their shop or bar. I ended up going back to the ship after an hour. Costa Maya was actually quite chill if you stay at the very end of the beach away from the cruise pier. The food at the local stalls in Mahahual was much better and cheaper than the port food. I think the time in each port was adequate, though Cozumel feels like it needs a full 12 hours if you want to do the mainland excursions to Tulum.

name: Priya_S
review: Cozumel was great for the kids. We did the chocolate making class and they loved grinding the beans. Roatan was a bit scary with the driving, the roads are very narrow and the drivers are aggressive. We did the beach day at Tabyana and the sand was like powder. Costa Maya was our 'rest' day. We just sat by the port pool which is actually very nice and free to use. The only thing is the drinks there are ship prices, not Mexico prices. It was a good mix of ports, though I wish there was more of a cultural focus in the ship-sponsored tours rather than just beach clubs.

name: R_M_Gardner
review: Roatan was amazing. We went to the iguana farm and saw hundreds of them. Very unique experience. Cozumel we did the tequila tasting—informative but obviously a sales pitch. Costa Maya was a bit boring if you don't do an excursion.

name: Jackson Family
review: We had a mixed experience. Roatan was beautiful but we had a bad experience with a local vendor who overcharged us for a simple taxi ride to the beach. Make sure you negotiate before you get in! In Cozumel, we did the Amazing Race excursion and it was a blast. It’s a great way to see the downtown area while having some fun competition. Costa Maya was our favorite because of the ruins. Chacchoben is stunning and our guide was a descendant of the Mayans, so the history felt very personal. The port time in Costa Maya was a bit tight though; we felt we had to run back to the ship as soon as the tour ended. I’d love more time to explore the town after the ruins.

name: Kimiko_Tanaka
review: Cozumel has the best shopping. Found some beautiful hand-painted ceramics. Roatan was all about the nature; the birds and monkeys were so colorful. Costa Maya was okay, but the walk from the ship to the exit is very long and hot. Take water with you!

name: Samuel L. Brooks
review: The snorkeling in Roatan is some of the best I have ever done. The wall drop-off is literally right there. We saw eagle rays and so many colorful fish. Cozumel was disappointing because of the sheer number of people. It felt like a theme park rather than a destination. Costa Maya was a nice surprise; we did a paddleboarding tour through the mangroves and it was very peaceful. The guides were great and really cared about the environment. I think the itinerary is well-balanced between adventure and relaxation. My only advice is to get off the ship as early as possible in Roatan to avoid the crowds at the animal sanctuaries.

name: V_Gomez
review: Costa Maya was beautiful. We did a beach club called Maya Chan. It’s a bit of a bumpy ride to get there but the service is incredible. It felt very exclusive. Roatan was a bit too 'busy' for us at the pier. We ended up just doing the chairlift to the beach which was okay but nothing special. Cozumel we stayed on the ship because we've been there so many times before. The port area in Cozumel hasn't changed much in years. This trip made me realize how much I prefer the smaller, less commercialized stops like Roatan used to be.

name: Chris_B_1975
review: Loved the ATV tour in Cozumel. We got really muddy and explored some hidden caves. Roatan was better for photos—the scenery is just incredible. Costa Maya was okay, but a bit too much of a 'mall' feel for my taste. I like to see the real country.

name: Nadia_E
review: In Roatan, we visited a local orphanage as part of a 'giving back' excursion. It was a very moving experience and much better than just sitting on a beach. In Cozumel, we took the ferry over to Playa del Carmen. It’s a long day and the ferry can be very rough, so watch out if you get seasick. The shopping on 5th Avenue was great though. Costa Maya was our last stop and we were pretty tired, so we just did the bird sanctuary in the port. It’s small but well-maintained. I think the itinerary gives a good taste of the Western Caribbean, but the port times could be shifted to allow for more time on mainland Mexico from Cozumel.

name: Benny_Cruises
review: Roatan snorkeling = A+. Cozumel shopping = B. Costa Maya port area = C-. The fake Mayan dancers at the port in Costa Maya felt a bit exploitative and weird. The ruins were cool though.

name: Jordan_and_Casey
review: This was a great trip for divers. Roatan has amazing reefs. We did a two-tank dive and the coral was very healthy. In Cozumel, we did the drift dive which is always an experience—you just float along with the current and see everything go by. Costa Maya was our only 'dry' day. We took a golf cart rental and explored the coastline. It was fun to drive around on our own without a guide. The only issue was the map they gave us wasn't very accurate and we got a bit lost in the residential areas. The locals were very helpful in pointing us back to the ship though. It’s a solid itinerary for people who love the water.

name: Carlos Mendez
review: Roatan ended up being our favorite stop. We booked a small-group tour that included a sloth sanctuary and a short beach break, and it felt much less rushed than I expected. The drive itself was interesting, seeing neighborhoods and daily life outside the cruise bubble. Costa Maya was the opposite experience for us. We tried to explore on foot beyond the port area but quickly realized it’s pretty limited unless you take a taxi somewhere farther out. Cozumel was more flexible—we just grabbed a cab downtown, walked around, and had plenty of time to get back. Overall, the itinerary works, but I wish Costa Maya had more accessible options close to the ship.

name: Tanya L.
review: Cozumel was easy and fun to navigate on our own. We skipped excursions and just found a beach club after a short taxi ride, which gave us a relaxed day without a tight schedule. Roatan felt more structured because we booked an excursion, but it was worth it for the snorkeling. Costa Maya was a bit confusing logistically—lots of vendors right when you get off, and it wasn’t super clear how to get beyond that area. Not bad, just less intuitive.

name: Ayesha Rahman
review: I’m usually more into cultural stops than beaches, so Costa Maya surprised me in a good way. We did a ruins excursion, and although the bus ride was long and early, the site itself was impressive and not overly crowded. Roatan felt more nature-focused; we did a guided walk through a botanical area and learned about local plants, which I didn’t expect to enjoy as much as I did. Cozumel was more commercial, but we still found a quiet spot away from the main shopping streets. Timing-wise, Roatan felt a bit rushed, but the other two ports gave us enough breathing room.

name: Jake_and_Lina
review: We’ve cruised before, and this itinerary is pretty standard, but still enjoyable. Roatan had the best excursion options in our opinion—we did a combo tour with wildlife and snorkeling. Costa Maya felt the most manufactured, like everything was designed for cruise passengers, which isn’t always a bad thing but didn’t feel very authentic. Cozumel was a nice middle ground. You can do excursions or just wander. We chose to rent scooters for a few hours and explore on our own, which made it memorable.

name: Gregory P.
review: Roatan was incredible for snorkeling. The water clarity was better than I expected, and the reef was close enough that we didn’t spend all day on a boat. Costa Maya didn’t click for me. We stayed near the port, and it felt crowded and a bit overwhelming with people trying to sell things. Cozumel was easier—we just walked around, grabbed a drink, and headed back early. If I did it again, I’d definitely book an excursion for Costa Maya instead of winging it.

name: Mina K.
review: As a solo traveler, I was mostly concerned about how easy it would be to get around at each stop. Cozumel was by far the simplest—I felt comfortable walking around and finding things to do without a plan. Roatan required a bit more planning, so I joined a group excursion to avoid dealing with transportation. Costa Maya was the only place I felt slightly unsure about where to go after leaving the port area. I ended up just staying within the complex, which was fine but not very memorable.

name: Devon Brooks
review: We traveled with our teenage kids, and Roatan was the highlight because of the animal encounters. They’re still talking about holding a sloth. Costa Maya was more educational since we did a ruins tour, but the long bus ride tested everyone’s patience. Cozumel was the easiest day—we let the kids pick what to do, and they chose snorkeling near shore. The schedule overall felt balanced, but I would have preferred a little more time in Roatan.

name: Elena V
review: Costa Maya actually ended up being my favorite, which surprised me. We booked a small cooking-style experience where we made traditional food and spent time in a quiet setting away from the port crowds. It felt more personal than the larger excursions. Roatan was beautiful but busy, especially at the beach areas we visited. Cozumel was fine, but very tourist-heavy in the main areas. Still enjoyable, just less unique compared to Costa Maya.

name: Marcus J.
review: Roatan felt the most authentic to me. The drive through the island gave a glimpse of local life, not just tourist zones. Costa Maya was more controlled—everything seemed centered around the port unless you went far out. We didn’t want a long bus ride, so we stayed close and probably missed the best parts. Cozumel was easy to customize. We didn’t book anything and still had a full day just walking, eating, and relaxing.

name: Sophie Nguyen
review: We’re not big on excursions, so we judged the ports based on what you can do independently. Cozumel wins for that. It’s very easy to get around and find things at your own pace. Roatan was trickier without a plan, so we ended up joining a last-minute tour. Costa Maya felt the most limited unless you committed to an excursion ahead of time. If you like structure, it’s fine, but spontaneous travelers might find it frustrating.

name: Harold T.
review: Cozumel was our best day. We took a taxi to a quieter beach area and spent hours swimming and relaxing without feeling rushed. Roatan had great scenery, but our excursion felt a bit packed schedule-wise. Costa Maya was the only port where we decided to stay on the ship after getting off briefly—it just didn’t appeal to us compared to the other stops.

name: Priya Desai
review: Roatan had a really nice mix of nature and activity. We did a guided snorkeling trip and still had time to relax afterward. Costa Maya felt more like a stop where you need a plan, otherwise you’re stuck in the port area. Cozumel was easygoing and flexible, which I appreciated.

name: Ben R.
review: We skipped Roatan excursions and just found a nearby beach, which was fine but nothing special. Costa Maya was better because we actually booked something—a ruins tour that gave the day more purpose. Cozumel was the easiest to enjoy without planning ahead.

name: Luz Herrera
review: Costa Maya surprised me in a good way. We went beyond the port to a quieter beach and had a really relaxing day. Roatan was more active with tours and movement. Cozumel felt busy but convenient.

name: Omar Siddiqui
review: Roatan was the highlight for me because of the scenery and water activities. Costa Maya felt a bit underwhelming unless you go far out. Cozumel was fun but crowded.

name: Emily Carter
review: Cozumel was super easy to explore on foot and by taxi. Roatan needed more planning. Costa Maya was okay but not my favorite stop.

name: Jordan K
review: We really liked Roatan’s snorkeling and wildlife stops. Costa Maya felt more like a shopping stop unless you booked something. Cozumel was a nice balance.

name: Nina Petrova
review: Costa Maya was relaxing for us since we found a quiet beach away from the port. Roatan was more structured. Cozumel had a lot going on but felt crowded.

name: Derrick W.
review: Roatan was beautiful but a little rushed. Costa Maya didn’t offer much close to the port. Cozumel was easy and enjoyable without much effort.

name: Isabella Rossi
review:
This itinerary gave a really clear contrast between different types of cruise ports, which I actually appreciated more than I expected. Roatan felt like the most immersive stop. We booked a longer excursion that included a drive across different parts of the island, and it gave us a better sense of the place beyond just beaches. It did feel slightly rushed at the end, though, as we had to keep an eye on the return time.

Costa Maya was the most challenging for us. We didn’t book an excursion ahead of time, thinking we’d explore independently, but the area right outside the ship is very controlled and crowded. We ended up taking a taxi to a beach club, which improved the experience a lot, but it required more effort than the other stops.

Cozumel was the easiest and most flexible day. You can plan something structured or just wander, and either way it works. We chose to keep it simple and still had a full, enjoyable day. Overall, the ports vary a lot, which keeps the trip interesting.

name: Marcus Thompson
review: Roatan was the absolute highlight for us. We booked a private driver who took us away from the main port chaos. We visited a local orphanage which was a humbling experience, then spent the afternoon at West Bay Beach. The water was crystal clear, though the vendors selling jewelry and massages every five minutes got a bit tiring after a while. In Costa Maya, we didn't have anything planned and just walked around the port area. Big mistake. It felt like a giant outdoor mall designed just to take your money. Everything was overpriced and didn't feel authentic at all. If I go back, I’ll definitely book a tour to the ruins or something further out to avoid the tourist trap. Cozumel was much better for wandering. We found a small taco shop about four blocks away from the main drag that had the best al pastor I've ever tasted. We spent maybe $20 total and had a much better time than the people we saw paying for the crowded beach club buffets. The time in Cozumel felt sufficient, but I wish we had more hours in Roatan to see the east side of the island.

name: Sarah J.
review: Did the Chacchoben ruins in Costa Maya. The bus ride was nearly an hour each way but the guide was so knowledgeable about Mayan history that the time flew by. Seeing the pyramids poking through the jungle canopy was breathtaking. Just be prepared for the humidity! We also did a catamaran snorkel in Cozumel. The reef was okay, but I've seen better in other parts of the Caribbean. The crew made the trip fun with music and drinks on the way back, though. Roatan felt a bit rushed; our ship cleared customs late so we only had about four hours on the island, which wasn't enough to really see anything beyond the immediate port area and a quick lunch. I'd recommend prioritizing the ruins if you haven't seen them.

name: BeachLover88
review: Cozumel was fantastic! We spent the day at Mr. Sancho's. It was a bit crowded because there were four ships in port, but the service was fast and the pool was refreshing. My kids loved the inflatable water park. In Roatan, we did the monkey and sloth hangout. My daughter is still talking about holding the sloth. It was a short excursion, which was nice because we had time to walk through the shops at the pier afterward. Costa Maya was our 'stay on the ship' day. We'd been there before and find the port area too artificial, and we weren't up for a long bus ride to the ruins this time around. The kids enjoyed having the ship slides to themselves.

name: David Kapur
review: Our stop in Roatan was incredible for diving. The reef is so close to shore and the visibility was at least 80 feet. We saw a sea turtle and several rays. The local dive shop we used was much cheaper than the ship's excursion. In Costa Maya, we rented a golf cart and drove down to Mahahual. It's a bumpy ride but worth it to get away from the cruise pier. We found a quiet spot on the beach with cheap buckets of beer. Cozumel felt way too commercialized for me. The 'downtown' area is just one jewelry store after another. We ended up just grabbing some margaritas and heading back to the ship early because the constant sales pitches were getting on my nerves. It's a shame because the water is beautiful.

name: Linda G.
review: The tour of the San Gervasio ruins in Cozumel was a bit underwhelming. There isn't much left of the structures compared to the ones on the mainland, and it was scorching hot with very little shade. I wish we had just gone to the beach. However, our day in Roatan at Little French Key was like paradise. It was so peaceful and the lunch was delicious. Costa Maya was just okay; the port is nice for a walk, but it's very 'Disney-fied'. We did a tequila tasting there which was fun, but mostly just an excuse to buy expensive bottles. I think next time I'll look for a more cultural experience in Mexico rather than the tourist traps. Overall, the itinerary was good but felt a little repetitive with the Mexican ports.

name: CruisingKevin
review: Roatan’s infrastructure is a bit rough. The traffic from the port to West End was terrible, and we almost missed our excursion start time. Once we got to the beach, it was nice, but the stress of the commute dampened the mood. Cozumel was much more organized. We took a taxi to the Money Bar and did some shore snorkeling right off the rocks. Tons of fish! Highly recommend for a cheap day. Costa Maya was a letdown. We booked a water park excursion through the ship, but it felt a bit run down for the price they were charging. It's okay for kids, but adults might find it boring after an hour. Watch out for the long walk back to the ship in Costa Maya, it's brutal in the sun.

name: Aisha_Travels
review: I loved the cooking class in Costa Maya! Learning to make authentic salsa and tortillas from scratch was such a highlight. The hosts were so welcoming and the small group size made it feel personal. Roatan was also great; we did a glass-bottom boat tour because I don't like getting in the water, and the coral formations were stunning. Cozumel was just for shopping. I found some beautiful silver earrings, but you have to be prepared to haggle. It's not for everyone, but I enjoy the back-and-forth. Overall, a solid itinerary, though I felt we stayed in Cozumel way too late and didn't have enough time in Roatan to explore the local culture.

name: Robert Miller
review: If you go to Cozumel, do the Jeep tour to the east side of the island. It’s completely undeveloped compared to the port side—no electricity, just wild beaches and crashing waves. It was the most beautiful part of the whole trip. Roatan was fine, we just did a beach day at Tabyana. The sand is white and soft, but it was very crowded with other cruise passengers. Costa Maya was my least favorite. The walk from the ship to the exit of the port is incredibly long and they force you to walk through a maze of shops. It felt like they were holding us hostage just to make us look at souvenirs before we could actually leave. I'd skip the port area entirely next time and head straight to a taxi.

name: Elena_R
review: Costa Maya snorkeling was a bit disappointing due to the seagrass, but the beach club staff worked hard to clear it. In Cozumel, we did the chocolate and tequila tour which was informative and tasty. Roatan was definitely the winner for natural beauty. We just took a taxi to the West End and walked around. It has a very laid-back, backpacker vibe that I loved. The local food there was much better than the buffet options at the organized beach clubs. I felt like the 8 hours we had in each port was generally enough, though Cozumel gets a bit repetitive if you aren't doing a specific activity like diving or visiting ruins.

name: Greg P.
review: Roatan ziplining was a blast! We did the extreme course and the views of the ocean from the top of the mountain were incredible. The guides were very safety-conscious which I appreciated. In Cozumel, we did a helmet dive. It was cool to walk on the ocean floor, but it felt very controlled and a bit artificial. Costa Maya was where we did the Chacchoben ruins. It was a long day and quite exhausting, but the history is fascinating. Our guide was a descendant of the Mayans and had great stories. Just a warning: the bathrooms at the ruins are... basic. Use the ones on the bus if you can! The schedule was tight but we made it back with 30 minutes to spare.

name: WanderlustWendy
review: Costa Maya was beautiful. We went to Maya Chan and it was the most relaxing day of my life. They only take a few people, so it was quiet, and the food was amazing. In Cozumel, we did the Amazing Race excursion. It was a fun way to see the town and interact with locals, though we were running around so much we didn't really get to 'see' the sights. Roatan was a bit disappointing. We went to a beach club that was recommended, but it was so noisy with loud music that we couldn't relax. I wish we had done something more nature-focused there instead. It felt like a missed opportunity.

name: Jim_and_Pam
review: We really enjoyed the chocolate factory in Roatan. It’s a small operation but you get to taste so many different varieties. We bought way too much to bring home! Cozumel was our day for a private boat charter. It was expensive but having the snorkel spots to ourselves before the big excursion boats arrived was worth every penny. Costa Maya was the only 'meh' day. We just stayed in the port pool. It’s a nice pool, but it’s loud and the drinks are pricey. It felt like being at a resort in Vegas rather than Mexico. If we go back, we will definitely try to get out to the Bacalar lagoon instead of staying local. The boat timing for the charter in Cozumel was perfect.

name: Samira Khan
review: The history in this itinerary is great. In Costa Maya, the ruins were a must-see. In Cozumel, we took the ferry over to Playa del Carmen to see Tulum. It's a very long day and the ferry ride can be rough if you get seasick, but seeing the ruins on the cliff over the ocean is a core memory now. Roatan was more about the scenery for us. We just took a tour around the island in an air-conditioned van. The island is much more mountainous than I expected. The local village of Coxen Hole was very colorful and interesting to drive through. I wish we had more time to stop and talk to people there, but the ship schedule was a bit restrictive for deep exploration.

name: Tom_H
review: Cozumel snorkeling at Palancar Reef was top-notch. The coral heads are huge! Roatan was okay, but we had some issues with our tour operator being late to pick us up, which made me nervous about getting back to the ship on time. We ended up cutting the beach time short just to be safe. Costa Maya was our favorite shopping stop. We found some really cool hand-painted pottery just outside the main port gate. It was much cheaper than the stuff inside the terminal. The heat in Costa Maya was intense though, definitely stay hydrated. I think the port times were generally okay, but the logistics in Roatan need work for independent travelers.

name: Karen S.
review: Did the dolphin swim in Cozumel. It was okay, but felt very scripted and hurried. I felt bad for the animals after a while. In Roatan, we just walked to the beach right by the pier. It’s not the best beach on the island, but it was free and easy. Costa Maya was surprisingly fun; we did a salsa dancing and salsa making class. I have two left feet but the instructors were so patient. It was a great way to spend a few hours. The port itself is a bit of a maze, but we found a nice spot for a quick drink before heading back on board. Not a bad trip, but I preferred the Mexican stops over Honduras.

name: Chris_B_82
review: Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown. We took a taxi to Mahahual and the beach was covered in seagrass. Not the beautiful blue water we saw in the pictures. We ended up just having lunch and heading back. Cozumel saved the trip—we went to Buccanos and the food was five-star quality. The snorkeling right there was also surprisingly good for a beach club. Roatan was beautiful from the ship, but we didn't get off. We were just tired and wanted a quiet day by the pool. From what others said, the sloths were the big hit, so maybe next time. I think this itinerary is best if you have specific excursions booked.

name: Maria_G1990
review: Cozumel bar hop tour on the east side was a blast. You get to see the rugged side of the island and try different drinks at these little 'off the grid' bars. Roatan was all about the water for us. We did a kayak tour through the mangroves which was very peaceful and different from the usual beach excursions. Costa Maya was just a quick walk through the shops. I bought some vanilla extract and some hot sauce. The prices at the port are definitely higher than what you'd find in a normal town, but it's convenient I guess. I wish the ship stayed in Costa Maya longer so we could have seen the night life in Mahahual.

name: The_Smiths
review: Roatan was great for the kids. The monkey park was a huge success. The monkeys actually jump on your shoulders! Cozumel was a bit too busy for us. With so many ships, the sidewalk was a wall of people. We found a small museum that was quiet and had some interesting artifacts, but otherwise, it was a bit overwhelming. Costa Maya was the most relaxing. We stayed at the port pool and the kids played for hours. It’s not 'authentic' Mexico, but after a long week of travel, sometimes you just need an easy day with a pool and a snack bar nearby. We didn't feel rushed at any of the ports, which was a nice change from our last cruise.

name: Gary_V
review: Costa Maya ruins tour was spectacular. It’s a further drive than some other options, but there were hardly any other people there. The carvings into the temple were incredible. Roatan felt very commercialized near the pier, but once we got out to the West End, it felt like a different world. Very quiet and local. Cozumel is just... Cozumel. It’s the same every time. Good for a cheap massage and some margaritas, but don't expect a deep cultural experience unless you really go looking for it away from the tourist zones. The transportation for the ruins tour was efficient and well-timed.

name: Anita_R
review: We did the clear kayak excursion in Cozumel. It was cool to see the fish beneath you without getting wet! In Roatan, we spent the day at a resort called Maya Key. It was a short boat ride from the pier and had a nice little animal rescue and a replica of some ruins. It was a good 'all-in-one' stop for families. Costa Maya was our last stop. We were pretty tired by then, so we just did a little shopping and went back to the ship. The port area is very clean and well-maintained, which I appreciated. Overall, a great mix of activities across the three islands. The time allocation for each stop felt just right for a first-timer.

name: Daniel Okafor
review: Roatan was easily the standout stop for me. I booked a small snorkeling excursion and the water clarity was unreal, like you could see everything without even diving deep. The only downside was how tight the schedule felt toward the end, we were watching the clock a bit. Costa Maya was more confusing. Once you get off the ship, it feels like you're funneled into a shopping area, and we didn’t have a clear plan so we just wandered. In hindsight, definitely should have booked something. Cozumel was simple and flexible. We just grabbed a taxi and spent time near the water, no pressure, no rush.

name: Heather J.
review: We did a ruins excursion in Costa Maya and honestly that was the most memorable part of the trip. The bus ride was long and early, which I didn’t love, but once we got there it felt worth it. Roatan was more of a beach and wildlife day for us, which was fun but a little more touristy. Cozumel was the easiest day, we didn’t even book anything and still had a full day just walking around and finding places to sit by the water.

name: Luis_G_87
review: Cozumel felt very easy to explore on your own. I liked that we didn’t need an excursion to enjoy it. Roatan was more structured, we did a tour that included animals and a beach stop, and it was nice but felt a bit rushed. Costa Maya was the only stop where I felt like we didn’t really get much out of it. We stayed close to the port and it just felt like a big tourist zone.

name: Megan Liu
review: As a first-time cruiser, I didn’t realize how different each port would feel. Roatan was very nature-focused, we did a guided eco-style tour and learned a lot. Costa Maya felt more commercial unless you go further out, which we didn’t. Cozumel had a nice balance—you could explore, shop, or just relax without needing much planning.

name: Travis Cole
review: Roatan had the best excursion options in my opinion. We did snorkeling and a short island tour, which made the day feel full without being overwhelming. Costa Maya was not my favorite. We tried to explore without booking anything and ended up just staying near the port, which got old fast. Cozumel was easygoing, we rented a scooter and just drove around for a few hours.

name: Anika Patel
review: I traveled with my parents and we were looking for something low-key. Cozumel worked best for that—we took a short taxi ride and spent the day relaxing by the water. Roatan was a bit more active since we joined a group excursion. Costa Maya felt like it required more effort to enjoy fully, and we didn’t quite figure that out until it was too late.

name: Bryce_W
review: Costa Maya was the most disappointing stop for me. We didn’t book anything and the port area felt crowded and repetitive. Roatan made up for it though—we did a combination of snorkeling and a wildlife visit, and that was probably the highlight of the trip. Cozumel was somewhere in the middle, easy to navigate but definitely busy.

name: Fatima Zahra
review: Roatan felt the most unique out of the three ports. We did a small group tour that included a cultural stop and a beach break, and it didn’t feel overly commercial. Costa Maya was a bit overwhelming when you first get off the ship, lots of vendors and noise. Cozumel was more relaxed and easy to figure out.

name: Connor D.
review: Cozumel was my favorite just because of how simple it was. You can get off the ship and figure things out as you go. Roatan was great too but required more planning. Costa Maya felt like you really needed an excursion to make the most of it.

name: Jasmine Rivera
review: We traveled as a group of friends and each port felt very different. In Roatan, we booked a longer excursion that combined snorkeling with a stop at a small animal sanctuary. It was fun, but the timing was tight and we didn’t get much free time. Costa Maya was more frustrating because we didn’t plan ahead and ended up stuck in the port area longer than we wanted. It felt very commercial and crowded. Cozumel was the easiest day by far. We split up, some of us went snorkeling, others just walked around and found a place to relax. It was nice not having a strict schedule. Overall, the itinerary is good but definitely rewards planning ahead, especially for Costa Maya.

name: Ethan Miller
