review: Roatan had some of the best views on the whole trip. We didn’t do a formal excursion, just hired a local driver, and it worked out really well. Costa Maya was less interesting to me. Cozumel was fun but crowded.

name: Sofia K.
review: Costa Maya was actually my favorite because we did a smaller, more personal excursion. It felt less rushed compared to Roatan. Cozumel was easy but a bit too busy for my taste.

name: Marcus Lee
review: Roatan was the highlight thanks to the snorkeling. Costa Maya didn’t offer much without an excursion. Cozumel was convenient and flexible.

name: Olga Petrenko
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to enjoy without planning. Roatan required more structure. Costa Maya felt crowded and less authentic.

name: Darius H.
review: We skipped Costa Maya entirely after getting off briefly. Roatan and Cozumel both had better options for excursions and exploring.

name: Nadia El-Sayed
review: Roatan was beautiful but rushed. Costa Maya required more planning than we expected. Cozumel was relaxed and easy.

name: Caleb R.
review: Cozumel felt very touristy but still enjoyable. Roatan had more unique experiences. Costa Maya was just okay.

name: Hannah B.
review: Roatan was our favorite stop because of the wildlife experiences. Costa Maya was less memorable. Cozumel was convenient.

name: Victor Santos
review: Costa Maya felt like it needed better planning. Roatan and Cozumel were both enjoyable in different ways.

name: Alison Greene
review:
This itinerary had a good mix, but the experience really depended on how much planning you did for each stop. Roatan felt the most immersive. We booked a longer excursion that included multiple stops across the island, and it gave us a better sense of the place beyond just beaches. The downside was that we didn’t have much downtime, and it felt a bit rushed toward the end.

Costa Maya was the trickiest port for us. We didn’t book anything in advance, thinking we could explore freely, but once we got off the ship, it felt very controlled and crowded. We eventually took a taxi out to a quieter beach, which improved things, but it required extra effort and time.

Cozumel was by far the easiest stop. You can plan an excursion or just wander, and both work. We chose to keep it simple and still had a really enjoyable day. It’s probably the most flexible port on this itinerary.

name: Camila Torres
review:
Roatan ended up being our favorite stop, mostly because we booked a small-group island tour that included a wildlife sanctuary and a quiet beach on the other side of the island. The drive itself was eye-opening, seeing neighborhoods beyond the cruise port area. Holding a sloth felt surreal, even if it was clearly set up for tourists. The beach portion was relaxed and not overcrowded, which we appreciated.

Costa Maya was more frustrating. We tried to do our own thing and walk beyond the port, but it felt a bit disorganized once outside. We ended up at a beach club that was fine but nothing memorable. Cozumel felt the easiest to navigate. We just grabbed a taxi downtown, wandered shops, and had lunch by the water. It was simple but enjoyable.

name: Darius Cole
review:
Cozumel was the highlight for me. I booked a snorkeling excursion and the reef visibility was incredible that day. The guides kept things moving without rushing us too much, which I appreciated. After that, we still had enough time to walk around near the port and grab a drink.

Roatan was good but felt more structured. We did a zipline and animal encounter combo. Fun, but a bit crowded and timed.

Costa Maya didn’t really do it for me. We stayed in the port area and it just felt like a big shopping complex. If I did it again, I’d probably book a ruins excursion or just stay on the ship.

name: JenTravels88
review:
We were first-time cruisers and honestly didn’t know what to expect from the ports. Roatan surprised me in a good way. We did a snorkeling trip off a small boat and I was nervous at first, but the water was calm and clear. I saw way more marine life than I expected.

Costa Maya was a long day because we chose the ruins tour. The bus ride felt longer than advertised, but once we got there, it was worth it. The guide explained a lot about the history, which made it more meaningful.

Cozumel was more relaxed. We didn’t book anything and just walked around, which was actually nice after two more structured days.

name: Derrick Shaw
review:
Roatan was great for adventure. I did an ATV excursion through some pretty rugged terrain, which was fun but also a bit chaotic. Helmets were optional, which was… interesting. Still, I had a blast.

Costa Maya felt rushed. We tried to explore beyond the port but didn’t have enough time to really settle anywhere.

Cozumel was the easiest day. Took a taxi to a beach club and just relaxed. That ended up being my favorite part of the trip.

name: Priya Nair
review:
Costa Maya ended up being the most memorable stop for me because of a small cooking class excursion we booked. It wasn’t fancy, but we learned how to make tortillas and salsa, and the group was small enough that it felt personal. The staff were funny and engaging, which made a big difference.

Roatan was beautiful but felt more commercialized in the areas we visited. We did a beach break that was nice, though a bit crowded.

Cozumel was very touristy but convenient. Everything felt easy to access, and we never felt rushed getting back to the ship.

name: TommyR_92
review:
Roatan was probably the most “active” day. We did a zipline and it was way higher than I expected. Views were amazing though.

Costa Maya we skipped getting off. Just didn’t see anything that interested us after hearing about long travel times.

Cozumel we explored on our own. Found a small spot near the water to hang out. Nothing planned, which made it relaxing.

name: Hannah Brooks
review:
Traveling with my parents, we tried to balance activity and relaxation. Roatan worked well because we booked a private driver who took us to a scenic overlook, a small beach, and a local restaurant. It felt more authentic than a large excursion group.

Costa Maya was tougher. We booked a beach club, but the taxi situation was confusing and a bit aggressive. Once there, it was fine, just not outstanding.

Cozumel was the easiest port logistically. We walked off the ship, found a taxi quickly, and spent the day at a quieter beach area. That simplicity made a big difference.

name: Leo Martinez
review:
Cozumel felt very polished compared to the other stops. We did a guided snorkeling trip and everything ran on time. Maybe a little too structured, but no complaints.

Roatan had more personality. We did a combined tour with animals and a beach stop, and while it was busy, it felt lively.

Costa Maya was my least favorite. The port area felt artificial and we didn’t venture far enough out to really see more.

name: Kelsey W
review:
Roatan was fun but also a bit overwhelming with how many excursion options there were. We chose a snorkeling trip and it was good, just crowded.

Costa Maya we did a ruins tour. Long bus ride, but really interesting once there.

Cozumel was easy and relaxed. We just walked around and had lunch.

name: Andre Dupont
review:
What stood out to me was how different each port felt in terms of pacing. Roatan was energetic and slightly chaotic, especially around the excursion meet-up points. We joined a small-group tour that included a botanical area and chocolate-making demonstration. That part felt more intimate and was a highlight of the entire cruise.

Costa Maya required more planning. We booked a ruins excursion, and while the site itself was impressive, the early departure and long transportation time made the day feel rushed. It’s not something I would repeat, even though I’m glad I saw it once.

Cozumel, on the other hand, was effortless. We skipped excursions entirely, walked into town, and spent time near the water. It felt like the only port where we could truly move at our own pace without worrying about logistics.

name: Samantha K
review:
Costa Maya was the biggest letdown for me. We stayed in the port area and it just felt like a big shopping zone with a pool in the middle. I wish we had planned an excursion instead.

Roatan was much better. We did a beach and snorkeling combo that felt more natural.

Cozumel was somewhere in between. Touristy, but easy to enjoy.

name: Dev Patel
review:
As someone who prefers independent travel, I found Cozumel to be the best port because it allowed flexibility. We rented a car for a few hours and drove along the coast, stopping at quieter spots. That freedom made it feel less like a cruise stop.

Roatan required more coordination. We joined a pre-arranged excursion that included wildlife encounters and a beach break. It was enjoyable but clearly designed for cruise passengers.

Costa Maya was the most challenging to navigate on our own. We tried to explore outside the port but didn’t feel like we had enough time to go far. In hindsight, booking something structured there would have been better.

name: Olivia Chen
review:
Roatan stood out because of the scenery. Even just driving to our excursion, the views were beautiful. We did a snorkeling trip and it was one of the better ones I’ve done.

Costa Maya felt more limited unless you booked something specific.

Cozumel was easy and convenient, which I appreciated after two busier days.

name: Brian T
review:
Roatan was crowded at the excursion sites we visited, but still fun overall. The animal encounter part felt a bit rushed though.

Costa Maya we did a beach club. It was decent, just nothing special.

Cozumel was the best for just walking around and doing your own thing.

name: Elena Petrova
review:
I was traveling solo and found Roatan to be surprisingly easy to navigate with a group excursion. I joined a snorkeling tour, and the guides made sure everyone felt comfortable, which I appreciated. It didn’t feel isolating even though I was on my own.

Costa Maya was less enjoyable. I stayed in the port area and didn’t feel motivated to explore further. It felt designed for quick shopping rather than cultural experience.

Cozumel was a nice balance. I joined a small walking group I met on the ship and we explored together, which made it more enjoyable. It’s probably the port I would return to independently.

name: Chris & Dana
review:
Traveling as a couple, we preferred slower-paced days. Cozumel worked perfectly for that. We went to a quieter beach club and spent hours just relaxing.

Roatan was more activity-focused. We did a combo excursion and felt a bit rushed between stops.

Costa Maya didn’t leave much of an impression. Next time we’d plan something more structured.

name: Miguel Santos
review:
Roatan had the best mix of nature and activity. We did a guided tour that included a viewpoint, local market, and beach stop. It felt like we saw more of the island beyond the typical tourist spots.

Costa Maya was more hit or miss. The beach we went to was nice, but getting there involved some confusion with taxis and timing.

Cozumel was very straightforward. Good for a relaxed day without much planning.

name: Rachel Green
review:
Costa Maya surprised me in a good way because we booked a small, less crowded beach club. It felt more relaxed than I expected after hearing mixed reviews.

Roatan was fun but busy, especially at the popular excursion spots.

Cozumel felt the most polished and tourist-friendly, which made it easy but less unique.

name: Noah Williams
review:
Roatan felt like the most adventurous stop. We did ziplining and it was honestly a highlight of the trip.

Costa Maya was quieter for us since we stayed close to the port.

Cozumel was the easiest to just explore without a plan.

name: Fatima Zahra
review:
Looking back, the itinerary itself was well balanced, but how much you enjoy each port really depends on planning ahead. In Roatan, we booked a multi-stop excursion that included a wildlife sanctuary, a short cultural stop, and a beach break. It was a long day, but it gave us a broader sense of the island beyond the cruise terminal. That said, it did feel somewhat rushed at each stop.

Costa Maya required the most effort to enjoy. We chose a ruins excursion, which meant an early start and a long ride inland. The site itself was fascinating and worth seeing at least once, but the logistics made it a tiring day.

Cozumel ended up being our most relaxed stop. We intentionally didn’t book anything and instead explored locally, had a meal by the water, and took our time getting back. It was the only port where we didn’t feel like we were on a schedule.

name: Derrick H.
review: Roatan ended up being the highlight for me. We booked a small group snorkeling trip and the reef was surprisingly vibrant, way better than I expected. The boat ride out was short, which I appreciated since I get motion sick. Costa Maya felt more manufactured. We walked out of the port area and tried to find something local, but it just kept turning into more souvenir shops. Cozumel was somewhere in between. We grabbed a taxi to a public beach and spent a few hours there. It was nice but pretty crowded. Overall I liked the itinerary, but I wish we had more time in Roatan and less in Costa Maya.

name: Priya Desai
review: In Costa Maya we did the ruins excursion, and I’m glad we went, but the bus ride was long and we felt rushed once we got there. It didn’t leave much time to really take it in. Roatan was much more relaxed. We hired a local driver and he took us around the island, including a stop where we saw sloths and monkeys. Cozumel we honestly just walked around near the port and got back on early because nothing stood out to us.

name: Caleb Turner
review: First cruise for us and the ports were what we cared about most. Roatan did not disappoint. We went ziplining through the jungle and it felt very authentic, not overly commercialized. Costa Maya was the opposite experience. The port area is basically a giant shopping complex and it took effort to get beyond that. We ended up renting a taxi to a beach club which was decent but not memorable. Cozumel had better structure. We did a snorkeling excursion that was organized well, but it felt a little too crowded with other groups.

name: Marisol Vega
review: Cozumel was my favorite stop because we did a cooking class in a small group setting. It felt more personal and we actually learned something instead of just being shuffled around. Roatan was nice but the beach we went to had a lot of vendors approaching constantly, which got tiring. Costa Maya was just okay. We stayed near the port and didn’t venture far, which might have been our mistake.

name: Jason Wu
review: We’ve cruised before and this itinerary is pretty standard, but the time in each port felt uneven. Roatan deserved more hours. We did a combination excursion with snorkeling and a short island tour and still felt like we had to rush back. Costa Maya we had extra time but not much to do unless you book something far out. Cozumel was efficient and easy to navigate on your own, which I appreciated.

name: Lena K.
review: Roatan was incredible. We visited a wildlife park and got to interact with sloths and parrots. It felt well organized and not too crowded. Costa Maya, on the other hand, felt like a missed opportunity. We didn’t book an excursion and regretted it because the port area alone didn’t offer much beyond shopping. Cozumel was fun but busy. We did a beach break and it was relaxing, just not very unique.

name: Andre Baptiste
review: Costa Maya surprised me in a good way. We booked a small beach club outside the main port and it was peaceful, not crowded at all. Roatan was more active for us—we did snorkeling and a quick stop in town, but the timing felt tight. Cozumel was the busiest stop by far. Too many ships in port made everything feel packed.

name: Nadia El-Sayed
review: We skipped Roatan entirely and stayed onboard because none of the excursions appealed to us, which I slightly regret now after hearing others talk about it. Costa Maya we explored independently and ended up renting bikes, which was actually fun and different. Cozumel was easy to navigate and we found a small café away from the crowds that made the day worthwhile.

name: Tomislav Petrovic
review: Roatan felt the most authentic. We hired a guide at the port and he showed us parts of the island that weren’t just tourist spots. Costa Maya felt overly staged, especially near the port entrance. Cozumel had better infrastructure, but also more crowds. I enjoyed the variety across the stops though.

name: Emily Nguyen
review: We did a snorkeling trip in Cozumel that started early and that was probably the best organized excursion of the trip. Everything ran on time. Roatan was beautiful but transportation felt a bit chaotic getting to our beach location. Costa Maya we mostly wandered and shopped, which was fine but not memorable.

name: Hector Alvarez
review: Cozumel was easily the most tourist-friendly stop. We took a taxi downtown and walked around, had lunch, and made it back with no stress. Costa Maya felt more limited unless you plan ahead. Roatan had great scenery but getting around took longer than expected, so we didn’t get to everything we wanted.

name: Rachel O'Connor
review: Roatan ended up being my favorite port because it felt less commercial compared to the others. We booked a snorkeling excursion that took us out to a reef with clear water and not too many people around. Costa Maya felt very controlled, almost like a theme park version of a port. We walked through the shopping area and decided to go back early. Cozumel had a better balance. We went to a beach club that limited capacity, so it didn’t feel overcrowded. That made a big difference. I think planning ahead really matters for these stops, especially Costa Maya.

name: Omar Haddad
review: Costa Maya was our least favorite stop. We tried to explore on our own but kept running into tourist-heavy areas. Roatan was better, especially the beach we visited. Cozumel was fun for shopping and walking around, but nothing stood out as exceptional.

name: Chloe Martin
review: We did a guided ruins tour from Costa Maya and while it was interesting, the time spent traveling compared to time at the site felt off. Roatan was much more relaxed and enjoyable for us. Cozumel was easy but crowded, especially near the port.

name: DeShawn Carter
review: Roatan had the best excursion options by far. We did a combination of snorkeling and a short cultural tour, and it felt like we saw a lot in one day. Costa Maya felt like it required more planning. Without an excursion, there isn’t much beyond the port area. Cozumel was somewhere in the middle. We went to a beach club that was nice but fairly standard. I think the biggest issue was timing. We docked later in one port and it cut into what we could do, which made it feel rushed. Still, the variety between ports made the trip interesting.

name: Yuki Tanaka
review: We focused on relaxing, so in Cozumel we picked a quiet beach and stayed there most of the day. Roatan we tried to explore more but transportation delays made it harder than expected. Costa Maya we didn’t leave the port area and honestly that was enough for us.

name: Gregory P.
review: Roatan was great for snorkeling, very clear water. Costa Maya felt too commercialized for my taste. Cozumel had good balance but also the most crowds. I’d still do this itinerary again for Roatan alone.

name: Lucia Fernandez
review: Roatan offered the most memorable experience for me. We joined a small group excursion that combined a wildlife visit and a beach stop, and it felt well paced without being rushed. Costa Maya was a bit disappointing because we didn’t book anything in advance and ended up staying within the port complex, which felt artificial. Cozumel redeemed things slightly. We found a quieter beach farther from the main dock area, but it required negotiating with taxi drivers and figuring out timing to get back. Overall, I’d say planning ahead is essential for these ports.

name: Benjamin Okeke
review: Cozumel was probably the easiest port to enjoy without much planning. We just walked, ate, and relaxed. Roatan required more coordination but had better natural beauty. Costa Maya felt like you either book an excursion or you miss out.

name: Sophie Laurent
review: We had mixed feelings about the itinerary overall. Roatan stood out because of how much there was to see beyond the port. We booked a private driver who took us around different parts of the island, including a quieter beach and a small local market, which gave us a better sense of the place. Costa Maya felt much more controlled. The port area is designed for cruise passengers, and unless you commit to a longer excursion like the ruins, it’s hard to escape that environment. We chose not to do the ruins because of the travel time, so we ended up feeling like we didn’t experience much there. Cozumel was somewhere in between. We did a snorkeling trip that was enjoyable but crowded, followed by some time walking around town. It was convenient but less memorable compared to Roatan. If I were to do this itinerary again, I would plan more carefully for Costa Maya and prioritize unique excursions rather than staying near the port.

name: Jordan P.
review: Roatan was the highlight for us. We booked a private driver named Victor who took us all over the island before the crowds hit the West End. The greenery is stunning, though the traffic near the port at Coxen Hole is absolutely no joke. We spent about three hours at West Bay Beach and the snorkeling right off the shore was some of the best I've seen in the Caribbean. We did find the vendors a bit pushy there, but a polite 'no' usually worked. In Cozumel, we decided to skip the organized excursions and just rented a jeep to drive to the 'wild side' of the island. It was so much better than the crowded pier area. We stopped at Coconuts for a drink and just enjoyed the view of the waves hitting the rocks. The contrast between the two sides of the island is crazy. Definitely felt like we had enough time in both places, but I wish the Costa Maya stop was longer because by the time we got back from the ruins, we had zero time to explore the actual port shops or the pool area.

name: bobby_travels88
review: Cozumel was okay but very commercial. We did the chocolate and tequila tasting tour which felt a bit rushed. You spend a lot of time waiting for other people to get off the bus. I think next time I'll just take a taxi to a private beach club. Costa Maya was much better for us; we did the Chaccoben ruins and our guide was so knowledgeable about the history. The bus ride is about an hour each way, but it's worth it to see the structures. Roatan was beautiful but we had a major delay getting back to the ship because of a road accident that backed up traffic for miles. It was a bit stressful watching the clock, but the ship waited since it was an official excursion. The island itself is gorgeous, just be mindful of the narrow roads.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: The water in Cozumel is crystal clear! We went to Palancar Beach and it was so peaceful. Roatan was also great, we did the sloth sanctuary. Those animals are so sweet. Costa Maya was the only letdown, it felt like a giant outdoor mall designed just to take your money. I'd recommend getting out of the port area immediately if you want a real experience.

name: Kwame Akoto
review: Wasn't a fan of the Costa Maya port. It's basically a maze of souvenir shops that you have to walk through to get anywhere. We ended up just taking a quick taxi to Mahahual for some street tacos which were excellent, but the beach there had a lot of seaweed when we visited. Cozumel was a much smoother experience. We did the America's Cup 12-meter regatta excursion and it was exhilarating to actually help sail those boats. Roatan was nice but very humid. We did a glass-bottom boat tour because my wife doesn't like to swim, and we still saw plenty of coral and fish. The pier in Roatan is quite long, so be prepared for a bit of a hike if you aren't using the chairlift thing they have there.

name: SunSeeker_92
review: Roatan: Little French Key is a literal paradise. Quiet, clear water, and great food. Cozumel: Avoid the main shopping strip unless you like being yelled at by jewelry salesmen. Costa Maya: The ruins were cool but the mosquitoes were brutal. Bring spray!

name: Mrs. Higgins
review: We stayed on the ship during the Costa Maya stop. We've been there before and found the walk from the ship to the exit very tiring. However, we loved Cozumel! We took a taxi to the town square and just sat at a local cafe watching the world go by. It felt very authentic once you got away from the cruise terminal. In Roatan, we took the 'Best of Roatan' bus tour. It was a good way to see the island without much walking, which was perfect for us. The island is much more mountainous than I expected. Our driver was very proud of his home and told us a lot about the local economy and schools. It was a very pleasant day and we never felt rushed back to the ship.

name: Liam O'Connor
review: I'm a big diver so Roatan was the main event for me. The reef there is in much better shape than Cozumel. We did a two-tank dive and saw a few reef sharks and a massive grouper. The boat pick-up was right near the pier, very efficient. In Cozumel, the current was quite strong the day we were there, so the drift dive was a bit faster than I like. For Costa Maya, I decided to just relax. I walked down to the Malecón in Mahahual and found a small spot with a bucket of beers for twenty bucks. It was a nice break from the ship's prices. If you're a diver, Roatan is a must, but Cozumel is still decent for the convenience.

name: Chen Wei
review: The excursion we booked in Costa Maya was cancelled last minute, which was frustrating. We ended up just staying in the port pool area. It was fine, but very crowded and loud with the music. Cozumel was much better; we did a cooking class where we learned to make authentic salsa and tamales. It was held at a beautiful location overlooking the ocean. Roatan was my favorite though. We went to a botanical garden and the smells of the tropical flowers were amazing. It's a very green island compared to the others. I wish we had more time in Roatan, six hours felt way too short to really see the whole island.

name: Bethany G.
review: Cozumel was way too busy with five ships in port. We couldn't even find a taxi for twenty minutes. Once we got to the beach club it was packed. Roatan was much more laid back and the sloth park was worth every penny. Costa Maya was just okay, felt very artificial.

name: Raj Singh
review: I was really looking forward to the Mayan ruins in Costa Maya, and they did not disappoint. We went to Kohunlich to see the masks. It’s a very long drive, nearly two hours each way, so be prepared for a lot of time on a bus. The site itself is stunning and much less crowded than the more popular spots. Cozumel was a bit of a different vibe. We did a catamaran snorkel trip. The crew was great, but the snorkeling spots were very busy with other boats. Roatan was the most beautiful port aesthetically. We did a zip-line tour through the jungle canopy. It was professional and felt safe, and the views of the ocean from the top of the hills were breathtaking. My only complaint is the organization at the Roatan pier—it was total chaos trying to find our tour operator among the hundreds of people holding signs.

name: Chloe_Adventures
review: If you go to Cozumel, go to the East side! It's rugged and there's no electricity in most places. We found a little bar called Chen Rio and had the best fried fish of our lives. Roatan was also great for exploring. We rented a water taxi from West End to West Bay for $5 and it was a fun little ride. Costa Maya... meh. It's a purpose-built port. If you don't leave the port area, you haven't really seen Mexico. We did the bird sanctuary in the port which was actually kind of cool for the kids, but otherwise, it's just a lot of shops selling the same t-shirts. The pier in Costa Maya is also extremely long, so if you have mobility issues, wait for the little trolley.

name: Derek Vogel
review: Roatan is beautiful but the poverty is very visible once you leave the tourist areas. It's a bit of an eye-opener. The beach at Tabyana was nice but overcrowded. In Cozumel, we did the tequila seminar. It was informative, though clearly a sales pitch at the end. The food provided was actually the best meal of the trip. Costa Maya felt like a tourist trap. Everything is priced in USD and quite expensive for Mexico. We tried to walk to the local village but it was further than it looked in the heat. Next time I'd book a proper excursion there or just stay on the boat and enjoy the empty pool.

name: S_Van_Dyk
review: Great itinerary overall. Roatan was the star. We went to Daniel Johnson's Sloth and Monkey Hangout. The staff really cares for the animals. We also did a snorkel at the shipwreck nearby. Cozumel was good for shopping. We got some great vanilla and leather goods. Costa Maya was our 'lazy day'. We just hung out at the port pool. It's huge and free to use, which was nice. Drinks are pricey there though. If you want a more authentic feel, Costa Maya might not be for you, but for a relaxing day with a clear pool, it works.

name: Amara J.
review: Cozumel was fantastic for our family. We went to Chankanaab and the kids loved the sea lion show and the lagoon. It’s a very easy taxi ride from the pier. Roatan was a bit more difficult with kids; the roads are bumpy and the excursions take a while to get to. We did a beach day which was pretty, but the sand flies were an issue—make sure to bring the right repellent. Costa Maya was our least favorite stop. We took a bus to some ruins and the guide was a bit boring, plus it was incredibly hot with very little shade. I think I would have preferred a water-based activity in Costa Maya instead.

name: Priya K.
review: Loved the snorkeling in Cozumel, so many colorful fish! Roatan was also great, we did a private island tour. Costa Maya was a bit disappointing as the beach was full of seagrass, but the port itself is nice for a quick walk.

name: Tom_and_Linda
review: We are experienced cruisers and this was our third time on this route. In Roatan, we always go to the West End for lunch at a local spot. It’s much cheaper than the cruise-sanctioned areas. Cozumel is getting very crowded; we noticed three other massive ships there, which made the downtown area almost impossible to navigate. We ended up just going back to the ship early. In Costa Maya, we tried the Maya Chan beach club for the first time. It is a bit of a bumpy ride to get there, but the service is incredible. They limit the number of guests so it felt very private. If you want a quiet day away from the thousands of other passengers, that's the place to go. Just book early as they sell out.

name: Javier M.
review: Costa Maya ruins are a must-see! Chaccoben is amazing. The jungle setting makes you feel like Indiana Jones. The guides are very respectful of the history. Cozumel is better for water sports. We did a helmet dive which was a unique way to see the reef without needing to know how to scuba. Roatan was a bit of a mixed bag. The scenery is 10/10 but the infrastructure is struggling. We were stuck in a shuttle for 45 minutes just trying to get out of the port area. Once we got to our destination it was fine, but the logistics on that island need work.

name: Nia Williams
review: I wish we had more time in Cozumel. There is so much to see beyond the jewelry stores. We found a great little museum about the island's history that was almost empty. Roatan was okay, but very commercialized around the sloth attractions. It felt a bit like an assembly line of tourists. Costa Maya was actually better than I expected. We did a paddle boarding excursion and the water was very calm and shallow. It was a great workout and the guides were very helpful. The walk back to the ship is long, so don't wait until the last minute.

name: Greg L.
review: The fishing in Roatan was incredible. Caught a nice sized mahi-mahi. The local captain knew exactly where to go. Cozumel was just okay for me, too many people. Costa Maya was a total skip.

name: H_Yamamoto
review: For those visiting Roatan, the monkeys are very clever, so watch your pockets! We had a wonderful day at the Gumbalimba Park. It was very well organized. In Cozumel, we participated in a turtle salvage program which was very educational. It was moving to see the conservation efforts. Costa Maya was our last stop. We found the port area to be very loud with music, which made it hard to relax. We walked further out to find a quiet spot on the beach. The sand is very white and beautiful, but there is a lot of coral rubble, so water shoes are a good idea. Overall, a good variety of islands, though the time spent in each felt a little rushed due to the ship's schedule. I would have liked to stay in Cozumel until sunset.

name: Ethan Morales
review: Roatan was easily my favorite stop. We booked a snorkeling trip and the reef was actually impressive, not just hype. The water was calm and visibility was great. Costa Maya felt very staged to me. Once you leave the ship, it’s basically shops and more shops unless you’ve planned something. We didn’t, so that’s on us. Cozumel was more flexible. We grabbed a taxi to a quieter beach area and just relaxed for a few hours. It wasn’t groundbreaking, but it was pleasant. I think the itinerary is decent overall, but Roatan carries it.

name: Anika Patel
review: Costa Maya was the only port where I felt like time was poorly used. We did the ruins excursion and while the site itself was interesting, the long bus ride and early departure made it feel rushed and tiring. Roatan was much more enjoyable. We visited a small wildlife sanctuary and spent time at the beach after. Cozumel we just wandered around downtown, which was easy and stress-free.

name: Logan Pierce
review: First time cruising and I didn’t realize how different each port would feel. Roatan felt more natural, less built up. We did ziplining and it was a highlight. Costa Maya felt like a controlled environment. We stayed near the port and it didn’t feel very authentic. Cozumel was busy but had more to do without booking anything in advance.

name: Hannah Goldberg
review: Cozumel ended up being my favorite because of how easy it was to explore. We took a taxi to a beach club that limited guests, so it wasn’t overcrowded. Roatan was nice, but getting transportation took longer than expected. Costa Maya didn’t really stand out to me, we mostly walked around and went back early.

name: Trevor Jenkins
review: We’ve done similar itineraries before and this one felt a bit rushed in certain ports. Roatan deserved more time. We did a combo excursion with snorkeling and a short island tour and felt like we barely scratched the surface. Costa Maya had the opposite problem, more time but not much to do unless you book something far out. Cozumel was balanced but crowded.

name: Camila Rojas
review: Roatan was beautiful but also a bit chaotic when it came to getting to our excursion meeting point. Once we got there, the beach was worth it. Costa Maya felt like a missed opportunity since we didn’t book anything. The port area alone didn’t hold our interest long. Cozumel was enjoyable, we did a snorkeling excursion that was well organized.

name: Noah Kim
review: Costa Maya surprised me. We rented a golf cart and drove down to a quieter beach area, which made the day feel more independent. Roatan was good for snorkeling, though it felt a bit rushed getting back to the ship. Cozumel was very crowded the day we were there, which made it less enjoyable.

name: Amara Okafor
review: We skipped Costa Maya completely and stayed onboard because nothing really appealed to us. In Roatan, we did a small group tour that included a wildlife stop and a beach visit, which felt well paced. Cozumel was easy to navigate, but also the most touristy of the three.

name: Viktor Sokolov
review: Roatan had the most character. We hired a local guide and saw parts of the island beyond the typical tourist stops. Costa Maya felt very commercialized, especially near the port. Cozumel had better infrastructure but also more crowds, so it was a trade-off.

name: Lily Chen
review: We did a snorkeling trip in Cozumel that started early and it was the smoothest excursion of the cruise. Everything was organized and on time. Roatan was enjoyable but transportation took longer than expected. Costa Maya we mostly just walked around and didn’t find much beyond shopping.

name: Miguel Santos
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to explore without planning. We just walked around and found a place to relax. Roatan required more coordination but had better scenery. Costa Maya felt like you needed an excursion or else it was pretty limited.

name: Claire Dubois
review: Roatan ended up being the highlight for us. We booked a small group excursion that included snorkeling and a short island tour, and it felt like a good mix of activity and relaxation. Costa Maya felt more like a constructed port experience, especially if you don’t leave the main area. We chose not to do a long excursion there and it showed. Cozumel was easier to manage. We went to a quieter beach away from the main port, which helped avoid the crowds. Overall, I think the itinerary works best if you plan ahead, especially for Costa Maya.

name: Jamal Reed
review: Costa Maya was my least favorite stop. We tried to explore on our own but didn’t find much beyond tourist shops. Roatan was better, especially the beach we visited. Cozumel was decent but very busy.

name: Sofia Dimitriou
review: We did a ruins excursion in Costa Maya and while it was interesting, the long bus ride made it feel rushed. Roatan was more relaxed and enjoyable. Cozumel had good options but felt crowded near the port.

name: Darnell Price
review: Roatan had the best excursion options in my opinion. We did snorkeling and a short island tour and it felt like a full day. Costa Maya required more planning, otherwise you’re just in the port area. Cozumel was fine but not particularly memorable.

name: Keiko Yamamoto
review: We preferred relaxing, so in Cozumel we went to a quieter beach and stayed there most of the day. Roatan we tried to explore more but transportation delays cut into our time. Costa Maya we didn’t leave the port and that was enough for us.

name: Oliver Bennett
review: Roatan was great for snorkeling, very clear water and not too crowded. Costa Maya felt too commercialized for my taste. Cozumel had a good balance but was busy.

name: Daniela Costa
review: Roatan offered the most memorable experience for me. We joined a small group excursion that combined a wildlife visit and a beach stop, and it felt well paced without being rushed. Costa Maya was a bit disappointing because we didn’t book anything in advance and ended up staying within the port complex, which felt artificial. Cozumel redeemed things slightly. We found a quieter beach farther from the main dock area, but it required negotiating with taxi drivers and figuring out timing to get back. Overall, I’d say planning ahead is essential for these ports.

name: Samuel Wright
review: Cozumel was probably the easiest stop to enjoy without much effort. We walked around and relaxed. Roatan required more planning but was more rewarding. Costa Maya felt limited unless you had an excursion booked.

name: Ingrid Larsen
review: We had mixed feelings about the itinerary. Roatan stood out because there was more to see beyond the immediate port area. We booked a private driver who showed us different parts of the island, including a quieter beach and a small local market, which made it feel more authentic. Costa Maya felt much more controlled and commercial. Without a long excursion, it’s hard to experience anything beyond the port complex. We chose not to do the ruins due to the long travel time, so we ended up heading back earlier than expected. Cozumel was somewhere in between. We did a snorkeling excursion that was enjoyable but crowded, followed by some time walking around town. It was convenient but didn’t feel as unique as Roatan. I think this itinerary works best if you prioritize excursions in advance, especially for Costa Maya, otherwise it can feel repetitive.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown for us. The port area feels like a massive, manufactured mall and it’s hard to find anything authentic without a long taxi ride. We tried to walk to the local fishing village, Mahahual, but the heat was intense and the sidewalk just sort of ends. However, Cozumel was a total 180. We avoided the ship excursions and grabbed a cab to the East side of the island. It’s rugged, no electricity in most of the bars, and the waves are huge. We had fresh ceviche at Chen Rio and it was the best meal of the entire trip. Roatan was beautiful from the ship, but the pier was so congested with shore excursion groups that it took us nearly 40 minutes just to get through the gate to meet our private driver. Once we were out, the island was lush and green, but the logistics at the port really need some work if they are going to host ships this large.

name: Darnell_W84
review: Roatan was the highlight for me. I did the scuba diving excursion and the reef is in incredible shape compared to other spots in the Caribbean. We saw a couple of reef sharks and a massive sea turtle. In Cozumel, we did the 'Amazing Race' style scavenger hunt through the town. It was a fun way to see the backstreets and interact with locals rather than just sitting at a bar. Costa Maya was our 'ship day'—we looked at the crowds on the pier and decided to just stay on board and enjoy the quiet pool. If you've seen one Mexican port mall, you've seen them all, and Costa Maya is the king of them.

name: Priya Sharma
review: First time cruising and I loved the variety! In Roatan, we went to Daniel Johnson’s to see the sloths. They were so soft and the guides were very gentle with them. The monkeys are a different story—one tried to open my backpack! Cozumel was very busy with four other ships in port, so the shopping area was a bit overwhelming. We escaped to a beach club called Mr. Sanchos which was fun but very loud. For Costa Maya, we did the Chaccoben ruins. It’s about a 50-minute bus ride each way, but seeing the temples peeking out from the jungle was unforgettable. I just wish we had more than six hours in Costa Maya because we felt very rushed getting back to the ship before all-aboard.

name: Greg_V
review: If you go to Cozumel, skip the ship tours and go to the Money Bar. Great snorkeling right off the shore and the margaritas are actually strong. Roatan was okay, but the traffic near the port is a nightmare. It took us an hour to move three miles.

name: Linda Bernstein
review: I have limited mobility, so I was worried about the ports. Costa Maya has a long pier, but they have a trolley that takes you to the port entrance which was a lifesaver. We just stayed in the port area and watched the salt water pool performers. In Cozumel, we took a taxi to the main square. The sidewalks are mostly flat and easy to navigate. Roatan was much harder; the terrain is very hilly and the port area is quite steep in places. We did a bus tour of the island which was perfect. We got to see the views from the highest point without having to hike. The scheduling for Roatan was a bit tight, though. We only had from 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM, which doesn't leave much room for error if the traffic is bad. I'd recommend sticking to ship-sponsored tours there just for the peace of mind.

name: Hassan J.
review: Roatan snorkeling is top tier. We went to West Bay and the reef starts just a few feet from the sand. Crystal clear water and tons of purple fans. Cozumel felt too commercialized for my taste. Costa Maya ruins were cool but bring bug spray, the mosquitoes are the size of birds.

name: Mei_Chen_Travels
review: We did the Chaccoben Mayan ruins in Costa Maya and it was the best part of our vacation. Our guide, Leo, was so passionate about his heritage and explained the carvings in a way that made history come alive. It is a bit of a hike to the top of the structures, so wear good shoes. In Cozumel, we did a chocolate making workshop. It was informative, but the facility felt a bit like a tourist factory. Roatan was our last stop. We just did a simple beach break at Little French Key. It was picturesque, but the boat ride over felt a little sketchy with how many people they crammed into the small panga. Overall, the itinerary is great for history buffs and beach lovers alike, though I think the time allocation in Cozumel is almost too long—we were bored by 4 PM.

name: Liam O'Shea
review: Cozumel is the place for deep sea fishing. We caught three Mahi-Mahi and the captain was a legend. Roatan was too crowded at the sloth place, felt like a tourist trap. Stayed on the ship for Costa Maya because the port prices are a joke.

name: Aiyana Miller
review: I really appreciated the cultural excursion in Costa Maya called the 'Ancestral Kitchen.' We learned how to grind corn and make tortillas from scratch with local Mayan women. It felt authentic and the food was incredible. Compare that to Cozumel, where everything feels like it’s owned by a big corporation. We tried to find a local spot for lunch in Cozumel and ended up walking six blocks away from the waterfront to find a small taco stand that didn't have menus in English. That was the best part of Cozumel! Roatan was stunningly beautiful, especially the drive over the mountain ridge, but the poverty is very visible once you leave the gated port area. It’s a bit of a reality check. I wish the cruise line did more to support the local infrastructure there because the roads are in rough shape.

name: S_Kulkarni
review: Roatan: Did the Zip-line and it was exhilarating. The views of the ocean from the top of the trees are amazing. Costa Maya: Too many vendors hassling you. Cozumel: The submarine tour was unique, seeing the shipwreck was the highlight for the kids. Good mix of ports.

name: Mateo Santos
review: My wife and I are experienced cruisers and we usually avoid the 'private' beach breaks. In Roatan, we rented a water taxi for $5 to take us from West End to West Bay. It’s a great way to see the coastline. The water in Roatan is much bluer than in Costa Maya. Speaking of Costa Maya, we found a tiny massage tent about a mile down the beach from the port that was half the price of the ship's spa. Cozumel is always a reliable stop for a rental jeep adventure. We drove the entire loop of the island in about three hours, including stops for photos. The logistics of getting off the ship in Cozumel can be a nightmare if there are five ships in port like there were for us. Plan for at least 30 minutes just to get off the pier and past the security guards.

name: Hiroshi Tanaka
review: The port in Costa Maya is very beautiful but very expensive. 15 dollars for a margarita is more than I pay in Tokyo! We enjoyed the Cozumel ruins at San Gervasio, they are smaller than the ones in Costa Maya but very peaceful. Roatan was very green and lush, we enjoyed the botanical gardens.

name: Clara_H_99
review: I'm a solo traveler and I felt very safe in all three ports. In Roatan, I joined a group tour for the glass-bottom boat. The reef is spectacular and you don't even have to get wet. Cozumel was easy to navigate on foot, I just spent the afternoon at the museum and then had some coffee at a local cafe. Costa Maya was the only place I felt a bit uneasy because the taxi drivers are very aggressive when you walk out of the port gates. I ended up just going back inside the port area and sitting by the pool. The scheduling was decent, though we arrived in Cozumel very early (7:00 AM) and nothing was open yet. It would be better if we arrived a bit later and stayed into the evening so we could see the nightlife.

name: David Levinson
review: Costa Maya has some of the best beach clubs. Maya Chan is the way to go, but you have to book it months in advance. The food there is better than the ship food. Cozumel is just okay, mostly for shopping. Roatan is the most scenic port by far.

name: Yasmine_B
review: We had a bit of a disaster in Roatan. Our tour bus broke down on the way back from the beach and we were stuck on the side of the road for forty minutes. Luckily, it was a ship excursion so they had to wait for us, but it was nerve-wracking watching the departure time come and go. Aside from that, the beach at West Bay was lovely, though very crowded. Cozumel was much more relaxed for us; we did a tequila tasting and learned a lot about the different aging processes. Costa Maya was our least favorite—it just feels like a giant tourist trap. The pool in the port is nice, but the music was so loud we couldn't even have a conversation. I think next time I'd look for a more remote excursion in Costa Maya to get away from the 'Spring Break' vibe of the port.

name: Terrence_Hill
review: Cozumel snorkeling at Chankanaab was great for the kids. They loved the statues underwater. Roatan was a bit of a trek to get to the good spots, but the sloth park made up for the long bus ride. Costa Maya port is just a giant mall, honestly.

name: Kwesi_Travels
review: This itinerary is a great mix of history and relaxation. Roatan offers the best natural beauty; we took a private tour to the East End which is much less developed than the West End. It was fascinating to see the 'Venice of Roatan' where the houses are all on stilts over the water. Cozumel is the best for logistics; everything is very organized and it's easy to get a taxi with set rates posted on a board. Costa Maya is the weak link. The port was built specifically for cruise ships and it shows. There’s no soul to it. We did the Bacalar Lagoon excursion from there, which is a very long day (over 6 hours of travel time) but the 'Lake of Seven Colors' is absolutely stunning. If you hate long bus rides, skip Bacalar, but if you want to see something truly unique, it's worth the exhaustion.

name: Svetlana_K
review: In Roatan we did the underwater scooters. It was the coolest thing I've ever done! You don't even need to know how to swim. Cozumel was just for shopping for us, got some nice silver. Costa Maya we stayed on the boat, too hot and the port looked boring.

name: Jackson_Family_5
review: We traveled with three kids and these ports were perfect for them. In Cozumel, we did the sea lion encounter at Chankanaab and they haven't stopped talking about it. The park is very well maintained. In Roatan, we did a beach day at a resort that had a big inflatable water park in the ocean. It kept them busy for hours. Costa Maya was actually quite fun for the kids too because of the big pool right in the middle of the port area. It’s shallow and safe. My only complaint is the cost of food in the ports. A simple lunch for five people in Cozumel cost us nearly $120. Next time we might just head back to the ship for lunch before going back out. The port times were generous enough that we could have done that if we had planned better.

name: Zoe_Quinn
review: Roatan is gorgeous but the flies at the beach are no joke. Bring the heavy duty bug spray! Cozumel was a bit too 'commercial' for me, but the snorkeling was decent. Costa Maya is basically a mall with a pool. Not much culture unless you leave.

name: Derrick L.
review: Roatan ended up being our favorite stop mainly because we booked a small group snorkeling trip instead of a big excursion. The reef was really close to shore and the water visibility was better than I expected. Costa Maya felt the opposite. We walked around the port area for a bit and it just felt like a giant shopping complex built for cruise passengers. We debated going out to the ruins but didn’t want to spend most of the day on a bus. Cozumel was somewhere in the middle. We grabbed a taxi downtown and wandered, which was easy enough, but the time in port felt short compared to what we wanted to do.

name: Mei Chen
review: In Costa Maya we signed up for a ruins tour, and while the site itself was impressive, the long drive took away from the experience. It felt like half the day was sitting on a bus. Roatan was more relaxed—we just went to a beach club and stayed there the whole time. Cozumel was crowded the day we arrived, so we stayed near the port and didn’t venture far.

name: Carlos Jiménez
review: Cozumel was the highlight for me. I rented a scooter and explored parts of the island away from the main tourist strip. It felt more authentic than the port area. Roatan had nice scenery, but our excursion felt rushed with too many stops packed in. Costa Maya honestly didn’t leave much of an impression—we walked around, bought a few souvenirs, and went back early.

name: Hannah Brooks
review: We tried to do something different at each port. In Roatan, we visited a wildlife park where we saw monkeys and sloths, which was fun but also a bit crowded. Costa Maya was more low-key—we took a short taxi ride to a quieter beach instead of staying at the port. Cozumel felt very commercialized, but the snorkeling there was excellent, probably the best water we experienced all trip.

name: Omar El-Sayed
review: Roatan surprised me in a good way. We did a cultural tour that included a small village visit and lunch, which felt more meaningful than just going to a beach. Costa Maya was more frustrating because of aggressive vendors near the port area. Cozumel was easy to navigate, though, and we enjoyed just walking around without a strict plan.

name: Priya Nair
review: I preferred Roatan because the excursion felt more personal. We had a guide who explained local history and didn’t rush us through each stop. Costa Maya felt more like a manufactured stop, and I didn’t feel motivated to explore beyond the port. Cozumel had a better mix—you can choose structured excursions or just explore on your own.

name: Ethan Caldwell
review: We skipped Costa Maya entirely and stayed on the ship because nothing really appealed to us excursion-wise. In Roatan, we did a zipline tour that was fun but a bit disorganized with long waits between runs. Cozumel was straightforward—we took a cab to a beach and stayed there all day, which was relaxing and easy to manage.

name: Lucía Torres
review: Costa Maya ended up being more enjoyable than I expected because we booked a small cooking experience outside the port. It felt less crowded and more interactive. Roatan was beautiful but very humid, and our walking tour became tiring quickly. Cozumel was the most convenient stop, especially for shopping and short activities close to the ship.

name: Ben Whitaker
review: Roatan had the best natural scenery by far. We spent most of our time at a beach with calm water and didn’t feel rushed. Costa Maya, on the other hand, felt like it required more planning to get the most out of it. Cozumel was fine, but it felt very geared toward tourists, which made it less memorable for me.

name: Aisha Rahman
review: In Cozumel we tried a snorkeling excursion that included multiple stops, but it felt a little too structured and timed. Roatan was more relaxed—we booked a private driver and created our own itinerary, which worked much better. Costa Maya was the only stop where I felt unsure what to do once we got off the ship.

name: Trevor G.
review: We really enjoyed Roatan because we booked a smaller excursion that combined snorkeling and a short island tour. It didn’t feel rushed, which was a big plus. Costa Maya was more confusing to navigate independently, and we ended up just staying near the port longer than planned. Cozumel was easy and predictable, which isn’t necessarily a bad thing.

name: Yuki Tanaka
review: Costa Maya was my least favorite stop because the port area felt overly commercialized, and we didn’t venture far beyond it. In contrast, Roatan felt more natural and less crowded once we got away from the main docking area. Cozumel offered a balance, but it was definitely busy with multiple ships in port.

name: Nolan Pierce
review: Roatan stood out because of how green and undeveloped parts of it felt compared to the other stops. We did a guided tour that included a short hike and beach time, which worked well. Costa Maya felt more like a stop built around the cruise industry. Cozumel had great water activities, but you had to deal with crowds.

name: Sofia Alvarez
review: We focused on excursions at every port, and Costa Maya was the most educational with the ruins visit. That said, the early departure time made it a long day. Roatan was more fun and relaxed, especially the beach portion. Cozumel felt like the easiest place to just wander and enjoy without a strict schedule.

name: Gregory Dunn
review: Cozumel was the only port where we didn’t book anything in advance, and it ended up being one of our better days. We just walked, ate, and explored. Roatan required more planning to avoid crowded excursions. Costa Maya didn’t offer much flexibility unless you booked something ahead of time.

name: Amira Haddad
review: Roatan felt like the most authentic destination on this itinerary, especially once we left the immediate port area and drove into smaller communities. We booked a local guide rather than a cruise-organized excursion, which made a big difference in how much we learned. Costa Maya was a mixed experience. The ruins we visited were fascinating, but the transportation logistics were frustrating. There were delays leaving the port, and it cut into our time at the site. Cozumel was efficient and easy to navigate, but also the most crowded. We still enjoyed snorkeling there, though, as the water conditions were excellent.

name: Logan Price
review: Costa Maya didn’t really click for me. We walked around, grabbed a drink, and went back early. Roatan was better because we had a planned activity that kept us engaged. Cozumel was enjoyable just because of how easy it was to get around and find something to do quickly.

name: Chloe Bennett
review: Roatan was the most memorable stop for me because we did a combination of snorkeling and a short island tour. The guide was knowledgeable and gave context about the area, which I appreciated. Costa Maya felt more like a place where you needed to commit to a longer excursion to make it worthwhile, which we didn’t do. Cozumel was somewhere in between—we found a beach spot not too far from the port and spent a relaxing afternoon there without feeling rushed.

name: Raj Patel
review: Cozumel felt very tourist-heavy, but it also had the best infrastructure for visitors. Roatan required a bit more effort to plan ahead, but it paid off with a better experience overall. Costa Maya was the only stop where I felt like I didn’t maximize my time.

name: Elena Petrova
review: Across the three stops, I think the itinerary worked best for travelers who plan excursions in advance. In Roatan, we did a nature-focused outing that included both wildlife and beach time, and it felt well-paced. Costa Maya required more commitment because many of the worthwhile activities are farther from the port, and we underestimated that. Cozumel was the easiest to enjoy spontaneously, but also the most crowded. Overall, I preferred the ports where we had a clear plan rather than trying to figure things out after arriving.

name: Elena Rodriguez
review: Cozumel was the highlight for our group. We bypassed the standard cruise excursions and took a taxi to Punta Sur Eco Beach Park. It’s about a 35-minute drive from the pier, but absolutely worth it for the lighthouse views and the crocodile spotting in the lagoon. The beach there is much quieter than the clubs closer to the ship. Roatan was also beautiful, but the pier area in Coxen Hole felt a bit chaotic with everyone trying to sell you a taxi ride. We eventually made it to West Bay for some snorkeling, which was world-class, though we felt a bit rushed to get back before the ship departed. Costa Maya was our 'stay on the ship' day. We walked around the port area for twenty minutes, but it felt like a giant, overpriced shopping mall designed just for tourists. If you aren't doing a long bus ride to the ruins, there isn't much substance right at the dock. We preferred the quiet pool on the ship instead.

name: D_Chen82
review: Had an okay time in Costa Maya. We did the Chacchoben ruins tour. The history is fascinating and the guides really know their stuff, but be warned: the bus ride is nearly an hour each way and the humidity in the jungle is intense. I wish we had more time to explore the site because it felt like we were being herded back to the bus just as I started getting good photos. In Cozumel, we just walked to a local spot for fish tacos and did some light shopping. It’s a very easy port to navigate on your own if you don't want to spend $100 on a scheduled tour.

name: Terrence Miller
review: Roatan is stunning but the transportation is a mess. We booked a private driver to take us to the East End to see the mangroves. It was peaceful and a nice break from the cruise crowds, but the traffic getting back to the port was stressful. We barely made it back 20 minutes before all-aboard. If you go to Roatan, definitely build in a huge buffer for travel time. The roads are narrow and one accident can block everything. Cozumel was much more organized and the pier layout makes getting in and out of town very simple for older travelers.

name: S_Gupta_Travels
review: We spent our day at Costa Maya at the Maya Chan Beach Club. It’s a bit of a bumpy ride in a taxi to get there, but the service was incredible. It felt much more authentic than the port village. They limit the number of guests so it wasn't crowded at all. In Cozumel, we did the Amazing Cozumel Race, which was a fun way to see the downtown area while getting some exercise. It was a bit competitive for my taste, but the kids loved it. Roatan was just a beach day at Mahogany Bay—convenient, but very 'theme park' feeling.

name: Gary Thompson
review: I was disappointed with the timing in Cozumel. We arrived at 10 AM and had to be back by 5 PM. By the time you get off the ship and through the shops, half the morning is gone. We tried to go to San Gervasio ruins but felt so rushed we couldn't enjoy the site. Roatan was better; we did the sloth sanctuary which was a treat for the grandkids. Just make sure you bring plenty of small bills for tips because the local vendors struggle with change. Costa Maya port is basically a maze of jewelry stores.

name: Chloe Bennett
review: For Roatan, skip the ship tours and find a local guide. We went to Daniel Johnson's Sloth and Monkey Hangout and it was the best $20 I spent all week. The animals are well cared for and the guides are so proud of their island. We also visited a local school which was eye-opening. Cozumel was mostly a shopping day for us. We found some great silver pieces away from the main drag. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown; we tried the 'trolley' into Mahahual but the beach was covered in seaweed so we just headed back early.

name: A_Abebe
review: The snorkeling in Cozumel at the Palancar Reef was breathtaking. I've been on five cruises and this was the clearest water I've ever seen. We saw sea turtles and several rays. However, the boat ride out was quite choppy, so take your ginger pills! Roatan was also nice, we just did a glass-bottom boat tour because my partner doesn't swim well. It was a good compromise. Costa Maya felt a bit fake to us, just a purpose-built port for tourists, so we didn't stay long.

name: Mr_Henderson
review: Costa Maya was great for the Mayan Healing Ritual excursion. It was very relaxing and felt unique compared to the usual beach breaks. It's held in a small village area that felt much more 'real' than the port. In Roatan, we just stayed at the port beach (Mahogany Bay) because we didn't want to deal with the hills and the heat. It's a nice beach, but the water isn't as clear as other parts of the island. Cozumel was fine, just very busy with four other ships in port at the same time.

name: Lars Nielsen
review: I really enjoyed the history at Chacchoben in Costa Maya. Our guide was actually of Mayan descent and told us stories passed down through his family. It's a long day though, especially with the bus ride. Cozumel is just too crowded for me these days. Every time I go, there are more people and more vendors yelling at you to buy t-shirts. Roatan is still the gem of this itinerary. The greenery and the hills are beautiful as you sail in. We did a catamaran sail there that was very peaceful.

name: Priya K
review: Cozumel was fantastic. We did a cooking class where we learned to make authentic salsa and mole. The chef was hilarious and the food was better than anything on the ship. Roatan was a bit of a struggle for us because of the walking. The pier is long and the hills to get to some of the excursions are steep. If you have mobility issues, definitely check the details before booking in Roatan. Costa Maya was very easy to navigate, we just spent an hour in the saltwater pool right at the port.

name: Jackson Lee
review: Roatan scuba diving is a must. The reef is so close to the shore and the coral is in great shape. We went with a local shop instead of the ship's excursion and saved about 40 dollars. Cozumel was okay, we went to a beach club called Mr. Sanchos. It was very loud and crowded, definitely a party vibe which wasn't exactly what we wanted but the food was decent. Costa Maya was underwhelming. We walked to the lighthouse and back, then just went back to our cabin to read.

name: Nancy_G
review: Loved the port in Costa Maya, specifically the chocolate making demo near the back of the plaza. It’s free and very educational. We also took the tram to the local village which was $4. The beach there is narrow but the restaurants have great vibes. Cozumel felt a bit too commercialized this time around. We’ve been there many times and it seems like the prices for taxis just keep going up. Roatan remains my favorite because of the lush landscape, though the humidity was nearly unbearable the day we visited.

name: Benny_W
review: We did the dune buggy tour in Cozumel. It was dusty and bumpy but a total blast. You get to see the 'wild side' of the island where there's no electricity. It's a completely different world from the cruise docks. In Costa Maya, we just stayed in the port area and watched the bird show. It's a nice way to spend two hours without spending a lot of money. Roatan was our snorkeling day, and the fish variety was incredible. We saw parrotfish and even a small nurse shark.

name: Yasmine Mansour
review: Costa Maya Chacchoben ruins were impressive, but the bus didn't have great AC. It made for a very long, sweaty trip. Once we got there, the site was beautiful and we saw some spider monkeys in the trees. In Roatan, we did a private island tour. Our driver was great and showed us the local residential areas, not just the tourist spots. Cozumel was a bit of a wash because it rained most of the day, so we just did some quick duty-free shopping and headed back to the ship early.

name: Derek Vaughn
review: Cozumel is the best for logistics. Everything works. Taxis are regulated, prices are posted, and the drivers are professional. We went to Chankanaab and had a great day snorkeling and seeing the sea lion show. Roatan is beautiful but feels much less developed. The roads are a bit scary if you aren't used to island driving. Costa Maya was my least favorite. The port feels like a trap where they try to keep you from leaving the shopping area to go into the actual town of Mahahual.

name: K_Yamamoto
review: We took a ferry from Cozumel to Playa del Carmen to see Tulum. It's a long day and the ferry ride can be very rough. If you get seasick, stay on Cozumel! The ruins were amazing but we only had about 90 minutes there before we had to start the trek back. Roatan was much more relaxing. We just hung out at a small resort on West End. Costa Maya was just for shopping. I bought some nice vanilla and hot sauce, but that's about it.

name: Monica Smith
review: Roatan was lovely. We did a zip-line tour through the canopy. It was exhilarating and the views of the ocean from the top were spectacular. The equipment felt safe and the staff were very encouraging for those of us who were a bit nervous. In Costa Maya, we did the lagoon tour. It was peaceful, but honestly a bit boring compared to the zipline. Cozumel was our last stop, and we were pretty tired, so we just walked around the San Miguel plaza and had some coffee.

name: Victor P
review: Cozumel has great snorkeling at Money Bar. No entry fee, just buy a drink or some food. The reef is right there. Costa Maya is okay if you like pools and bars, but it lacks the culture of the other stops. Roatan felt the most 'authentic' to me, especially seeing the local fishing boats and the stilt houses. The contrast between the wealthy tourist areas and the local villages is very sharp there, which is something to be aware of.

name: Rachel_Travels
review: I really enjoyed the botanical gardens in Roatan. It was so lush and green, and we learned a lot about the local plants. Cozumel was a bit too much 'sell, sell, sell' for me. Every shop owner is trying to pull you in. Costa Maya was better in that regard, though the prices in the port shops are very high. We found much better deals on souvenirs in the little town of Mahahual just a short cab ride away.

name: Samuel L. Davis
review: The itinerary was decent, but the time in port felt short. In Cozumel, we barely had enough time to finish our lunch at a local cafe before we had to run back. Roatan was the highlight; the monkeys at the sanctuary were so fun to interact with. Costa Maya is a beautiful port, very well-maintained, but it feels a bit like a movie set rather than a real place in Mexico. Overall, a good trip, but I wish we had 2-3 more hours in each location to really explore.

name: Andre Wallace
review: Roatan ended up being the most memorable stop for us. We booked a snorkeling trip through a smaller local operator, and the water clarity was unreal. It didn’t feel overly commercial once we got away from the immediate port. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown in comparison. The port area felt like a controlled shopping zone, and we didn’t book an excursion, which I think was a mistake. Cozumel was easier to manage since taxis were straightforward, but it was definitely the busiest port of the three.

name: Linh Pham
review: In Costa Maya, we did a ruins tour that required an early start. The site itself was impressive and worth seeing, but the long bus ride made the day feel rushed overall. Roatan was more relaxed—we chose a beach break and stayed there the whole time without moving around. Cozumel felt crowded when we arrived, so we stayed closer to the port area and didn’t explore as much as we originally planned.

name: Trevor H.
review: Cozumel was probably my favorite because I like doing things independently. I rented a bike and rode along parts of the coast, which gave me a different perspective than just staying near the port. Roatan was nice, but our excursion had too many stops packed into a short time. Costa Maya didn’t offer much unless you had something booked ahead, so we ended up heading back early.

name: Daniela Ruiz
review: We tried to mix structured excursions with free time. In Roatan, we visited a wildlife park and also had some beach time, which made for a balanced day. Costa Maya felt more limited unless you committed to something like the ruins. Cozumel was flexible—you could just walk around or grab a taxi and find something easily. It was busy but manageable.

name: Caleb Morgan
review: Roatan was the standout for me because of the scenery and the snorkeling. It felt less commercial compared to the other stops once you got out of the port area. Costa Maya didn’t impress me much since we stayed near the port and it felt like a tourist hub. Cozumel was fine, but very crowded and a bit predictable.

name: Farah Siddiqui
review: I actually enjoyed Costa Maya more than I expected because we booked a small cooking class excursion away from the port. It felt more personal and less crowded. Roatan was beautiful but very humid, which made walking around a bit tiring. Cozumel was convenient, though it felt the most tourist-oriented of the three stops.

name: Nathan Pierce
review: We skipped Costa Maya entirely and stayed onboard that day because none of the excursions appealed to us. In Roatan, we did a zipline course that was fun but had long wait times between runs. Cozumel was simple—we took a taxi to a beach club and spent the afternoon there, which was probably the most relaxing part of the trip.

name: Elodie Martin
review: Costa Maya surprised me in a good way because we left the port area quickly and found a quieter beach. The port itself didn’t feel authentic. Roatan had lush scenery but also felt a bit chaotic when disembarking. Cozumel was organized but very crowded with multiple ships in port at the same time.

name: Marcus Lee
review: Roatan had the best natural environment in my opinion. We spent most of our time in the water, and it didn’t feel rushed. Costa Maya required more planning than we gave it, so we didn’t get much out of it. Cozumel had plenty to do, but it felt heavily geared toward tourists.

name: Amina Yusuf
review: In Cozumel, we did a snorkeling excursion that included multiple stops, but it felt overly structured and timed. Roatan was more flexible because we hired a private driver and created our own plan. Costa Maya was the only stop where we felt unsure what to do once we got off the ship.

name: Dylan Carter
review: We really enjoyed Roatan because we booked a smaller group excursion that combined snorkeling and sightseeing. It didn’t feel rushed, which was nice. Costa Maya was harder to navigate independently, so we ended up staying near the port longer than expected. Cozumel was easy to get around, even without a plan.

name: Yara Haddad
review: Costa Maya was my least favorite stop. The port area felt like a shopping complex, and we didn’t go further out. Roatan felt more natural and less crowded once we left the main area. Cozumel was busy but offered more flexibility in terms of activities.

name: Connor Fields
review: Roatan stood out because it felt less developed in certain areas. We did a guided excursion that included both a short hike and beach time, which worked well. Costa Maya didn’t offer much unless you booked something in advance. Cozumel had great water activities but was crowded.

name: Luciana Costa
review: We booked excursions at every port, and Costa Maya was the most educational with the ruins visit. However, it started early and felt like a long day. Roatan was more relaxed and enjoyable overall. Cozumel was easy to explore without a strict schedule.

name: Greg Foster
review: Cozumel was the only port where we didn’t plan anything, and it worked out fine. We just walked around and found things to do. Roatan required more planning to avoid crowded excursions. Costa Maya felt like it needed a structured activity to really enjoy it.

name: Nadia Petrova
review: Roatan felt like the most authentic destination on this itinerary, especially once we left the immediate port area and drove into smaller communities. We booked a local guide rather than a cruise-organized excursion, which made a big difference in how much we learned. Costa Maya was a mixed experience. The ruins we visited were fascinating, but the transportation logistics were frustrating, with delays leaving the port that cut into our time. Cozumel was efficient and easy to navigate, but also the most crowded. The snorkeling there was still excellent despite the number of people.

name: Evan Schultz
review: Costa Maya didn’t really stand out to me. We walked around, got a drink, and went back early. Roatan was better because we had a planned activity that kept us engaged. Cozumel was enjoyable just because it was easy to find something to do quickly.

name: Maya Kapoor
review: Roatan was the most memorable stop because we did a mix of snorkeling and sightseeing with a knowledgeable guide. Costa Maya felt like a place where you needed a longer excursion to get value, which we didn’t do. Cozumel was easy and relaxing, with plenty of options close to the port.

name: Hector Salinas
review: Cozumel felt very tourist-focused, but it also had the best infrastructure for visitors. Roatan required more planning, but the experience felt more rewarding. Costa Maya was the only stop where I felt like I didn’t use my time well.

name: Anika Bose
review: Across all three stops, I realized how important planning ahead is. In Roatan, we booked a nature-focused excursion that included wildlife and beach time, and it felt well paced. Costa Maya required more effort because many activities are farther from the port, and we underestimated that. Cozumel was the easiest to enjoy spontaneously but also the most crowded. I think the itinerary works best if you balance pre-booked excursions with some flexibility.

name: Elena_V
review: Cozumel was the highlight of this trip for our family. We booked a private catamaran through a local company instead of the ship's crowded tour. The water clarity near the Palancar Reef was unbelievable, and we saw several sea turtles. After snorkeling, the crew took us to a private beach area for a fresh ceviche lunch. My only complaint is the pier logistics at the International Pier; it is a very long walk from the ship to the taxi stand, which was difficult for my elderly mother. We also visited Costa Maya, where we mostly stayed in the port village. It’s very commercialized and feels like a giant outdoor mall, but the salt-water pool was a nice way to cool off for an hour before heading back to the ship. Roatan had beautiful scenery, but the traffic on the island is no joke. We nearly missed our all-aboard time because of a minor accident blocking the main road back to the port. Definitely give yourself extra time there.

name: Kwame Thompson
review: I'm a fairly experienced cruiser, but this was my first time in Roatan. We did the Gumbalimba Park excursion. Seeing the capuchin monkeys and macaws up close was incredible, though the walking trails are a bit steep and humid. Cozumel was a bit too busy for my liking with five ships in port at once. San Miguel was packed and the vendors are extremely aggressive, constantly shouting at you to look at silver jewelry. In Costa Maya, we took a taxi to Mahahual for $4 per person. It was much more relaxed than the cruise port itself, and we found a quiet spot on the beach to grab some beer and guacamole. The seaweed was a bit of an issue on the shore, but the local staff worked hard to clear it.

name: Yuki Tanaka
review: Costa Maya ruins at Chacchoben were majestic. It is a long bus ride, nearly an hour each way, but our guide was very knowledgeable about Mayan history. I wish we had more than two hours at the actual site because it felt a bit rushed. In Roatan, we just went to West Bay Beach. The snorkeling right off the shore is some of the best I have ever seen. You don't even need a boat tour; just swim out about 30 yards and the reef is right there. Cozumel felt very touristy so we just walked around the pier area for some duty-free shopping and came back early.
