name: DeAndre Hills
review: Avoid the 'all-inclusive' beach breaks in Cozumel if you want a quiet day. We went to one that was wall-to-wall people and the music was so loud we couldn't even talk. It felt like a spring break party. On the other hand, Roatan was very peaceful. We hired a private driver named Ronny who took us all over the island, including some local spots away from the tourists. He took us to a small hilltop restaurant with a view of the whole harbor. Costa Maya was okay, but the port feels very fake. It's basically a maze designed to make you walk past every single shop before you can reach the exit to the street.

name: Lars_78
review: First time in Mexico and Honduras. Roatan was my favorite because of the lush green hills. We did a zip-line tour through the jungle which was thrilling, although the safety briefing was a bit disorganized. Cozumel was just for shopping. We found some nice vanilla and hot sauce at a supermarket downtown called Mega, which was way cheaper than the port shops. In Costa Maya, we stayed on the boat. We walked to the end of the pier, saw the crowds, and turned right back around. The ship was nice and empty while everyone else was fighting for a spot at the port pool.

name: Ayesha Khan
review: The timing in Cozumel was frustrating. We didn't dock until 1:00 PM, which meant most of the good morning snorkeling tours were already done. We ended up just taking a taxi to the town square. Roatan was much better scheduled. We had a full day and spent it at Little French Key. It’s a bit expensive but very beautiful and secluded. The lunch included was authentic island food—rice and beans with jerk chicken. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown. The 'Tequila Tasting' we did was mostly just a sales pitch to buy expensive bottles. I've had better experiences in other Caribbean ports.

name: Mateo Rossi
review: Costa Maya's port is a tourist trap but Mahahual is a gem. Rent a golf cart! It's the best way to see the coastline at your own pace. We found a small bar where we sat with our feet in the water for three hours. In Cozumel, we did the Atlantis Submarine. It was pricey but seeing a shipwreck at 100 feet deep was a once-in-a-lifetime thing. Roatan was beautiful but the insects are vicious. Even with bug spray, I came back with dozens of bites after our nature walk. Don't forget the DEET if you go into the woods there.

name: S_Patel
review: Roatan snorkeling at Blue Channel was amazing. The coral was very healthy and there were schools of blue tangs everywhere. Cozumel was a bit disappointing as our excursion to the cenotes was cancelled due to heavy rain. We ended up just sitting in a bar at the pier. Costa Maya was a surprise hit; we did a chocolate-making workshop that was very hands-on. We ground the beans ourselves and learned about the history of cacao in the region. It was educational and delicious. I wish we had more than six hours in Costa Maya, though, as we had to rush back for embarkation.

name: Brenda_CruiseGal
review: I've been to Cozumel ten times and it's getting too crowded. We tried a new beach club on the north side to escape the crowds, but even that was packed with people from three different ships. Roatan is still the best part of this itinerary. The people are so friendly and the island isn't as overdeveloped yet. We went to the West End and just hung out at a dive shop. Costa Maya is only worth it if you go to the ruins. If you stay in the port, you're just spending money on overpriced margaritas. The walk down the pier in Costa Maya is also incredibly long in the heat.

name: Dimitri_V
review: Cozumel jeep tour was a blast. We drove to the rugged east side of the island where there's no electricity and just crashing waves. It’s so much better than the touristy shops. Costa Maya felt like a mall, but the dolphin encounter was decent for the kids. Roatan was a bit of a logistics nightmare for us. The bus for our excursion was late, which cut our beach time in half. The island is beautiful but seems to struggle with the volume of tourists when the big ships are in. I'd go back to Roatan on a land vacation though.

name: Sonia Gupta
review: Roatan's Mahogany Bay is convenient because the ship docks right at the beach, but it's very artificial. If you want the real Honduras, you have to leave the gated area. We took a taxi to the East End and the scenery was breathtaking. Cozumel was great for a relaxing day at the Money Bar; great food and easy water access. In Costa Maya, we did the Bacalar Lagoon excursion. It is a very long drive, nearly 90 minutes each way, but the 'Lake of Seven Colors' is real. The water is stunningly blue. Just be prepared for a lot of time on a bus.

name: Niels_J
review: I was looking for history and found it in Costa Maya. The Chacchoben ruins are very impressive and you can still climb some of the smaller structures. Our guide was excellent. Cozumel was too commercial for me. It felt like every square inch was designed to sell me a t-shirt or a diamond ring. Roatan was better, but the pier at Coxen Hole is very cramped and chaotic. We did the sloth encounter which was cute, but the tour felt a bit like an assembly line moving people through as fast as possible. I would have liked more time to just sit and enjoy the jungle.

name: Rohan M
review: Cozumel snorkeling is still top-tier. We went to Columbia Reef and the visibility was at least 80 feet. We saw a nurse shark and a massive stingray. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown for us because we didn't book an excursion and there isn't much to do within walking distance if you don't want to sit at a loud pool. Roatan was lovely; we did a glass-bottom boat because my wife doesn't like to swim. She loved seeing the fish without getting wet. The port area in Roatan is actually quite nice for shopping compared to the others.

name: Tabitha_Cruise
review: Costa Maya had way too much seaweed on the beach in Mahahual. It smelled pretty bad so we didn't stay long. We went back to the port and used the pool there, which was crowded but clean. Roatan was the highlight. We went to a place called Maya Key which is just a short boat ride from the pier. It has a nice beach, a small zoo, and some replica ruins. It was very convenient and didn't feel as rushed as other excursions. Cozumel was fine, just did some basic shopping for silver and leather goods. The prices are better if you walk a few blocks away from the waterfront.

name: Carlos Eduardo
review: Great diving in Roatan! The reef is in much better shape than Cozumel. The dive masters were very professional and the equipment was high quality. Cozumel felt a bit 'tired'—the same shops and the same tours I saw five years ago. Costa Maya is growing fast, but the infrastructure isn't quite there. The line for taxis back to the port was nearly 40 minutes long in the blazing sun. They need more organization for the independent travelers. Still, the beach at Mahahual is beautiful if you find a spot without the sea grass.

name: Jin_Woo
review: In Cozumel, we did the chocolate and tequila tour. It was okay, a bit touristy, but the lunch was decent. Roatan was the best for nature. We did the monkey sanctuary and it was the highlight of the whole cruise for my daughter. Costa Maya was our least favorite stop. It felt very manufactured and expensive. $15 for a margarita in Mexico feels like a rip-off. We stayed for an hour and then went back to the ship to use the gym. The itinerary overall was good, but the ports are getting very crowded with the new mega-ships.

name: Helena G
review: Roatan is a beautiful island but the poverty is very visible once you leave the tourist areas, which can be sobering. We did a tour of the local orphanage which was very moving. Cozumel was a total contrast—very polished and wealthy-feeling. We spent the day at a high-end beach club with excellent service. Costa Maya was somewhere in the middle. The ruins are a must-see, but the port itself is a bit of a nightmare if you don't like crowds and loud music. I would suggest booking a private tour in every port to get away from the cruise ship herds.

name: Xavier_B
review: I'm a solo traveler and found Cozumel very easy to navigate. I just rented a scooter and rode around the island. The east side is deserted and beautiful. Roatan was a bit harder to do alone as the taxis are expensive for one person. I ended up joining a group for a snorkeling trip. Costa Maya is impossible to enjoy alone without spending a lot of money on a taxi or a tour. The port area is fine but everything is priced for tourists. I'd recommend Cozumel for anyone who likes to explore independently.

name: Leila Mansour
review: The beach in Costa Maya was very disappointing due to the algae. It wasn't the white sand paradise I expected. However, the Mayan ruins tour was spectacular and made up for it. Roatan was much more like the postcard—clear water and white sand at West Bay. We had some issues with the local vendors in Roatan being very pushy, but once we got in the water, it was pure bliss. Cozumel was our last stop and we just used it to buy some duty-free items. The port is very efficient but lacks character. It feels like an airport terminal.

name: Tunde_Travels
review: Roatan was incredible. We did a deep-sea fishing charter and caught two mahi-mahi. The crew was local and knew exactly where to go. Cozumel was also fun; we did the electric bike tour around the town. It was a great way to see the backstreets and local murals. Costa Maya was our only bad experience. Our tour bus broke down on the way back from the ruins, and we were stuck on the side of the road for an hour in 95-degree heat. The cruise line held the ship for us, but it was a very stressful end to the day. I'll stick to shorter excursions next time.

name: D_Chen82
review: Roatan was the highlight for us. We booked a private driver named Victor who took us all around the island. The stop at the monkey and sloth sanctuary was great for the kids, but the real winner was West Bay Beach. The water was crystal clear, though the vendors there are extremely persistent—you have to say 'no' about twenty times while trying to relax. Cozumel was a bit of a letdown this time. We’ve been there three times now and it just feels like one giant shopping mall once you get off the ship. We tried to walk to a local spot for lunch, but the humidity was brutal and the sidewalks are so uneven. Costa Maya was interesting, we did the Chacchoben ruins. It’s a bit of a hike to get out there, nearly an hour on a bus that had questionable air conditioning, but seeing the structures peaking out from the jungle was worth the sweat. I wish we had more than six hours in Costa Maya though, because by the time we got back from the ruins, we had about twenty minutes to shop before all-aboard.

name: Haley Rogers
review: Cozumel was fantastic for snorkeling! We headed over to Money Bar. It's an easy taxi ride from the pier and the entry is free if you eat or drink there. The gear rental was cheap and the reef right off the shore is surprisingly healthy. Lots of parrotfish and even saw a small ray. Roatan was also nice, but the pier area is a bit chaotic with everyone trying to sell you tours. We ended up just taking a water taxi to West End which was much more chill than the main cruise beach. Costa Maya was our 'stay on the ship' day. The port area there feels very artificial, like a theme park version of Mexico. We did walk out for half an hour but it was just rows of the same jewelry and souvenir shops you see at every other Caribbean stop. If you don't book a specific excursion to the ruins or a private beach club, there isn't much 'real' culture to see within walking distance of the boat.

name: Kumar_Travels
review: Did the salsa and salsa excursion in Cozumel. It was okay, a bit cheesy but the margaritas were strong. Roatan was better for nature lovers. We did the glass bottom boat because my wife doesn't swim, and she loved seeing the coral. Costa Maya was a mess with three ships in port. Way too crowded.

name: Jared Thompson
review: Our time in Costa Maya was spent at Maya Chan. I'd read reviews saying it was the best way to escape the port crowds and they weren't lying. The road to get there is basically a series of massive potholes, so be prepared for a bumpy ride in the back of a taxi. Once you arrive, it's paradise. Open bar, great food, and they give you your own little designated area. In Cozumel, we took the ferry over to Playa del Carmen for a mainland tour, which was a mistake. The ferry ride was so rough that half the passengers were sick, and it ate up four hours of our day just traveling back and forth. Next time I'm staying on the island. Roatan was beautiful from the ship but we had a bit of a logistics issue. Our pre-booked tour operator was nowhere to be found at the gate. We ended up haggling with a local driver which worked out fine, but it was a stressful start to the morning. The island greenery is stunning though.

name: Lashonda W.
review: Roatan snorkeling is 10/10. We went to Maya Key. Private island feel, very clean, and the rescue animals on site were a bonus. Avoid the 'free' shopping shuttles in Cozumel, they just take you to specific stores where they get a kickback. Just walk or take a regular cab.

name: Terrence Boomer
review: This was our first time on this itinerary. Cozumel is definitely the place for shoppers. We spent most of our time at Los Cincos Soles getting vanilla and ceramics. We did have a bit of a scare with the time; the traffic near the downtown area can get backed up, so give yourself plenty of time to get back to the ship. In Costa Maya, we did the Maya Healing Ritual excursion. It was a bit 'touristy' but the shaman was very kind and the lunch they served afterward was the best meal we had off the ship. Traditional pork cooked in the ground. Roatan was our favorite for scenery. The drive across the island offers some incredible views of the ocean from the hills. We didn't do a beach day there, just a sightseeing tour. My only complaint is that the port in Roatan felt very pushy. You can't walk five feet without someone offering a braid or a massage. It makes it hard to just enjoy the atmosphere. But the island itself is lush and gorgeous.

name: Yuki Tanaka
review: Cozumel was okay. We did a jeep tour. It was dusty and the jeeps were old, but getting to the 'wild' side of the island where there's no electricity was cool. The waves on that side are huge, so no swimming, but the photos were great. Roatan was better. We just walked around the port and then found a small local cafe for baleadas. Much cheaper than the ship food! Costa Maya was disappointing because our excursion to the lagoon was canceled due to rain, and the port area gets very slippery when wet. We just went back to our cabin and watched a movie.

name: Greg P.
review: Not a fan of Costa Maya. It's basically a mall built for cruisers. If you don't leave the port area, you haven't seen Mexico. We took a taxi to Mahahual for $4 a person and it was much better. Just a small fishing village with beach chairs and buckets of beer. Cozumel was better for diving. I booked a two-tank dive through a local shop instead of the cruise line. Saved about $60 and it was a small group of four people instead of twenty. The wall dives there are world-class. Roatan was where we did the zipline. The views of the jungle floor are incredible, but man, the mosquitoes are something else. Even with DEET, I got bit. Make sure you bring the strong stuff if you're going into the trees. Overall, the itinerary is good but the times in port are a bit short. Eight hours in Cozumel is enough, but six hours in Roatan felt rushed, especially with the 45-minute drive back from the beach.

name: Malik J.
review: Roatan is the goat. Sloths were cute and the ziplining was high energy. Cozumel felt like a giant pharmacy/jewelry store. Costa Maya was okay but the walk from the ship to the exit is like a mile long. Not great if you have mobility issues.

name: Linda_and_Bob
review: We are experienced cruisers so we usually skip the big bus tours. In Cozumel, we rented a car for the day. It's very easy to navigate the island as it's basically one big loop. We found a secluded beach on the east side and had it all to ourselves for two hours. Just watch out for the moped renters; they drive a bit crazy. In Costa Maya, we actually stayed in the port and used the pool. It was crowded but convenient since we didn't want to deal with a long day. Roatan was a bit of a struggle. We tried to do a walking tour but the hills are very steep and there aren't many sidewalks once you leave the immediate port area. We ended up just grabbing a taxi back. If you go to Roatan, definitely plan for transportation. Don't think you can just wander around on foot like you can in some parts of Cozumel. The port schedule was okay, but I wish we had stayed later in Cozumel to see the sunset from the shore.

name: S_Gupta_99
review: Costa Maya was beautiful but the bus ride to the ruins is no joke. It's long and boring. Once you get there, the history is fascinating, but be prepared for the heat. There is almost no shade at the site. Roatan was much more relaxing. We went to Daniel Johnson’s Monkey and Sloth Hangout. It was well organized and the animals seemed very well cared for. For Cozumel, we just did a little shopping and had some guacamole at a spot near the plaza. The port was very busy with four ships, so everything took longer than expected. Security lines to get back on the ship were quite long in Cozumel compared to the other two stops.

name: Zoe Miller
review: Cozumel was where we did the Amazing Race excursion. It was a fun way to see the city and interact with locals while solving puzzles. A bit exhausting in the heat though! In Roatan, we just wanted a beach day so we went to Tabyana. The sand was white and the water was calm, perfect for just floating around. Costa Maya was our least favorite. The pier is very long and if you're at the end of it, it's a trek. The port shops are overpriced. We tried to find a local market but everything nearby is clearly just for tourists. I'd recommend a proper excursion there next time.

name: Bernice Higgins
review: The ports were nice but I felt like the excursions were overpriced for what you got. In Roatan, we paid for a 'private beach' that turned out to be packed with people from three different cruise lines. The buffet lunch was cold chicken and rice. Cozumel was better because we just walked to a local pharmacy and then a grocery store to buy real Mexican vanilla and candy. Much better prices than the 'official' shops. Costa Maya was fine, we just sat by the port pool. It’s free, but the drinks are expensive. The time in port was enough for us, honestly, we were ready to get back to the AC by 2 PM.

name: Mateo S.
review: Cozumel is great for the bars. Margaritaville is right there but go a few blocks in for better prices. Costa Maya is a bit of a trap. Roatan is beautiful, very green and lush compared to the others.

name: Kim_Family_Fun
review: We did the glass bottom boat in Cozumel with our two young children. It was great because they could see the fish without having to swim in the deep water. The guide was very knowledgeable about the different types of coral. In Roatan, we did a similar thing but with a semi-submarine. Roatan definitely had more colorful fish. Costa Maya was a bit harder with kids. The walk from the ship is long and there isn't much for them to do in the port unless you pay for the dolphin encounter, which we didn't want to do. We ended up just getting some ice cream and heading back early. If you have little ones, Roatan is definitely the best stop of the three for activities they can actually participate in.

name: Arthur Pendergast
review: The port in Costa Maya is a bit of a maze. They force you to walk through all the shops before you can exit. It’s annoying. Once we got out, we took a taxi to the lighthouse in Mahahual. Very peaceful. Cozumel was much more commercialized. We did the chocolate factory tour there which was interesting and tasty. Roatan was the most natural-looking island. We took a tour to the east end of the island, which is much less developed. It was great to see how the locals actually live away from the cruise terminals. The scheduling was okay, but we left Roatan at 3 PM which felt very early. I would have liked another two hours there to have dinner on the shore.

name: J_Russo
review: Cozumel was cool, we did the underwater oxygen helmet walk. Very weird feeling but seeing the fish right at your face was awesome. Costa Maya we stayed on the ship because it looked too crowded. Roatan we did a park with birds and monkeys. Good day.

name: Nadia B.
review: I was really looking forward to Roatan and it didn't disappoint. The snorkeling at West Bay is some of the best I've ever done. You just swim out about 20 yards and you're in a forest of coral. Cozumel was a bit too busy for me. There were five ships in port that day and the downtown area was just a wall of people. It made it hard to enjoy the local architecture. Costa Maya was okay, but very windy. We tried to go on a boat tour but it was a bit rough. The ruins excursion seemed to be the most popular choice there, and everyone we talked to who did it loved it. I'll probably try that if I ever go back on this itinerary.

name: Dmitry Volkov
review: Everything was good but the port times were short. In Cozumel, we barely had time to eat after our excursion. Roatan is the most beautiful island. Very green and the water is perfect. Costa Maya is just for shopping.

name: Carly_Sunshine
review: We had a blast in Cozumel doing the electric bike tour. It takes you along the coast away from the main town and you get to see some of the smaller ruins on the island. It’s much cooler than sitting on a bus. Roatan was also great; we did the 'Little French Key' day trip. It was a bit pricey but it was so peaceful and the lunch was delicious—fresh lobster and coconut rice. Costa Maya was the only 'meh' day. We did the Bacalar Lagoon tour, which was a very long drive. The lagoon is stunning, the 'seven colors of blue' are real, but I spent more time on a bus than I did in the water. If you don't mind long travel times, it's worth it, but if you want to relax, maybe pick something closer to the pier. Overall, a great mix of activities across the three stops. I felt like I got a good taste of both Mexico and Honduras.

name: D_Chen92
review: Roatan was the highlight for us. We booked a private driver through a local company called Victor’s and they took us to the monkey sanctuary and then over to West Bay beach. The monkeys were hilarious and definitely worth the visit, though it felt a bit rushed because the driver wanted us back before the afternoon traffic hit. I will say, the roads in Roatan are pretty rough so be prepared for a bumpy ride. Cozumel was a bit of a letdown in comparison because we just stayed in the port area and it felt like one giant shopping mall designed to take your money. We did end up grabbing some tacos at a small place about two blocks away from the main pier which were decent, but Costa Maya was even more of a 'fake' feeling port. If you don't book an actual excursion to the ruins in Costa Maya, there really isn't much to do except sit by the pool with hundreds of other people from the ship. Next time we will definitely book a trip to the Chacchoben ruins because we heard great things from other passengers at dinner.

name: Ellie Rodriguez
review: Costa Maya was beautiful but we felt very pressured by the vendors as soon as we stepped off the ship. We ended up taking a taxi to Mahahual for $4 per person. It was much more relaxed once we got away from the main cruise terminal. The beach there is stunning but the seagrass was a bit heavy the day we visited. Cozumel was much better for snorkeling; we went to Money Bar and just swam out from the shore. You don't even need a boat there to see some amazing fish. Roatan was okay, but we found the port area quite confusing to navigate and the excursion we had booked was cancelled at the last minute, so we just ended up walking around the local shops which wasn't very exciting.

name: Gary_W_1974
review: Did the catamaran sail and snorkel in Cozumel. The crew was fantastic and kept the drinks flowing, though the snorkeling spot was a bit crowded with other tours. We still saw a stingray and plenty of colorful fish. In Roatan, we just went to the beach at Tabyana. It's a gorgeous stretch of sand but definitely packed with cruise passengers. If you want peace and quiet, this isn't it. Costa Maya was our 'stay on the ship' day. We walked through the port shops for 20 minutes and decided it wasn't worth the heat. It just feels very manufactured.

name: Ayesha Khan
review: Our trip to the Chacchoben ruins from Costa Maya was incredible. Our guide, Ivan, was so knowledgeable about Mayan history and made the long bus ride fly by with stories. The ruins themselves are breathtaking, especially since you can still see where the jungle is reclaiming some of the structures. It was a bit hot and humid, so bring plenty of water. In Cozumel, we did a chocolate making workshop which was fun for the kids, but I felt it was a bit overpriced for what it was. We skipped Roatan entirely because the weather looked shaky and we wanted to enjoy the quiet ship.

name: Terrence Miller
review: Cozumel was great for a bar crawl. We hit three different spots downtown. Roatan was better for the scenery, especially the lush green hills. We didn't do an excursion in Costa Maya and honestly regretted it. The port is just a tourist trap.

name: K-Smith_Travels
review: Roatan's West End is a must-see! We took a water taxi from West Bay and it was the best $5 we spent. It felt much more authentic than the cruise port area. We had a great lunch at a little hole-in-the-wall place overlooking the water. In Cozumel, we went to Paradise Beach. It was $65 for the all-inclusive pass which felt worth it for the pool and the service, but the ocean was a bit rocky. Costa Maya was the only disappointment. We tried to find a local craft market but it's mostly just mass-produced souvenirs. The dolphin encounter at the port looked sad to me, so we didn't stay long.

name: Hiroshi Tanaka
review: The time in Cozumel felt way too short. We did the Jeep tour to the eastern side of the island which is rugged and beautiful, but we had to rush back to make the 4:30 all-aboard. I wish we had more time to explore the lighthouse area. Roatan was much more laid back. We did a mangrove tour in a small boat and it was so peaceful. The guide pointed out various birds and even a small crocodile. Costa Maya was just okay—we went to a beach club called Blue Bayou. The food was excellent but the service was a bit slow, probably because there were three ships in port that day.

name: Linda G.
review: Loved Roatan! We went to the sloth sanctuary and it was the highlight of my whole year. Holding a sloth is a memory I will cherish forever. Cozumel was fine, just very busy. We did some shopping and bought some vanilla.

name: Desmond_606
review: For Costa Maya, I highly recommend Maya Chan Beach Resort. It's a bit of a bumpy 30-minute ride in a shuttle, but it's limited to a small number of guests so it's very private. The food was some of the best I've had in Mexico—fresh fish and amazing guacamole. In Cozumel, we went to the San Gervasio ruins. They aren't as impressive as the ones on the mainland, but it's a nice way to spend a few hours if you like history. Roatan was beautiful but the zip-lining excursion we saw looked a bit sketchy so we stuck to the glass-bottom boat tour. It was decent, saw a few turtles and a lot of fan coral.

name: Priya_Chopra
review: Cozumel is a diver's paradise. I did a two-tank dive at Palancar Reef and the visibility was stunning, easily 100 feet. The coral formations are huge. My husband stayed on the ship that day but joined me in Roatan for the beach. Roatan has a lot of charm but the local infrastructure is clearly struggling with the amount of tourists. Costa Maya was just a shopping day for us. We found some nice silver jewelry in one of the shops near the back of the port, though you have to haggle quite a bit to get a fair price. Overall a good mix of ports.

name: S_Venkatesh
review: Costa Maya ruins were worth the drive. Very hot though! Cozumel was too crowded with 5 ships in port, we just stayed near the pier. Roatan snorkeling was the best of the three stops.

name: Jackson_Family_Trip
review: We did the clear kayak tour in Cozumel and it was a neat perspective, though the wind made it a bit of a workout! We saw some small rays through the bottom of the boat. Roatan was our favorite because of the sloths and monkeys. The kids haven't stopped talking about it. The guide was very patient with our youngest. Costa Maya was a bit of a letdown—we just stayed in the port pool because the kids were tired. It's a nice pool, but it gets very crowded and the music is quite loud. If you're looking for a quiet day, stay on the ship in Costa Maya or head far away from the pier.

name: Bernadette Lynch
review: In Roatan, we rented a private boat for the day to go snorkeling away from the crowds. It was pricey but worth every penny to have the reef to ourselves. The water is crystal clear. Cozumel was a bit of a mess—transportation was disorganized and we waited 40 minutes for our excursion bus to even show up. Once we got to the beach club it was fine, but the delay took a big chunk out of our day. Costa Maya was a pleasant surprise. We did a cooking class where we learned to make authentic salsa and tamales. The chef was hilarious and the food was incredible. Highly recommend that for something different.

name: Mateo_Cruises
review: The port in Costa Maya is basically a theme park. It's fun for an hour but then you realize everything is priced in US dollars and is twice as expensive as it should be. We took a taxi to the local village and enjoyed a much cheaper beer on the beach. Cozumel is much the same unless you get out of the downtown area. We rented a car in Cozumel and drove to the 'wild side'—no electricity, just waves and small beach bars. It was spectacular. Roatan was the most natural feeling of the three. We did a botanical garden tour that was very educational and saw some beautiful tropical flowers.

name: dflowers_82
review: Snorkeling in Roatan is top tier. The reef is so close to the shore at West Bay you don't even need a boat. Cozumel felt very commercialized. Costa Maya ruins were cool but the bus had no AC.

name: Clarence Wiggs
review: I've been to Cozumel many times, so we decided to try something new and did the underground river (cenote) excursion on the mainland. Be warned: it's a long day with a ferry ride that can be very rough if the seas are high. Half our group got sick on the boat. The cenotes were beautiful, but I'm not sure the 2-hour travel time each way is worth it. Next time I'll stay on the island. In Roatan, we just walked to the beach at the end of the pier area. It was clean and the water was warm, perfect for a lazy afternoon. Costa Maya was just a quick stop for us to buy some vanilla and coffee.

name: SunSeeker_99
review: Costa Maya was great for the kids because of the port pool and the chocolate shop. Cozumel is where we did our big excursion—the dune buggies. It was dusty and dirty but so much fun! We drove through some muddy trails and ended up at a nice beach for lunch. Roatan was a bit slower paced for us. We did a scenic bus tour around the island. The views from the highest point are incredible, you can see the reef surrounding the whole island. Our guide told us a lot about the history of the island and the different cultures there. It was a very relaxing day compared to the others.

name: Lars_Johanssen
review: Roatan has the best diving I've seen in the Caribbean. The walls are covered in life. Costa Maya is skip-able if you aren't doing the ruins. Cozumel has great shopping but the pier is a long walk.

name: M_Thompson_Adventures
review: We did the Amazing Race excursion in Cozumel and it was a blast! You have to solve clues and run around the downtown area. It's a great way to see the city and interact with locals, though you'll be exhausted by the end. Roatan was much more about nature. We visited the Iguana farm which was... interesting. There are hundreds of them everywhere! It’s a bit weird but definitely unique. Costa Maya was a bit of a 'meh' day. We did the fly fishing excursion but didn't catch anything. The guide tried hard, but the fish just weren't biting that day. Still, it was nice to be out on the water away from the ship crowds.

name: Becky_Cruiser
review: Costa Maya's port is actually quite pretty with the flamingos and the birds near the entrance. It's a bit of a maze to get through the shops though. We went to a small beach club called Tropicante in Mahahual and had a wonderful day. The massages on the beach were only $25 for an hour! In Cozumel, we just did some souvenir shopping and had lunch at Pancho's Backyard. The margaritas are huge and very strong! Roatan was a bit rainy when we were there, so we didn't get to do our snorkeling tour. We ended up just staying in the port area and getting some local coffee, which was actually quite good. Wish we had better weather for Honduras.

name: Devon Alvarez
review: Roatan was the clear highlight of this itinerary for me. We booked a half-day snorkeling trip and the reef conditions were way better than expected, with calm water and lots of fish near the coral edges. Getting in and out of the port was a bit crowded but manageable. In Costa Maya, we mostly stayed within the port area since we didn’t pre-book anything, and it felt more like a shopping village than a destination experience. Cozumel was the easiest to navigate independently; we took a taxi into town and just walked around the waterfront and central plaza. It felt safe and straightforward, though very busy with other cruise passengers. Overall, Roatan felt the most authentic, while the other two were more about convenience and short exploration windows.

name: Lila Johnson
review: We did a guided wildlife and beach excursion in Roatan, which ended up being the best-planned part of the cruise. Seeing the animals up close was memorable and the beach stop afterward was relaxing. Costa Maya felt more restricted since most activities required longer transport times, so we stayed near the port pools and shops instead. In Cozumel, we joined a small group city tour that included local markets and a short coastal drive, which helped us see more than just the tourist strip. I think Roatan had the strongest excursion options, while Cozumel was best for casual exploring. Costa Maya seemed like it needed either more time or a clearer plan to really enjoy it.

name: Mateo Rivera
review: Cozumel stood out because we rented scooters and explored beyond the main port area, which made the day feel more flexible. Roatan was more structured for us, we joined a pre-arranged island tour that included viewpoints and a beach stop, but the timing felt a little rushed. Costa Maya was the most limited experience since we didn’t book an excursion and mostly stayed inside the port complex. It was clean and organized but didn’t feel like we actually saw much of the region. If I did this itinerary again, I’d prioritize booking something in Costa Maya ahead of time and allow more independent exploration in Cozumel.

name: Keisha Thompson
review: Roatan felt very laid back compared to the other ports, and we opted for a beach day instead of a structured excursion. The water was clear and not too crowded. Costa Maya was more commercial than expected, and without an excursion it didn’t offer much beyond shops and a pool area. In Cozumel, we did a short snorkeling trip that was decent but quite busy with other groups in the water. The port itself was easy to get around, but the sheer number of visitors made it feel less relaxed. I appreciated having variety across the three stops, even if not all of them felt equally engaging.

name: Bruno Almeida
review: Roatan was the only stop where we felt like we actually experienced the island beyond the cruise infrastructure. We joined a small island drive and stopped at a viewpoint that overlooked the coast. Costa Maya was very organized but felt contained, almost like everything was built around cruise passengers. We didn’t venture far. Cozumel was the most active day for us since we walked around town and visited a couple of local shops and cafes. It was easy to move around but also quite crowded with other cruise guests. The itinerary overall was fine, but Roatan clearly had the most memorable off-ship experience.

name: Nia Okoye
review: In Roatan, we booked a snorkeling and beach combo and the water conditions were excellent, with good visibility. Costa Maya was more of a casual stop for us since we didn’t plan anything ahead, so we just stayed in the port area and relaxed. Cozumel felt like the busiest of the three ports, but we managed to find a quieter spot away from the main shopping streets and spent time just walking along the waterfront. Each stop had a very different vibe, and I think Roatan was best for activities, while Cozumel worked better for independent wandering.

name: Caleb Winters
review: Roatan had a solid snorkeling excursion, though the logistics getting from ship to boat took longer than expected. Costa Maya was disappointing for us since we didn’t realize how much of it depends on booking something in advance; we ended up just staying in the port complex. Cozumel was more flexible, and we took a taxi to a beach area that wasn’t part of an organized tour, which worked out better than expected. I think this itinerary rewards people who plan ahead, especially for Costa Maya and Roatan, where independent wandering is more limited.

name: Anika Shah
review: We chose a cultural tour in Roatan that included small towns and scenic stops, which gave a better sense of the island beyond the port. Costa Maya was more relaxed for us; we didn’t book anything and spent time walking around the port shops and pool area. In Cozumel, we joined a short guided walking tour that covered local streets and a small market area, which was interesting but very hot and crowded. I think Roatan had the most balanced experience between activity and scenery, while Costa Maya was more passive unless you plan something specific.

name: Hugo Martinez
review: Cozumel was the easiest port to explore on our own, and we spent most of the time just walking around and stopping at small shops near the waterfront. Roatan was more excursion-based for us, and we did a zipline and beach combination that was fun but slightly rushed. Costa Maya didn’t offer much for us without an excursion, so we stayed within the port zone. It was clean and safe but didn’t feel like we experienced the destination itself. I’d say Cozumel works best for casual exploring while Roatan is better for planned activities.

name: Tessa Caldwell
review: Roatan was a nice balance of activity and relaxation since we did a short snorkeling trip followed by beach time. Costa Maya felt more limited because we didn’t book an excursion and ended up just browsing shops. Cozumel was more lively and had a lot going on near the port, but it also felt the most crowded. We walked into town briefly but didn’t stay long because of the heat and traffic. Overall, Roatan felt the most enjoyable in terms of actual experience, while the other ports were more situational depending on what you booked.

name: Imran Qureshi
review: We did a guided reef snorkeling tour in Roatan that was well organized and not too rushed. Costa Maya was a short stop for us since we stayed within the port complex and didn’t go on any excursions. Cozumel was more flexible, and we took a taxi to explore a quieter beach area rather than staying near the main cruise crowd. Each port had a different level of accessibility, with Roatan feeling more excursion-dependent and Cozumel allowing more independent movement. Costa Maya was the least engaging without pre-planning.

name: Bailey Foster
review: Roatan was the most memorable stop because we did a mix of sightseeing and beach time, which felt well paced. Costa Maya was more about walking around the port area since we didn’t book anything in advance, and it felt somewhat limited. Cozumel was very busy, but we still managed to find a local café and walk around the central area. The port itself is easy to navigate but crowded. I think Roatan offered the best overall experience if you’re looking for variety in one day.

name: Darius Bennett
review: Roatan was great for snorkeling, though the boat transfer took longer than I expected. Costa Maya we skipped excursions entirely and just stayed in the port area, which was fine but not very memorable. Cozumel was more active; we walked around town and did some light shopping before heading back early due to crowds.

name: Mei Chen
review: Costa Maya ended up being our most relaxed stop since we didn’t plan anything and just stayed near the port pools and shops. Roatan was more structured with a beach excursion that was enjoyable but a bit time-limited. Cozumel felt very busy, and we mostly just walked a few blocks from the port before heading back.

name: Sofia Delgado
review: Roatan was the highlight with clear water and a well-run snorkeling trip. Costa Maya didn’t offer much for us without booking ahead, so we stayed local to the port. Cozumel was easier to explore independently, and we spent time walking and browsing shops near the waterfront.

name: Owen Gallagher
review: In Roatan we did a quick island tour that gave us some nice viewpoints. Costa Maya felt like a stop where excursions really matter because the port itself is limited. Cozumel was more flexible, and we just walked around the town area before heading back early due to crowds.

name: Zahra El-Sayed
review: Roatan felt more natural and less built up than the other ports, especially during our beach stop. Costa Maya was very structured and we didn’t venture beyond the main area. Cozumel had more energy but also more crowds, which made it harder to relax while walking around.

name: Lucas Pereira
review: This itinerary really depends on how much planning you do ahead of time. In Roatan, we booked a private driver instead of a large group excursion, which made a huge difference. We were able to stop at a couple of scenic overlooks, a small beach stretch, and even a local area where it felt like everyday life was still going on outside the tourism zones. That flexibility made Roatan feel the most authentic of the three stops. Costa Maya, however, felt very controlled in comparison. We didn’t book an excursion there and ended up staying inside the port complex longer than planned. It was clean and organized, but it didn’t feel like we saw anything beyond the immediate cruise area. Cozumel was somewhere in the middle for us. We walked into town and explored a few streets, but the heat and crowds made it less comfortable to linger. Transportation back to the ship was easy, but the overall experience felt more tourist-heavy than immersive. If I were to redo this itinerary, I’d prioritize pre-booking something meaningful in Costa Maya and allow more structured exploration in Roatan, because the contrast between planned and unplanned days really shaped how each port felt.

name: Harper Quinn
review: Roatan was enjoyable for a simple beach excursion we booked last minute. Costa Maya felt very commercial and we mostly stayed in the port area. Cozumel was crowded but easy to walk around and navigate on our own.

name: Elijah Morgan
review: We did a snorkeling trip in Roatan that was the best part of the cruise stops. Costa Maya didn’t offer much without an excursion so we stayed near the port. Cozumel was lively, and we walked around town before heading back early due to the heat.

name: Evan Morales
review: Roatan ended up being the most memorable stop for us because we booked a small snorkeling excursion that took us out to a calmer reef area. The water visibility was surprisingly good and the guide kept things organized so we weren’t rushed. Costa Maya felt more like a controlled shopping area unless you had a pre-booked tour, so we mostly just walked around the port complex there. In Cozumel, we grabbed a taxi into town and wandered the central square and waterfront, which was easy but very crowded with other cruise groups. I’d say Roatan had the best balance of activity and time, while the other two felt more dependent on planning ahead.

name: Nadia Karim
review: We did a wildlife-focused tour in Roatan that included a short drive through inland areas and a beach stop, which felt more personal than the typical port experience. Costa Maya was underwhelming for us because we didn’t schedule anything in advance and ended up staying inside the port area most of the time. Cozumel was the most flexible port, and we walked quite a bit around the town before heading back early due to the heat. Each stop had a different feel, but Roatan clearly stood out for actual sightseeing.

name: Logan Pierce
review: Cozumel was the easiest to explore on our own since taxis were readily available and the town layout was simple to navigate. Roatan was more structured for us, we joined a guided island tour that included viewpoints and a short beach break. Costa Maya was the least engaging because we didn’t book an excursion and just stayed within the port zone. It was clean and organized but didn’t really feel like we experienced the destination itself. I think this itinerary rewards advance planning more than anything else.

name: Amira Haddad
review: Roatan felt much more relaxed compared to the other ports, especially during our beach excursion where we had time to just sit and swim. Costa Maya was very built around tourism, and without an excursion it didn’t offer much beyond shops and a pool area. In Cozumel, we joined a short walking tour that showed us local streets and markets, but it was extremely crowded with other cruise visitors. I appreciated the variety, but I wish port times were slightly longer, especially in Roatan.

name: Caleb Simmons
review: Roatan was the highlight for snorkeling, the water was calm and clear and the guide made it easy even for beginners. Costa Maya felt like a stop where you either do an excursion or just stay in the port area, which we did. Cozumel was busy but manageable, and we explored a bit on foot before returning early due to the crowds near the dock. The pacing of this itinerary felt a bit tight, especially in Roatan where I would have liked more time.

name: Yasmin El-Amin
review: We chose a small-group cultural tour in Roatan, which included scenic overlooks and a beach stop that wasn’t too commercialized. Costa Maya was more of a relaxed day since we didn’t pre-book anything and just walked around the port shops. Cozumel felt the most active but also the most crowded, especially near the main tourist areas. Overall Roatan felt the most meaningful in terms of actually seeing the island.

name: Derek Holloway
review: Roatan had a solid snorkeling excursion but the transfer process from ship to boat took longer than expected. Costa Maya was disappointing without an excursion since we mostly stayed in the port complex and didn’t see much else. Cozumel was more flexible, and we took a taxi to a quieter beach area which ended up being the best part of that stop. This itinerary really seems to depend on how much you plan ahead.

name: Sana Rahman
review: Roatan was the most natural-feeling stop, especially compared to the other two ports. We joined a guided island drive that gave us a better sense of the area beyond the cruise zone. Costa Maya felt structured and very tourist-focused, and we didn’t leave the port area. Cozumel was lively but very crowded, and we only stayed a few hours in town before heading back. Roatan definitely had the strongest excursion options overall.

name: Tyrell Boone
review: Cozumel worked best for us because we didn’t book anything and still had plenty to do just walking around town. Roatan was more excursion-based, and we did a zipline plus beach combo that was fun but slightly rushed. Costa Maya didn’t offer much without planning ahead, so we stayed within the port area. I’d rank Roatan highest for activities, Cozumel for flexibility, and Costa Maya lowest for spontaneous exploring.

name: Elena Varga
review: Roatan was enjoyable because the beach excursion felt calm and not overly crowded. Costa Maya was more limited since we didn’t schedule anything and mostly stayed near the port pools. Cozumel was the busiest stop and we walked around town briefly but didn’t stay long due to the heat and congestion. Each port had a different pace, but Roatan felt the most balanced overall.

name: Mark Lind
review: We booked a reef snorkeling trip in Roatan and it was well organized with good visibility underwater. Costa Maya felt like a port where excursions are almost necessary, since the immediate area is mostly shops. Cozumel was easier to navigate independently and we walked into town for a few hours before heading back. Roatan was the only stop that felt truly scenic.

name: Talia Brooks
review: Costa Maya ended up being our most relaxed stop because we didn’t plan anything and just stayed in the port area. Roatan was more structured with a short excursion that included beach time. Cozumel was very active but also overwhelming with crowds, so we didn’t explore as much as we expected. Roatan stood out as the most enjoyable day overall.

name: Omar Delgado
review: Roatan gave us a nice mix of sightseeing and beach time on a small group tour. Costa Maya didn’t leave much impression since we stayed near the port and didn’t go further out. Cozumel was lively and easy to walk around, but it was very crowded with multiple cruise groups. Roatan was the only stop where I felt we actually saw something unique.

name: Sophie Nguyen
review: We did a short island tour in Roatan that included scenic stops and it felt more personal than expected. Costa Maya was very commercial and we didn’t feel motivated to leave the port area. Cozumel had more energy, but the crowds made it harder to enjoy walking around. I wish Costa Maya had more accessible independent options.

name: Isaac Moreno
review: Roatan snorkeling was excellent and the water conditions were better than expected. Costa Maya felt like a place where you really need a planned excursion to get value, otherwise it’s just the port complex. Cozumel was easier for independent exploration and we walked around town for a few hours. Roatan was the standout for natural scenery.

name: Riya Nand
review: We chose not to book anything in Costa Maya and ended up just walking through the port shops, which felt limited. Roatan was more interesting because we did a guided drive and beach stop. Cozumel was crowded but still manageable for a short walk into town. Roatan had the best overall experience for us.

name: George Ellis
review: Roatan was calm and enjoyable, especially during our beach stop where it didn’t feel overly developed. Costa Maya was more of a shopping stop unless you had excursions planned. Cozumel was busy but had easy access to town, so we explored a bit before heading back early. Roatan felt the most relaxing of the three.

name: Hiroshi Tanabe
review: Roatan was the most interesting stop because we booked a small guided excursion that included both scenic viewpoints and a beach break. The timing felt reasonable and we didn’t feel rushed. Costa Maya, on the other hand, felt very limited without an excursion, so we stayed in the port area and it didn’t feel very immersive. Cozumel was easier to explore independently, and we walked through town and the waterfront area, but it was quite crowded with other cruise guests. Getting back to the ship in Cozumel was straightforward, though the traffic near the port slowed things down a bit. If I were to redo this itinerary, I’d definitely prioritize booking something in Costa Maya in advance, because that stop felt the most dependent on planning.

name: Megan Foster

name: Dre Williams

name: Darius Coleman

In Cozumel, we explored on foot near the downtown area, but it was very busy with multiple ships in port. Roatan felt like the only place where we actually experienced something beyond the cruise infrastructure.

name: Mei Alvarez

It was easy to get around but quite crowded and hot. If I had to rank them, Roatan felt the most worth it, Costa Maya the most limited without planning, and Cozumel somewhere in the middle for flexibility.

Costa Maya felt the weakest of the three because we didn’t book an excursion and ended up just staying in the port area. It was clean and organized but didn’t feel like we actually saw much of the region.

Cozumel was busy but manageable, and we walked around town briefly before heading back early due to the crowds. The pacing of each stop felt very different, with Roatan being the most relaxed.

name: Calvin Brooks

It was crowded but still enjoyable. Roatan definitely stood out as the most interesting stop overall.
